Chapter 36. Journey to the west - 5
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Huff. Huff.!!

Will’s breath came out rough and uneven as his legs carried him forward through the dense, blackened forest.

Every step sent a dull pain shooting up from his right ankle, the wound there still raw and not fully healed despite the potions he had consumed earlier.

His lungs burned, not from lack of air, but from the constant tension that refused to leave his body even for a moment.

[Calm down, host. They cannot see you. They can only hear. Just stay silent.]

"You are not helping at all," Will muttered under his breath as he forced himself to slow down.

He crouched beside the thick roots of a twisted tree, pressing his back against the bark while trying to steady his breathing.

The bark felt rough and warm, still holding residual heat from the earlier fire he had unleashed. His chest rose and fell as he tried to suppress every unnecessary sound.

He was being chased.

Not by something he could clearly see, but by something that never let him go.

The cut on his ankle was proof enough that whatever hunted him did not need eyes to land a strike.

The pain flared again as he shifted his weight, and he clenched his teeth to keep from making a sound.

At first, he had fought back properly. He had relied on his sword, on sword intent, and on raw instinct.

Each sudden ambush had been met with quick reactions and brutal counterattacks. But time passed, and exhaustion piled up steadily. His reactions slowed, his mind dulled, and even his perception began to lag.

He had consumed too many potions in too short a time.

Mana recovery potions kept his reserves from drying up completely, while healing potions sealed wounds that would have otherwise ended him already.

However, each bottle left something behind. His head throbbed constantly, his thoughts felt heavy, and his emotions were frayed. He could feel the strain on his mind far more clearly than on his body.

The invisible figures never stopped.

They followed him through the burned forest, through the fog, and through the narrow paths between dead trees.

They never lost his trail. Whether it was the scent of blood or the lingering presence of stellar fire that drew them in, Will could not tell. He only knew that wherever he went, they followed.

And the Universal Tongue made it worse.

He could hear them.

Their voices did not come from a single direction. They overlapped, whispered, and echoed through the fog as if the forest itself was speaking.

Hunger. Stop this hunger. So hungry. It hurts.

The words clawed at his mind. They were not cries of rage or malice. They were cries of pain.

Endless, consuming hunger twisted into madness. Just listening made his stomach churn and his head ache.

He could not tell what they were.

They did not sound like beasts, nor like humans, nor like demons. Their voices carried suffering, but their intent was simple and terrifying.

They wanted to eat him.

"Damn it," Will muttered, pressing his palm against the ground as he steadied himself. "I should have reached it by now. Where is the damn temple?"

He had been moving toward it for days. Or maybe weeks. He had lost track of time completely. The fog and tress never changed, the sky remained the same blanketing this place from the sun.

Every direction felt the same, and the landmarks he tried to remember either crumbled away or shifted when he was not looking.

Slash.

Pain exploded across his shoulder before he could even react.

Will gasped as something sliced through his shoulder’s side, sending him stumbling forward. His vision blurred for a split second as the shock traveled through his body. He did not know what hit him.

Without thinking, he released a burst of Stellar fire.

Golden flames erupted outward, spreading in a wide arc around him. The heat surged through the fog, and for the first time, a shape appeared within the fire.

A shrill scream pierced the air.

The flames outlined a humanoid figure, smaller than a human but distorted. Its head was too large for its body, its ears elongated and sharp, and its limbs bent at unnatural angles. It looked like a grotesque fusion of a goblin and a dwarf, twisted by something potentially worse.

Will did not hesitate.

He stepped forward and unleashed the first form of his god-rank heaven sundering divine sword art, Origin Severance.

Although he had not even reached the newbie rank of this technique, but just the partially mastered technique was destructive enough.

The slash moved faster than sound.

The figure burst apart instantly, its form collapsed into mangled but still invisible flesh as the blade passed through it. The moment the strike landed, a sharp headache stabbed into Will’s skull, making him stagger.

"Argh!"

He clenched his teeth as his Aeon Blade shimmered faintly in his hand. Something unseen flowed into the blade, like thick liquid drawn into metal.

it was drinking the blood of the invisible entity.

[ding!! Aeon Blade has acquired the trait ’Invisibility’.]

[Host, activate the trait immediately.]

Will tightened his grip on the sword and forced his breathing to steady. With a mental command, he activated the newly acquired trait.

The air shifted, as his figure disappeared from sight.

"Show me the description," he said quietly.

----------------

TRAIT – Invisibility

SOURCE – Nether Dwellers

Effects:

• Grants invisibility to the user while the Aeon Blade is held.

• Nether dwellers become visible to the host while the trait is active.

• Nether dwellers can also clearly see the host while the trait is active.

• The host remains invisible to all other beings.

-------------------

"What the hell?" Will muttered, rubbing the back of his head. "Was detecting me through breathing not enough? Now they can see me too?"

[Host, earlier they could already sense your breathing through their unique perception. You, on the other hand, lacked any divine sense at your F+ rank. Now the situation is balanced. They can see you, and you can see them.]

Will paused, considering that.

"You are right," he admitted. "But this even playground does not help me right now."

He glanced down at his trembling hands.

"I am not in shape to fight. Sword techniques drain mana, which I barely have. Sword intent and stellar fire require focus, and my mind is exhausted. I have been fighting, hiding, and running without rest for god knows how many days."

He looked ahead into the fog, frustration tightening his chest.

"That temple still feels impossibly far."

[At least you gained a new affinity.]

Will sighed and opened his status panel. A new line glowed faintly.

----

Affinity – Nether Energy (Absolute)

When sin meets divinity, the nether is formed. It disrupts life and nurtures decay.

--------

At first, the awakening of the affinity had felt like good news. His wounds stopped worsening, and the constant decay in this place no longer ate away at him. But that relief lasted only moments.

The nether dwellers sensed it immediately.

They were drawn to him like starving animals to meat. The more nether energy he released, the more frantic they became. Their hunger intensified rather than eased.

"These idiots do not even know that eating me will make it worse for them" Will muttered.

The nether dwellers were senseless creatures. They were trapped in an endless cycle of hunger and decay. Consuming nether energy did not satisfy them. It deepened their suffering.

"An affinity is useless if I cannot use it properly, I don’t have a technique or spell to use it" Will said with a tired sigh.

[(┬┬﹏┬┬)]

He shook his head lightly.

"Oh well. Is the SATD ready?"

[Yes and no.]

"What do you mean?"

[The vehicle requires nether cores as an energy source in this environment.]

Will blinked. "Of course it does."

[Nether cores are expensive, you don’t have enough SP to purchase them . You will need to hunt them yourself. C-rank nether dwellers, to be precise.]

Will leaned his head back against the tree, staring up into the fog-filled canopy.

(*￣︿￣)!

"Sigh.", "At this point, I do not expect anything to go my way in this wretched place."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 37. The Price of Freedom - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

*creak* *creak*

The interior of the SATD groaned as it tore through the dense fog of nether energy, its massive frame shook slightly with every movement forward.

The sound echoed through the cabin in uneven intervals, metal scraping against metal in a way that made Will narrow his eyes in annoyance.

The vehicle was fast, devastating, and heavily armed, yet the noises it produced right now reminded him far too much of an old bullock cart struggling uphill rather than a high-end bio-mech war machine meant to dominate hostile terrain.

"Why is the mighty SATD making noises like a bullock cart," Will mocked as he leaned back into his seat, gripping the armrest while the vehicle continued its relentless advance through the fog-choked forest.

The outside world was barely visible through the reinforced viewing panels.

Thick layers of grey-black mist twisted unnaturally, infused with nether energy that corroded the environment slowly but surely.

Trees stood warped and dead, their trunks were bent as if frozen mid-scream, while the ground beneath was churned with decay and hardened ash.

The SATD pushed through it all without slowing, its massive wheels flattened over roots and stone alike.

The system responded to him with a sigh, its tone was carrying clear irritation.

[Time was limited, so I installed a basic nether filtration layer. Unfortunately, the structural reinforcement was not optimized. That is why the internal frame is producing noises.]

Will smirked, clearly entertained by the system’s rare admission of fault.

"So you rushed the design and now my ears have to suffer," he said casually, resting his chin on his palm.

"Anyway, are we even going in the right direction, or are we just uprooting random trees?"

[Yes, host. It has been three days. We have been traveling west continuously without stopping.]

Before Will could reply, the SATD suddenly slammed into something solid.

The entire vehicle lurched violently as a deafening crash echoed through the cabin.

Will was thrown forward out of his seat, his body colliding with the monitor console as the safety restraints failed to engage in time.

"ARGH!!"

Pain shot through his shoulder and ribs as he struggled to regain his footing, meanwhile anger flashed across his face.

"What the hell happened to the shock absorption and damping systems you were bragging about so much," he snapped, rubbing his side.

The system hesitated for a fraction of a second.

[...I forgot to include them in the revised blueprint.]

Will stared at the air in disbelief before exhaling sharply.

"Shithead."

[Host, stop cursing and look at where we are.]

Will turned his gaze toward the forward display, and the irritation on his face vanished instantly.

The SATD had crashed into a towering white pillar made of smooth, marble-like stone. It stood tall and unbroken, its surface carved with faint symbols worn down by time.

Unlike the twisted trees and corrupted earth behind him, this structure was made artificially. It was proof of civilization.

Will stepped out of the SATD, his boots crunching against pale stone instead of rotting soil.

The air felt different here, more corrosive and denser. He walked forward slowly, then suddenly laughed out loud as realization fully settled in.

"Finally," he said, voice filled with relief. "Hahaha."

He placed both hands against the pillar and hugged it without hesitation, pressing his forehead briefly against the cool stone.

The journey through the Prison of the Fallen Gods had drained him physically and mentally, and this simple confirmation that he had arrived felt overwhelmingly satisfying.

[Host, perhaps you should do what you came here for instead of attempting to violate ancient pillars.]

"Ahem," Will cleared his throat quickly, stepping back and straightening his posture. "Shut up, shithead."

His expression hardened as he looked ahead.

Beyond the pillar stretched a narrow rocky path. It was smooth in places and worn in others, as if countless beings had once walked it long ago. The path cut directly through the fog, leading somewhere deeper into the heart of this forbidden land.

Without hesitation, Will stepped onto it.

The fog thickened as he walked, swallowing his figure almost immediately. The silence here felt unusual, as though sound itself had been muted out of respect or fear. After several minutes of steady movement, a massive structure emerged from the mist.

A temple.

It was built from luminous granite-like stone, tall and imposing, yet devoid of ornamentation. The walls were smooth and cold, untouched by decay despite the hostile environment around it.

Will approached it carefully and stepped inside.

The interior was dim, lit faintly by an unknown source. At the far end stood a large wooden door. Will placed his hand against it, and to his surprise, it opened effortlessly, its hinges moved as though they had been waiting for him.

He entered.

Inside the chamber, a single figure defined the space.

A woman knelt at the center of a marble stage her body was restrained by massive black metallic chains that emitted thick waves of nether energy.

Each chain was embedded into the floor and walls, anchoring her completely. Her hair were a darker shade of violet and her battle attire was pitch black perfectly blending into the shadows while still radiating danger and also making her look enticing.

Her head was lowered, hiding her face.

Will’s posture changed instantly. His casual demeanor vanished, replaced by absolute focus and respect. He stood straight with a sharp expression on his face.

[Centuries in that kneeling position. Her back would snap if she stood suddenly.]

For once, Will did not respond to the system’s joke.

"I greet the ruler of eternal darkness," Will said calmly. "Tamasya."

Silence followed his greeting.

The chamber remained still, the only sound being the faint hum of nether energy coursing through the chains. Minutes passed, stretching uncomfortably, until a faint breeze stirred the woman’s hair.

Slowly, she raised her head and two pitch-black pupils locked onto Will.

The gaze was cold, ancient, and crushing. But to Will it had been the most beautiful woman he had ever seen, from now his standards pertaining to women was going to change, Tamasya’s beauty had set a new bar in his mind.

A deep yet high-pitched voice echoed through the chamber, carrying authority that weighed down on his bones.

"Name."

"William, my lady," he replied without hesitation.

"Age."

"Fourteen."

"Motive."

"Your guidance," Will answered evenly, "and a gift."

For a brief moment, the woman stared at him. Then she laughed.

The sound was deranged, echoing violently through the temple as centuries of madness and isolation poured into that laughter.

"After so many centuries," Tamasya said, voice filled with disbelief and mockery, "this is the fruit of my patience? A child?"

She leaned forward slightly, chains rattling.

"Are you lost, brat?"

"No," Will replied calmly. "I am here to free you, help you, and to become your disciple."

As soon as those Words left his mouth a crushing pressure descended instantly.

A fraction of Tamasya’s presence flooded the chamber, crushing Will where he stood. His bones shattered. Organs ruptured. Blood burst from his mouth as his body collapsed onto the stone floor.

He did not die instantly.

Before Death could graze him Absolute Adaptation activated.

[A/n : capitalized D in death is intentional]

Energy surged violently through his broken form, rebuilding flesh and bone at an insane speed. Muscles reattached, organs regenerated, and his mana core got flooded with power beyond what his body had ever handled.

Agony followed after this, his talent was adjusting his body to Tamasya’s pressure, even if it was only a fraction of her power. The woman herself was someone who could threaten gods.

Will screamed relentlessly, he had never felt such kind of physical pain ever in his two lives.

A bitter realization was slowly settling in his partially conscious mind, the pain of reconstruction and adaptation to a certain attack burned far worse than the assault itself,

his nerves screamed as his body reforged itself in seconds. He convulsed, coughing blood before finally losing consciousness.

[A/N: The talent deliberately prevented him from losing consciousness instead of allowing him to faint and escape the pain. The reason was simple and cruel - if he had fallen unconscious in that state, he might never have awakened again.

After such a sudden and catastrophic shock to his body, remaining awake was essential to pull him back from the brink of death. so the talent let him rebuilt properly at first before letting him faint once his life was saved.]

Tamasya stared at the strange phenomenon in front of her

Her eyes widened slightly.

"Impossible," she whispered. "Not even gods..."

Her words trailed off as she watched the boy before her regaining his form back.

For the first time in centuries, disbelief crossed the face of the ruler of eternal darkness.

Chapter 38. The Price of Freedom - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Thousands of years ago..

The sky was murky as if the whole world was burning under it.

The sun was absent as if it never existed in this star system, but still there was an overwhelming radiance that pressed down on the world like a crushing weight, it was foreign and unwelcoming.

Suddenly,

The thick blanket of clouds was torn apart by waves of divine power, while arcs of lightning made entirely of holy energy ripped through the heavens and scorched the land below.

Mountains cracked under the pressure, forests were flattened, and the air itself trembled as if reality was struggling to remain intact.

At the center of the destruction stood a young woman with sharp features and pale skin, her hair was dark violet and her figure was that of an enchantress.

Tamasya’s body was covered in blood, her breathing was uneven and strained after countless hours of battle.

One of her arms hung loosely at her side beyond immediate repair, and although her legs trembled under her own weight, she still refused to kneel.

Her black battle attire was torn in multiple places, burnt by repeated strikes of holy light. Despite all this, she remained standing like she hadn’t given up yet, her back was straight and her sharp gaze pierced the figures surrounding her.

That fact alone unsettled the beings surrounding her.

Encircling Tamasya were figures wrapped in intense white radiance, their forms towering and indistinct.

Wings formed of condensed light extended behind them, and symbols of divine authority hovered around their bodies.

Each of them carried a presence that distorted the space nearby, their presence made it difficult for any mortals to breathe in their vicinity.

They were gods of the Light Faction.

One of them stepped forward, his voice echoing across the sky with authority that allowed no argument.

"Tamasya," he said, "you stand accused of following the path of darkness, of rejecting the guidance of the righteous heavens, and attempting to ascend to godhood without our supervision"

Tamasya let out a rough laugh that ended in a cough. Blood dripped from the corner of her mouth as she lifted her head.

"I never meant to interfere in your matters, all I wanted was freedom and non-alignment to any faction" she replied but her voice was hoarse but steady with determination.

"I walked my path alone. I sought power where it existed and paid the price for every step."

Another god raised her staff, and the surrounding light intensified.

"Shut up!! You have embraced a forbidden energy" she declared. "You cultivated demonic energies and killed countless innocent mortals, That alone condemns you."

Tamasya shook her head slowly.

"lies, "Darkness is not demonic, but you fear anything you cannot control." she said.

"Meanwhile the people who died by my hands, they warranted their own death when they decided to cross my path"

She had eradicated every bloodline that continued to hunt her. That was the truth they could not tolerate.

Tamasya had not been born with a divine bloodline, nor had she inherited blessings or guidance from any higher being.

She had no god standing behind her, no sacred scripture carved into her fate, and no faction shielding her from consequences.

She had been mortal in every sense of the word.

Yet she had risen.

She had survived wars that erased entire nations, endured environments that killed saints, and refined her power through understanding rather than worship.

She learned to control darkness in a way that made the element itself bless her. She became the

Ruler of Eternal Darkness

She had reached the threshold of godhood alone.

That frightened them.

A mortal who could ascend without divine assistance threatened the very foundation of their authority.

"Bind her," one of the gods commanded.

Light condensed instantly.

Chains formed in the air, created from divine law and holy elements, and slammed into Tamasya’s body.

The chains pierced her flesh and wrapped around her limbs, her torso, and her neck, anchoring her in place. She screamed as the bindings dug into her soul, suppressing her power and forcing it inward.

Still, she resisted.

Dark energy surged violently from her body as she fought against the chains, causing massive shockwaves that scattered the gods backward.

One chain shattered, followed by another, as she tore free at the cost of tearing her own body apart. Blood sprayed across the clouds, and the sky shook as opposing powers clashed.

She attacked without restraint.

A blade of darkness cut through one god’s chest dispersing his form into fragments of fading light.

(A/N: these forms are mere manifestations of god’s wills since they can’t descend directly i.e., why she could kill these manifestations with just one attack )

Another god lost an arm as her power tore through divine protection. The clouds darkened as divine blood and corruption mixed together.

But she was alone.

And they were many.

A spear of light pierced her abdomen and pinned her in place. A sword severed the tendons in her leg, forcing her to one knee.

A seal slammed into her forehead, disrupting her concentration and shattering her control. One by one, her resistance was crushed under overwhelming force.

When the final chain wrapped tightly around her neck, her body went still.

she did not say anything immediately, she simply looked at them with eyes filled with hatred.

"So this is how you all act, cowards!!"

No one answered.

They dragged her upward, lifting her beyond the clouds and toward the edge of the heavens. Below them, the world appeared small and insignificant, as if it no longer mattered.

"Your divine Essence will be used to raise a new god, feel grateful" one of the gods declared.

Listening to their plans, an expression of panic appeared on her face. With all her leftover energy she attempted a last struggle.

"hmph!!, your attempts are futile"

Suddenly a shining dagger appeared in her hands emitting chilling darkness. The chains snapped one by one freeing her from their grip.

she attempted to flee but as shot down by a beam of holy energy.

"catch her!!, don’t let her escape!!"

But it was already too late as her falling figure disappeared beyond the murky clouds, even the divine senses of gods could not point her out now.

The fall lasted longer than she could measure. Wind tore at her body, and consciousness flickered as her sealed power failed to respond. When she finally struck the ground, the impact shook the entire realm.

The realm was a prison of the Fallen Gods.

Suddenly the nether energy of the realm flooded her figure trying to eat her away before a thin veil of darkness sealed her from the intruding nether energy in itself and protected her from decay.

As the nether energy sensed the veil of darkness, the focus shifted to the broken remnants of divine chains, these chains got corrupted by the nether and started pulling her figure rapidly towards a white ancient temple nearby.

The nether energy did not serve the gods, but it seized the broken chains as anchors to feed on

Tamasya who was being protected by the thin veil of darkness.

***

Tamasya’s vision returned to the present.

The chains were still there, humming softly with nether energy. The chamber remained silent, unchanged. However, something unfamiliar stirred within her.

She looked down.

The boy lay near her, breathing calmly. His body had been rebuilt in a way that defied logic.

She noticed an unusual energy clinging to him rejuvenating him constantly.

He had survived her pressure while just being an F ranked being.

For the first time in centuries, something cracked within her heart that had been filled only with frustration and anger.

She saw hope in this Boy.

"A mortal," she murmured, her voice barely audible, "who does not kneel"

Chapter 39. Be a Good Disciple! - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Will coughed violently as his lungs struggled to pull in air. His body jerked forward as he retched, and thick, painful coughs tore out of his chest one after another.

His throat burned, his ribs ached, and his head felt heavy as if it had been struck repeatedly. Every breath felt shallow, and his vision swam as pressure pounded behind his eyes.

"Hrr-ack... cough... cough!"

His hands pressed against the cold stone floor as he tried to steady himself. Just before losing consciousness earlier, he had felt as if his entire body had been dragged under a raging ocean.

There had been no direction to the pain, only overwhelming pressure that crushed everything at once.

After that, his memories became fragmented. He remembered screaming, remembered his body tearing apart and reforming, and remembered a sensation that was far worse than dying.

He forced himself to sit up, groggy and disoriented, and looked around the chamber.

Nothing had changed.

The massive black chains were still embedded into the floor and walls, emitting nether energy. The chamber remained silent. At the center, Tamasya was still kneeling in the same position, restrained exactly as before.

Except now, she was looking directly at him.

Her pitch-black pupils were focused on his face without blinking. There was no hostility in her expression like before. It was an intense, curious gaze, as if she were examining a strange object rather than a living person.

A bead of sweat formed on Will’s forehead.

"She is a crazy bitch, isn’t she?" he muttered under his breath.

[For once, I agree, host. You can never be more right. She is a Bitch! One hell of a Bitch!!]

Will swallowed and slowly pushed himself fully upright. His body felt sore, but it was functional. He flexed his fingers, then his legs, confirming that nothing was broken.

"What happened to me?" he asked, keeping his voice steady as he looked at her. "You attacked me."

Tamasya tilted her head slightly and showed an oddly innocent expression

"I crushed you," she said plainly. "But then you came back to life."

His eyes twitched.

"You almost killed me," he replied flatly. "And before you say anything, you are supposed to be sealed here right? how did you even crush me?"

"I did not crush you with my own power,"

Will frowned deeply. "What does that even mean?" he asked. "You just told the darkness to do something? Are you messing with me because I am weaker than you?"

"I am not lying," Tamasya replied. "These are laws that exist beyond your current comprehension."

She paused briefly, then continued, "I will teach you about laws when you are ready."

Will stared at her for a moment.

[She said she will teach you. This bitch nearly turned you into toothpaste, and now she wants to be your teacher.]

Will ignored the system and focused on her.

"You almost murdered me!!" he said slowly. "And now you are saying you will be teaching me stuff?"

She looked at him calmly, her lips curving slightly.

"You are breathing here because I allow it," she replied. "And I allowed it for only one reason. I have accepted your offer."

"Offer?" Will repeated. "What if I refuse to free you now? What if I decide to leave instead?"

[Host, do not provoke this bitch. If you die, I die too. At least value my life if not your own.]

Tamasya’s expression did not change. Her tone remained even.

"It does not matter," she said. "You will still become my disciple."

Will clenched his jaw. "And if I say no?"

"If you step outside this place," she replied, "you will find your self inside again, the darkness here obeys me"

Will stared at her in disbelief.

"So this is blackmail" he said.

"never heard that word"

Will exhaled slowly and leaned back, sitting down on the cold floor beside her. His shoulders slumped slightly as the weight of the situation settled in.

He felt trapped in a way he had not felt in a long time.

She was stubborn, powerful, and completely unapologetic. Yet despite everything, he could sense that her gaze toward him had changed. It was no longer purely cold or hostile. There was something else beneath it.

"Maybe she feels guilty for turning me into paste," Will thought.

He sat there in silence for several minutes, thinking carefully.

His thoughts drifted from Tamasya to the situation he was in, and then to the simple fact that he had no one to consult on this matter. No ally. No mentor. No one he trusted enough to ask for guidance.

As soon as that thought surfaced, the system spoke up enthusiastically.

[If you need advice, you can always rely on me]

[Call me Senpai!!! (⌐■_■)]

"eat shit" Will muttered.

His thoughts continued drifting, and without warning, memories from his previous life surfaced.

The image of his father appeared in his mind. His father had been a hardworking man, always busy, always exhausted.

They had clashed often, mostly due to their stubborn personalities. There had been very few moments where they spoke as father and son rather than two male egos clashing.

One of those rare moments came back to him clearly.

"If it aligns with what you strive for,"

Will’s breathing slowed as the rare memory settled in, he wished for more of such candid moments, but unfortunately they were cursed.

they suffered from the curse of being weak.

His father had been torn apart by a monster. His family had suffered. Everything had been taken from him. Those moments, small and ordinary, had been stolen forever.

The pain he had buried resurfaced. He realized that as he had grown in this world, he had been slowly eroding his original self. The self that strived for revenge.

The self that had watched his brother’s heart be ripped out and placed in his hands for the entertainment of a demon.

[Host, calm down.]

A sudden surge of heat flared beside him.

Will snapped back to reality as he noticed a dark tree root near him had ignited, Stellar fire spread across its surface. The flames burned intensely for a brief moment before he forcefully suppressed them.

His control over the flames was bad, his emotional state had triggered it.

"Th..that!! fire!!"

He turned his head sharply.

Tamasya had said, her voice was not calm. There was shock in her eyes.

Will followed her gaze and looked at his own hand. Residual golden heat still shimmered faintly around his fingers.

Her eyes narrowed slightly as she examined him again, this time with far more intensity than before.

"That fire does not belong here" she said slowly.

Will frowned. "You mean the stellar fire?"

"Yes," she replied. "That is not ordinary fire. It contains certain authorities and laws and maybe even something deeper beyond my understanding"

She fell silent for a moment, then spoke again, her voice was slightly lower.

"A mortal body should not survive holding that kind of flame, you must have a special physique"

Will looked down at his hands, then back at her.

"I survived you too," he said quietly clearly conveying that he had his share of secrets.

The chamber fell into silence once more.

"I intend to use this fire to break these chains"

Chapter 40 Be a Good Disciple! - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Will stood in front of the kneeling woman and looked at the black metallic chains that restrained her body. They were not ordinary restraints.

Each chain was thick, uneven, and embedded deeply into the stone floor and walls, as if the prison itself had been shaped around them.

Nether energy flowed through the chains constantly, pulsing like a slow heartbeat, corroding the surrounding stone and filling the chamber with an oppressive presence.

Even standing several steps away, Will could feel the pressure pressing against his skin.

"Hopefully the stellar fire should work on them too!" he thought as he looked at its description again in the system window.

[Stellar Flame Sovereignty: Grants complete dominion over stellar flames capable of burning matter, energy, souls, and concepts like darkness, corruption, the undead, etc. ]

"Even though nether energy wasn’t mentioned, it should work since all the energies mentioned are closely related and have similar origins according to the novel."

"I intend to break these chains," Will said as he looked at Tamasya with determination.

His voice did not shake; it was a decision that had already settled firmly in his mind.

Tamasya lifted her head slightly. Her expression remained unreadable, but her eyes shifted toward him with faint disbelief.

Just moments ago, she had crushed him without touching him, reducing his body to something barely alive. And yet, he stood there now, speaking as if what had happened did not matter.

For thousands of years, no one had spoken to her like this.

A warm sensation bloomed in her chest, faint and uncomfortable, and she immediately suppressed it out of habit. Emotions were liabilities. She had learned that lesson a long time ago.

"But for that to happen," Will continued, "we both need to trust each other."

He stepped forward and lowered himself until he knelt on the cold stone floor in front of her. He deliberately brought himself to her level, aligning his gaze with hers instead of looking up or down. His posture was calm, as if he were afraid that sudden motion might provoke her.

"Can I trust you, Miss Tamasya?" he asked.

The question was simple, but the meaning was not.

Tamasya stared at him; there were no threats and no attempts to manipulate her.

All she could sense was just a straightforward question. It unsettled her more than any insult or challenge ever could.

She had faced gods, armies, and entire civilizations. She had endured betrayal, fear, and isolation beyond what most beings could comprehend. And yet, here she was, unable to read a fourteen-year-old boy.

Slowly, almost imperceptibly, she nodded.

The system screamed inside Will’s mind.

[Host, we don’t have any SP left for emergency teleportation. Don’t release this bitch, host, I beg you (T_T).]

Will ignored it.

"I am taking a chance," he replied mentally, "so if this goes wrong, I accept the consequences."

He stood up and raised his hands. Stellar flames ignited around his palms, golden-white and controlled. Unlike earlier outbursts, the fire did not surge outward. It remained restrained and stable.

As he approached the nearest chain, the nether energy reacted violently.

The chains hissed sharply and tightened around Tamasya’s body. The pressure increased instantly, and she gasped as pain surged through her. Her fingers clenched reflexively, and a low moan escaped her lips before she could stop it.

"Aaahh!"

Will did not retreat.

He pressed the stellar flames against the first chain.

The reaction was immediate.

The nether energy boiled and evaporated as the mystical fire consumed it.

clink

The chain cracked, then snapped completely, dissolving into black vapor that vanished into the air.

Chipping sounds echoed throughout the chamber.

clink*clink**clink*

Will moved to the second chain and then the third one. Each time, the chains resisted briefly before breaking under the flames. The nether energy hissed and recoiled, unable to withstand the purity of the stellar fire.

Minutes passed.

Sweat formed on Will’s forehead as he worked carefully, one chain at a time. He did not rush. Any mistake could worsen her condition or trigger something he could not control.

With every chain destroyed, Tamasya felt something return to her body. Her powers did not return fully since more than one god had placed seals on her power; hence, only a fraction of it returned. But what she felt right now was more than simple power.

When the final chain snapped, the oppressive pressure in the chamber lifted slightly.

Will stepped back and exhaled.

"It is done," he said. "You are free."

Tamasya did not move at first.

Her wrists, once deeply scarred by the chains, healed rapidly as a fraction of her power surged back into her body. The deeper seals remained intact, but even this limited freedom was overwhelming.

She stood slowly.

For the first time in thousands of years, nothing restrained her movement; her knees weakened.

Tears formed before she could suppress them.

Before Will could react, she moved.

[HOST!! RUN!!]

Even Will got alarmed and was about to run when he suddenly felt the space around him ripple.

But instead of the anticipated assault, he felt softness landing on him.

Tamasya, the eternal ruler of darkness, the cold slaughterer, had taken him into her embrace.

She had closed the distance instantly and wrapped her arms tightly around him. The force of the embrace was far stronger than he expected. He stumbled back slightly, his balance nearly lost, before he stabilized himself.

She was trembling.

Warm drops soaked into his shoulder.

She was crying.

Will froze.

He had prepared himself for things far worse, but he had not prepared himself for this.

He gently patted her back, choosing to stay quiet; he liked the warmth of her embrace after all. It had been a long time since he had felt this type of affection from someone.

She then kept him in her embrace as quite a few minutes passed by.

"I guess even beings like her feel emotional sometimes," he thought.

UGH!! HOST: SHE IS INTO YOU.]

"Eat shit. Do you ever think about something beyond mating and romance? Can’t a master hug her disciple?"

[A master doesn’t hug her disciple, and even if they do, they don’t cling to each other for more than five minutes, and they definitely don’t start rubbing their bodies together. TSK, just start mating already!!]

"You don’t know a thing; she has been trapped here for thousands of years and is emotionally vulnerable. If by chance there are any of these thoughts in her mind, they will go away eventually as she regains her bearing."

Will ignored the system’s assessment.

He was utterly wrong this time.

Inside Tamasya’s mind, thoughts spiraled uncontrollably. The warmth of his body, the steady rhythm of his breathing, and the simple fact that he had freed her without demanding much in return overwhelmed her senses.

Her mind only repeated the same sentence over and over.

"I will always protect you; you are mine, only mine."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 41. Our Home
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

An unhealthy obsession with her soon-to-be disciple bloomed quietly yet violently within Tamasya’s heart, taking root before she could even recognize it for what it was.

It was not something she wanted, nor something she understood, but it grew nonetheless, fed by centuries of isolation and the sudden, impossible presence of someone who had given her freedom without demanding submission in return.

The feeling was forbidden by every instinct she had forged over millennia, yet it was also too intense to simply discard or suppress.

Will, meanwhile, remained completely unaware of the storm forming within her. From his perspective, all he could see was a lonely poor woman who had been imprisoned for far too long, clinging to the first genuine connection she had experienced in ages.

To him, her behavior did not feel out of place, It felt human. He pitied her, in a quiet and restrained way, believing that her actions were merely the result of prolonged isolation rather than something romantic.

In his mind, she was like a koala clinging desperately to the nearest tree after being stranded for too long.

Several long minutes passed before Will managed to gently pry himself free from her embrace. It was not a quick or effortless separation.

Tamasya’s grip, though not intentionally forceful, carried the weight of desperation and unfamiliar attachment.

Will had to carefully shift his stance and apply steady pressure to loosen her arms without pushing her away.

By the time they finally separated, Will was breathing heavily, as if he had just endured a prolonged physical workout rather than an emotional exchange.

As he stepped back, Will noticed a subtle but unmistakable change in her gaze. The sharp aloofness that had once defined her eyes was no longer there.

In its place was something unfocused and lingering, something that followed his movements a second longer than necessary.

He dismissed it as he told himself that she would be fine once she regained her composure and that emotional instability was expected after thousands of years of imprisonment. There was no reason, in his mind, to assume anything more.

Before she could speak or regain control of the situation herself, Will turned back toward her and spoke again.

"I hope Master can accept me as her disciple."

As he said those words, he bowed deeply, following tradition. The gesture was formal and sincere.

Meanwhile, Tamasya’s expression shifted dramatically. Her face flushed a vivid shade of red, and the thoughts racing through her mind were anything but appropriate for the moment.

The sudden transition from captor and captive to master and disciple had collided violently with the unresolved emotions surging within her.

Whatever she was thinking, it was undeniably not right in any conventional sense, and she knew it.

"Yy...yes, I accept," she said with a few gasps.

Will straightened immediately after hearing her answer, relief was evident in the way his shoulders relaxed. A small smile appeared on his face.

"Disciple greets Master."

Tamasya turned away briefly, angling her body just enough to break eye contact. She forced herself to inhale slowly, steadying her breathing while suppressing the chaotic emotions threatening to surface again.

It took several seconds before she felt confident enough to face him without betraying herself.

"Master," Will continued after giving her that moment, "may I ask you for something?"

"Yes, say it," she replied, her voice returning to an aloof tone as she forcibly suppressed all lingering feelings.

"Will you give me a recommendation for the World Academy?" Will asked nervously, the uncertainty in his voice betraying him despite his efforts to remain calm.

He genuinely feared that she might reject the request or feel disappointed, believing that this had been his motive from the very beginning.

To his surprise, Tamasya smiled gently and nodded without hesitation.

"Yes, it is good since the academy will teach you all the basics. It actually saves me the effort of mentoring you in the boring stuff. Before I start training you, it is essential that you enroll in the World Academy."

The simplicity and practicality of her response caught Will off guard, but it also filled him with immense relief.

Will heaved a sigh of relief, now he could peacefully enroll in the academy and take the ranking test almost a year from now. This solved a significant portion of his immediate concerns and allowed his long-term plans to progress without obstruction.

"Let’s go," suddenly Tamasya said.

"Huh? Where to?"

"You don’t want to live in this place, do you? Now that you are my disciple, you will live with me until you attend the academy."

The statement surprised Will, and he paused to consider it. He had not expected her to propose something so immediately.

However, after a brief moment of reflection, he realized that there was no real reason to refuse. He did not have anyone waiting for him elsewhere, nor did he have a permanent residence that tied him down.

Staying with her would also provide him safety and guidance in his cultivation.

"Where are we going?" Will asked her.

"Our home!!" Tamasya replied with an unexpected cheer, only to realize how odd the tone sounded.

" ahem! I...I mean my previous house."

Will chuckled lightly.

"Well, since I am your disciple now, it is my home too," he said, trying to make her feel comfortable.

The words were spoken casually, without any deeper intention on his part. However, the effect they had on Tamasya was anything but light. Her heart tightened sharply at the phrase "my home too,"

Without warning, she stepped forward and reached out, taking his hand firmly. Her grip was steady, and her expression had regained its seriousness.

"Brace yourself."

Before Will could sense anything unusual or prepare himself, darkness surged toward both of them from all directions. It did not feel hostile, but it was overwhelming in scale, swallowing the chamber, the floor, and even the air itself.

In an instant, both of them vanished from the prison completely.

Will felt his vision distort violently. The oppressive atmosphere disappeared, replaced by open space. When his senses stabilized, he realized that he was floating high above a thick blanket of clouds that completely covered the realm of the Prison of the Fallen Gods.

Sunlight Immediately blessed him as if it was waiting for him to appear. His body immediately recovered all the fatigue and pent-up tension from days of struggle in the hell below.

Tamasya turned toward him and smiled.

Without warning, he felt a sudden jerk, and the realization hit him immediately that he had started flying along with his master. The sensation was disorienting at first, his stomach dropping as acceleration took hold.

A transparent bubble formed around him as their speed crossed a certain threshold. The air outside the barrier screamed as it tried to cut through, and the surroundings blurred due to the sheer velocity of their movement.

Several minutes passed before their speed gradually slowed. Will’s senses finally caught up with his surroundings, and he looked ahead to see an enormous piece of land floating freely in the sky.

This time, he was genuinely surprised.

The novel had never mentioned anything like this in relation to Tamasya. Her character had been barely explored, reduced to a minor supporting role that appeared briefly before being discarded.

The only thing the novel had described was how she had assisted Ethan during a later arc when he challenged several gods.

Even then, her involvement was minimal, and she had been killed unceremoniously by a powerful and omniscient being shortly afterward.

"That author was a piece of shit," Will thought as he looked at her. "He completely wasted her potential."

Before he could dwell further on that realization, notifications appeared in front of him.

[ding!! a significant part of fate has been disrupted by the host]

[ding!! host has changed the destiny of individual ’Tamasya’]

[ding!! the disruption is deemed too significant to ignore, talent effect: Lawless awakening is acting up..]

[ding!! Talent: Lord of slavery (legendary) is evolving....]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 42. Did I ever listen?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[ding!! the disruption is deemed too significant to ignore; the talent effect, Lawless Awakening, is acting up.

[ding!! Talent: Lord of Slavery (legendary) is evolving....]

[Ding!! Congratulations on gaining the talent Fatebound (supreme).

The notifications rang one after another inside Will’s mind.

Unlike earlier awakenings that had overwhelmed him with uncertainty or pressure, this time the system’s announcements had a distinctly uplifting effect on his mood.

The sense of progression was tangible, and the rewards felt deserved rather than forced since he actually changed Tamasya’s fate.

Ever since entering the floating island, Will had been trying to process the sheer absurdity of Tamasya’s existence.

She had told him, in a matter-of-fact tone, that the entire island belonged to her alone. There were no tenants, no guards, no servants in the traditional sense, and no neighboring structures to suggest shared ownership. This massive stretch of land floating high above the clouds was hers entirely.

At the center of the island stood an enormous mansion, not merely large but excessive in scale.

Its architecture reeked of elegance and grandeur. White stone walls reflected faint light rather than absorbing it, and the structure itself seemed to breathe subtly, as if it were alive in some distant and passive way.

Will could not help but feel thrilled as he took in the magnitude of it all. This was not the wealth of nobles or emperors, which relied on taxes and exploitation. This felt like the realm of a god.

Despite the mansion’s scale, the island felt eerily quiet.

There were no signs of daily life in the way Will had expected.

However, that silence did not mean inactivity. As he observed more closely, he noticed faint movements near the edges of corridors and across the vast halls.

Mysterious black figures moved through the mansion, performing tasks with mechanical precision. Some cleaned the floors, others tended to structural upkeep, and a few carried objects from one place to another without ever speaking or acknowledging his presence.

When Will had asked about them earlier, Tamasya had explained calmly that these figures were the Shadow Born.

According to her explanation, these entities were manifestations of her will and had given forms by the darkness.

However, due to certain limitations placed upon her existence, she could not grant them true consciousness. As a result, they functioned more like automations than living beings, executing simple commands without deviation or creativity.

In essence, they were little different from highly advanced machines.

Even knowing this, Will found the concept thrilling. The idea of an army of shadow constructs acting entirely according to his will stirred something ambitious within him.

He could already imagine countless applications, from logistics and defense to spying and intel gathering and large-scale operations.

At present, however, he remained down to earth; he hadn’t reached that level, he reminded himself.

Will sat on a comfortable couch inside a spacious room that had been assigned to him by his master.

The room was large enough to house several families together; it was the epitome of luxury.

Tamasya had instructed him to remain inside the mansion for the coming days.

She had explained that she needed to leave temporarily to take care of a few matters that required her personal attention. Will had not questioned her further.

He complied without resistance.

He understood that this period was an opportunity to grow. He needed to begin organizing his power, understanding his abilities, and laying a foundation for what came next; it was time for secluded training.

Will locked his room and settled himself comfortably. His posture was relaxed slightly as he focused inward.

"System, show me the effects of the new talent fate bound ."

The response was immediate.

The system window expanded in front of him, and the details of the talent unfolded clearly.

---------

Fate bound (Supreme)

Effects:

1. Slave Mark

Placement conditions: The host may mark an entity as a slave. If the target does not resist the mark for the next five minutes by the bearer, it becomes permanent and can only be removed by the host.

effects: The marked entity becomes an absolute slave; its body, mind, soul, and will belong to the host. The host may force obedience or gain the slave’s cultivation before killing them.

2. Servant Mark

Placement conditions: An entity that willingly submits may be marked by the servant mark.

effects:

- The host gains all talents and abilities of the servant but cannot control their existence or decide life and death.

- The host can bestow a lower-grade version of his talents or other powers to the servant.

- Betrayal by the servant results in its instant death.

3. Mark of Comrade

Placement conditions: can be placed by the host on a target that has a favorable impression of you; if not, the mark fails.

effects:

- When fighting alongside each other, both temporarily gain increased cultivation.

-Talents and abilities may be shared only with mutual consent.

4. Mark of Destiny

Placement conditions: Souls whose destined death is changed by the host are marked by the mark of destiny.

effect:

- They become completely loyal.

- Talents can be shared freely.

- The host can sense their location and senses and communicate mentally across any distance.

5. Mark of Soulmate

Placement conditions: The mark of destiny evolves in case a woman is recognized as the true soulmate of the host.

effect:

- Authority, talents, cultivation, physiques, and almost all powers can be shared.

- Senses are shared.

- Both may teleport to each other freely. Fate cannot interfere between them.

- If one dies while the other lives, the dead may resurrect using the soulmate’s blood.

-------------

As Will read through the effects, a slow smile formed on his face.

This talent was fundamentally different from what he had possessed before.

While Lord of Slavery had focused on enslaving,

Fate Bound offered flexibility, variability, and long-term utility. It was not just about enslaving beings anymore. It was about building networks, relationships, and layered hierarchies of connections.

The ability to share cultivation and talents with multiple entities was particularly significant.

While these marks did not offer the raw amplification of the system binders, which granted battle experience and enhancements at one hundred times efficiency, they still represented an enormous strategic advantage.

Unlike the binders, these marks carried conditions.

Will examined each condition carefully.

Among all the marks, the Slave Mark stood out as the only one that did not require the consent of the target. Even then, the restriction was severe. The target could resist the mark for a full five minutes, which meant that Will would need to completely suppress the opponent for that duration.

Compared to his earlier talent, this was a downgrade in speed but an upgrade in control.

Earlier, the talent Lord of Slavery had allowed enslavement if the target called him master even once. This new system demanded far more effort.

"What a pity," Will muttered to himself. "Before, I just had to make the demons call me master one time to enslave me, but now I will have to suppress them straight for five minutes to get the mark on."

The system responded immediately, shattering his delusions.

[It is not that simple, host. The condition to call ’master’ was misunderstood by you. The target should also mean it when he is calling you master for that to work. It was not just verbal consent but also consent on a conscious level.]

[That is why this is still better since you can force the person into submission.]

Will blinked.

"Woah, why didn’t you tell me earlier? I had already planned about enslaving SSS demons using illusion scrolls."

The system paused briefly, then replied.

So all this time, when you told me that you had a plan of tricking the demons, you thought of using illusion scrolls? Do you know how much they cost, and your life could still be in danger if they don’t work?]

Will shrugged mentally.

"Umm, I mean, in a world where you are not the strongest, even breathing is a risk. Who knows, you might offend an ancient witch and get crushed by her just because you said hello."

The system’s reply came instantly.

[Oii!! that actually happened, and at that time too I warned you].

Will leaned back against the couch, a faint smirk forming.

"Did I ever listen?"

[(┬┬﹏┬┬)]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 43. Stepping into training - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

*crunch!!*

The sound echoed softly through the spacious kitchen as Will took a deliberate bite from the apple in his hand.

The fruit was crisp and sweet, and the faint juice dripped down his fingers as he chewed slowly, his thoughts already drifting elsewhere.

With his free hand, he passed a neatly folded short note toward one of the shadow borne stationed nearby.

The shadow borne accepted the note without hesitation, its movements were smooth and devoid of any expression or curiosity.

"hand this to your master"

The entity did not respond verbally, it simply nodded and turned immediately, disappearing silently into the deeper corridors of the mansion.

Watching it leave, Will finished the last bite of the apple and wiped his hand casually against his sleeve.

He turned around and walked out of the kitchen at an unhurried pace, his footsteps echoing faintly through the vast halls.

The mansion was quiet as usual, despite its enormous size. The silence no longer felt oppressive to him. Instead, it felt strangely comforting after all he was not a fan of a crowded line of snotty children crying for unlimited chocolate.

When he reached his assigned room, Will entered and shut the door behind him. After a brief pause, he locked it carefully, more out of habit than necessity. He stood still for a moment, exhaled slowly, and let his shoulders relax.

He then turned toward the center of the room and spoke softly.

"let’s go"

His figure vanished instantly as he entered the Domain of Infinite.

Will reappeared in a vast expanse of open land, standing exactly where he had left weeks ago. The environment greeted him with familiar calm.

The sky stretched endlessly above him, and the air was dense with rich mana that seeped into his body with every breath.

He looked toward the cliff in the distance.

The same cliff he had obliterated during his previous training session now stood whole and unblemished, as if it had never been damaged at all.

Will nodded to himself, unsurprised.

"ok system show me the returns I gained from all the bound individuals"

The system responded instantly.

[ding!! while the rewards notifications were turned off, two bound individuals Leila and Seraphine awakened also awakened.]

[individual Leila has awakened SSS ranked talent CELESTIAL ARCHER and SSS ranked ICE affinity]

[individual Seraphine has awakened SSS ranked talent SPIRIT TRANSFORMER and SSS ranked LIGHT affinity]

[Ding!! do you want to receive 100X feedback?]

Will’s eyes lit up immediately.

He nodded fanatically without hesitation. These two were not weak in any sense, and although their talents were technically ranked lower than Ethan’s, they were terrifying in their own right. Each of them had the potential to become monsters in their respective paths.

The system processed his response.

[ding congratulations you have received the following rewards:

talent : Heaven piercing Archer (legendary)

talent : sovereign spirit transformer (legendary)

affinities : ICE (absolute), LIGHT (absolute)]

Will let out a slow breath as the information settled into his mind.

Two years ago, placing binders on these two heroines had not been easy.

Leila had been the daughter of a renowned noble house, surrounded by guards, servants, and strict social boundaries. Getting close to her directly had been impossible. Instead, Will had taken a more indirect approach.

He had befriended her personal maid.

It had taken time, patience, and an absurd amount of chocolate. He had given the maid so many chocolate sweets, often mixed with tiny amounts of his blood, under the pretense of friendship. His plan had relied entirely on probability.

And it had worked.

After countless liters of blood drained and an embarrassing number of free chocolates wasted, some of those treats had eventually reached Leila herself. The binder had taken effect quietly, without fanfare or immediate consequence.

Sadly the maid thought that Will was courting her, Naturally she was also into him due to his handsome face. Sadly a maiden’s heart would be shattered soon.

Meanwhile, Seraphine’s case had been entirely different.

She was a high blood elf, the rightful heir to the elf queen, although she did not know it herself.

As a child, she had been abducted by human slave merchants and sold into captivity. Her time in the slave market had been long, traumatic, and degrading.

The slave merchants had erased her memories of her homeland.

Fortunately, fate had intervened.

The tower master of the mage tower, who was also a spirit summoner, had sensed something unusual during a slave auction. Numerous spirits had been clustering around Seraphine, drawn instinctively to her latent talent.

Recognizing her potential, the tower master had purchased her freedom on the spot, Later accepting Him as her disciple.

As for how Will had fed her his blood, it had not been simple.

He had used a cloaking veil called Trickster’s veil , an item obtained through one of his free system purchases. The artifact was described as top-tier, worth billions of SP, and its description had unsettled him deeply.

The system had stated that the limit of its cloaking was undefined.

That explanation had not helped.

What did undefined mean? There was no rank threshold, no cultivation ceiling, no clear weakness. Despite his unease, Will had trusted the system’s assurance.

He knew the name of the slave house where Seraphine had been held.

Every night, he had gone to her.

He brought her food, gifts, and small comforts. He told her stories about the outside world and spoke to her softly, never demanding anything in return. Over time, a feeling of trust had formed in her.

This went on for countless nights, Eventually, he had fed her chocolates mixed with his blood.

Will did not lie to himself. He missed those days.

He remembered her innocent expressions and the way her eyes would widen in shock whenever he narrated folktales from Earth. She had been especially fond of Sun Wukong, the Monkey King.

She had once told him that she wanted to be a Monkey Queen someday if it were possible.

Unfortunately, Those days of innocence had ended abruptly.

She had grown up and now had been taken away to be sold at an auction, exposed to countless wretched gazes that made her cry throughout the night. Will had cried with her, even though she had not seen him.

He had wished desperately for her suffering to end.

And surprisingly it had.

One day, the tower master had arrived and taken her away.

[OK, LOVER BOY FOCUS NOW. will ya..?]

Will snapped out of his daze and sighed.

"yeah yeah," he muttered mentally before continuing.

"what about the rest?"

The system responded promptly.

[ding, individuals Maximus and Ethan have been training in sword techniques and have also started cultivating mana]

[ding!! would you like to claim 100X sword training and cultivation feed back]

Will did not hesitate long.

"take the sword training experience, save the cultivation feedback for later"

A massive influx of sword experience surged into his mind.

Sinclair’s family sword technique, known as the Sinclair Serpent Blade Art , unfolded within his consciousness. It was a Heaven-ranked sword art, considered among the strongest techniques on the Aris continent.

Heaven-ranked techniques were the peak of what the continent’s people could know about. Anything beyond that required beings like Storm Dragon or Tamasya to pass on such legacies.

Currently, Will had reached the intermediate stage of the first form of this technique. Maximus, by comparison, was still at the beginner stage.

Despite possessing Saint and God-ranked techniques, Will did not underestimate this art.

A mighty sword was needed to cut cloth, but a needle was needed to stitch it.

Will understood that not every situation required world-shattering destruction. Low-ranked techniques had their place, and he planned to master many for specialized applications.

As for saving the cultivation feedback, Will had something far more ambitious in mind. A crazy plan and for that he needed to wait a few more weeks.

"hehe!!"

[sigh!! After such a long time you finally listened to my wisdom-full advice]

"save the flattery, this thing was too good to miss so made an exception and decided to lend you an ear"

[hehe!!! admit it I am amazing]

twitch!!

"TSK, yes you are an amazing bitch"
