Chapter 216. The way to win this war - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Maris kneeled on one knee in front of William like a knight and accepted the golden shaitan mask , Bio-mech orb , a divine spark realm beast core and a special blueprint from him.

Till the camp got over, William couldn’t leave the domain, but he could make his servants do some stuff in the outside world acting as Ravens, the so called elders of the Eternal Sovreign.

He also handed her the Trickster’s veil for emergency purposes.

"All the best," William said with a smile.

"It’s my honor to be of service to my lord," Maris spoke as she accepted the shaitaan mask.

William felt a rare change in Maris, like she was acting a bit differently. A bit more refined and elegant than before. He wondered what had happened in a few days that he had let Maris have as a form of rest.

"And why are those two peeking from behind the tree??" he thought as he looked at Barash and Ronin hiding behind a huge tree in a distance as if looking at some significant event.

Keeping those thoughts aside, William said goodbye to Maris and left the domain.

Meanwhile, Maris looked at the shaitaan Mask with great respect and reverence before standing up and turning to look at the two who were hiding behind the tree all this time.

A smile bloomed on her face as she walked to them.

"How did I do? Did I look similar to the knight on the TV?" she asked while looking at the figures of Barash and Ronin.

"You were amazing, Boss. For a moment, I forgot whether I was watching TV or reality." Barash stuck to full flattery. While Ronin added, "Barash, you are undermining the boss’s skills; I believe the boss was much better than that frail, scrawny knight we saw on the TV."

"It’s enough you two, stop with those inflated words of praise~" Maris spoke with a huge grin on her face that suggested she was happy about their comments rather than annoyed.

Barash and Ronin called her boss, even though they were superior in cultivation ranks. Yet in William’s hierarchy, servants were above slaves, and even these two recognized this.

Maris did not treat them badly at all, although she was getting too spoiled by their flattery. As Barash and Ronnin started acting like Maris was their boss, even Maris had silently and unconsciously started acting as their leader.

Even now, the bridge of her nose and chin were tight with silent approval at the comments these two were making about her ability and intellect. Although she knew that much of her mimicking the TV act was due to her natural acting talent bestowed on her by her lord.

She looked at these two, "Look at this, you guys," she said, and put on the golden shaitaan mask on her face, her blood red demonic pupils turned gold.

Her draconic wings and horns disappeared as she used shapeshifting simultaneously; her scales disappeared, and her structure leaned. Her grayish hair turned golden as well, making her look completely unrelated to anything dragon-like or even a normal creature of Aris.

Barash and Ronin showed surprised expressions. "Boss!! You changed completely!!"

"Do I look like a golden raven??" Maris asked, turning her torso while checking her white dress.

"Boss, you should wear something dark with golden ornaments; you should try to look mysterious for the job," Ronin suggested.

Maris nodded, "Well, it seems like I will have to shop for a bit before going to make the deal, it should look intimidating during the meet, right?"

The two slaves nodded in agreement. "Yes, boss, we are the eternal sovereign’s cult; we should look like bringers of death," Barash said while Ronnin contradicted him.

"Eww, don’t listen to him, boss. He is a muscle head. You should look elegant and reflect the soft power of our cult. You should give them the idea that we control the world."

Barash looked at Ronin with annoyance while Maris just nodded, "I will try to..." before saying her goodbyes and turning to leave.

***

a few hours later...

No man’s land.

The academy was located on a floating island; these floating islands did not move in the air and instead remained stationary.

The Academy’s floating island was located above the no-man’s-land just between Riverdale to the north and the Skybreak Empire to its south.

The land just below the academy’s island belonged to the academy itself and was used to lift students to the premises through an elevation platform run by antigravity arrays.

Every supply and resource was transported through this platform. During the start of the conflict, the demons had managed to destroy this platform, thus isolating the academy partially.

At this moment, Andrea and Kevin stood in a silent clearing, a few thousand miles west of that point.

They feared that demons might notice them leaving, so they hadn’t left in Andrea’s flying carriage instead, they had sneaked out secretly.

Andrea made sure nobody followed them, they were called here by the Eternal Sovereign, Kevin to be specific, while Andrea had just decided to tag along.

"How will they find us??" Andrea asked

"Dunno," Kevin shrugged cluelessly.

Suddenly, the ground shook around them, making the peaceful birds and other flying beasts restless and forcing them to fly away. The trees shook as something was approaching.

Then something entered Andrea’s perception range.

"What is happening??" Kevin wanted to ask, but before he could, the object entered his perception too; the sound disappeared for a moment before an explosion rocked the surroundings.

Boom!!

Andrea and Kevin looked at the sky. A massive black metal airship with golden sigils rotating around its hull; its body was sharp like a weapon, and it radiated an aura of a divine spark beast. The aura was not restrained at all; sparks of thunder charged the air around the ship.

It appeared in a blink, breaking the sound barrier in the sky before it stopped before the two. They had never seen something so grand before; sure, flying artifacts existed, but they were not as fast and aggressive as this one, and no artifact released the aura of a live beast.

"I sense the aura of a thunder roc king from this ship, like it’s been forged from its bones," Andrea spoke, shocking Kevin.

"Is it even possible to make something like this??" he said with a stunned expression. His eyes were fixed on the runic characters etched on the body of this ship, and they seemed beyond his understanding.

Kevin was the best formation master on the whole Aris; generations looked up to him in the field of formation mastery, yet he felt clueless as he gawked at the peak of artificing and rune smithing.

He wondered when he had last felt so amazed, then he remembered the academy trials when William had somehow hacked the best and most complex formations in the academy and had managed to erase any signs of tampering later with ease.

He had felt clueless at that time, and now, yet again, he felt the same emotion with much more intensity.

The ship descended in front of them and stopped at a small height above the ground, its boosters turned off, and the noise it was making quieted down while the hatch on the ship’s stomach opened up and a figure appeared in Andrea and Kevin’s sights.

It was the figure of a lady with a golden raven mask.

Andrea recalled the white raven she had met during the recent operation in the south; the golden mask looked similar, with the only difference being its color.

She immediately realized that the visitor was surely an emissary of the Eternal sovereign.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 217. The way to win this war - 3
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Maris’s figure appeared in front of Andrea and Kevin as the hull of the black ship opened up. The massive craft hovered silently a few meters above the clearing.

Faint streams of energy ran along the seams of its metal surface, glowing softly in intricate patterns that hinted at complex artificing beyond conventional design.

Her outfit was a fitted black leather ensemble that clung tightly to her form, its surface traced with faint gold filigree along the seams.

A high collar had clasped neatly at her throat, while her loose golden hair had spilled across the shoulders of a dark cape patterned with subtle gilded designs.

Around her waist, a broad utility belt carried pouches and a sheathed rapier, and she wore a golden raven mask that transformed her pupils to a similar shade.

Her golden eyes landed on the two figures wearing loose robes and gawking at her with wonder.

That was the exact expression she had wanted to see on these faces.

Maris, satisfied by their reactions, descended the still-levitating aircraft through an extendable platform that had now been drawn out from the craft.

The platform slid outward smoothly before lowering gradually toward the ground while the aircraft remained perfectly suspended in the air.

Maris quietly came forward and stood in front of them. "Greetings to the crystal dragon. I see that you came uninvited, well, not that it matters." Maris chuckled and then addressed them both.

"Leaders from the academy, I am Golden Raven, a humble servant of my lord, the Eternal Sovereign," Maris introduced herself.

Andrea nodded. "We got a letter from the Eternal Sovereign claiming that you guys have a solution to our current predicament."

Maris nodded; she waved her hands, and from her space ring, a huge black metallic case appeared on the ground.

The sudden appearance of the object caused a dull thud as its heavy frame struck the ground.

The huge black box had the golden engraving of a sun within which an infinity symbol rested; this was now the official symbol of William’s organization.

"Please open it," Maris told them.

Kevin nodded and moved to the case’s front; he unhooked the front and opened its hatch-like body. What greeted his sight left him shocked.

Thousands of lightstones lined up neatly in front of him, emitting energy he had never felt from the trash sold by the cults.

The stones glowed with pure radiance; each one released dense light elemental energy.

Even standing a few steps away, Andrea could feel the radiance of these stones spreading through the air.

Her eyes widened too, and she gulped. This was exactly what they needed the most right now, but Maris didn’t give her a chance to speak. "164,000 lightstones, enough to last you for 3 months."

As Maris told them their exact requirement of the amount of light stones that they consumed, both Andrea and Kevin couldn’t help but shiver.

They realized that their daily consumption, which was a secret to most people, was known to Golden Raven, and that revelation made Eternal Sovereign’s identity even more unsettling in their minds.

But this was not all; Maris waved her hands, and a scroll appeared in her hands. She passed it to Kevin.

Kevin took the scroll and inspected its contents.

"This is a temporary formation blueprint."

"This will help end the war in a few hours," Maris said.

Kevin just stared at her in surprise at her claims; he then looked at the requirements of the array.

"Gasp!!! 10,000 lightstone just for one hour," Kevin gasped.

Maris only shrugged at him. "It’s your choice whether you want to end the battle soon or stretch it to a month or even longer."

Andria looked at Kevin’s changing expressions curiously. She then turned to Maris and nodded, "Thank you."

"Don’t thank us, Miss Andrea," Maris turned to speak. "My lord doesn’t help; he lends favors, and favors must be repaid." She spoke with a quiet steadiness.

Andrea gulped. "What does the eternal sovereign need from us??" She thought that these people would now demand something so exorbitant and hard for the academy to repay.

Maris looked at her for a moment. "Don’t worry; we are believers of equivalent exchange unless we are dealing with demons."

"The academy will assist my lord’s cause 3 times; you will have to take an oath of heavens," Maris said, taking out three fingers in front of Andrea.

Andrea gulped; even though Maris assured her that the favors they would ask would be worth the help they were providing, she was still skeptical.

But then she gave a second look to those lightstones; they were her last hope, and as long as the academy survived, then it would be all worth it, she told herself.

Within a few moments, the terms for the oath were decided, and a few clauses were added, ensuring the survival and interests of the academy.

Maris then handed Andrea another scroll. "This is the first assistance my lord needs from you; contact Riverdale’s emperor, Aurelius Dalton, after 15 days and assist him in subduing the Clayman cult. The timeline of the attack is in a few weeks."

Andrea and Kevin both got confused. "What?? Emperor Aurelius is going to attack the Clayman cult??"

Maris simply nodded and refrained from adding anything.

Kevin hesitated for a moment. "I doubt we will be able to get out of our own predicament sooner than 15 days."

Maris looked at Kevin and then pointed at the formation blueprint in his hands without saying anything.

Kevin, meanwhile, gave it a second look, still skeptical about its capabilities.

Maris then turned. "I’ll take my leave; take care, you two."

Andrea and Kevin looked at each other for a moment before turning to her.

"I think you created a lot of commotion in the area when you arrived; I sense a squad of several strong demons approaching this location. They will try to follow your flying artifact now," Andrea tried to warn as she sensed some presence in the periphery.

She was fully capable of handling these demons on her own, but she still warned the Golden Raven.

She wanted to access this individual’s strength and gain any insight about her she could.

Maris looked at Andrea. "You don’t need to worry, Miss Andrea; the demons will be only inviting death if they dare lurk around me," as if waiting on cue.

Maris raised her hand, and with that action, the massive structure of the spacecraft behind her transformed with a creaking noise as if a beast had awakened.

Before they knew it, metal panels shifted and folded across the ship’s hull, locking into new configurations as layers of armored segments slid apart, revealing a long, sleek cannon hidden within the craft.

Andrea and Kevin looked at the strange transformation of the flying artifact; they wondered what was happening.

With a charging sound, several floating runes materialized around the cannon’s mouth.

Maris then just clenched her hands inward, and a super-energized beam infused with the thunder element shot from the massive cannon toward the east.

Andrea looked at the massive beam being shot. She sensed the impossible speed at which the energized beam struck the squad of demons that was trying to lurk around this area.

Nothing remained of them; they were just vaporized into nothingness.

The forest in that direction lit up for a brief moment before falling silent again.

Andrea gawked at the marvel in front of her, even though she would not be affected by this weapon one bit since the level of attack was at the divine spark realm; yet, if countless of these ships were to be used in a war, then they would wreak havoc on the enemy side.

This ship did not just indicate massive firepower but also the advancement of artificing and engineering far superior to the academy’s.

At this point, Andrea’s speculation turned into beliefs; the Eternal Sovereign was not someone whom they could underestimate anymore.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 218. Inari
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Do you have any idea about its functions??" Andrea asked Kevin skeptically while looking at the formation blueprint in his hands.

"Not sure, since it uses light stones, it must be some anti-demonic formation, yet I can’t understand the structure and placements of the runes; it feels like..." Kevin tried to say something, but his mind remained blank.

"Feels like what?" Andrea asked with a serious expression.

"I think it’s a very powerful array..." Kevin spoke cluelessly, lost in inspecting the treasure in front of him; Andrea’s questions became secondary in front of the gold mine that had been handed to him.

Right now both of them had just returned after making the deal with the golden raven.

Andrea witnessed the destruction piling up on the battlefield outside the academy from above.

The academy grounds below them had turned into a grim landscape of shattered stone, collapsed towers, and scattered debris.

Smoke curled upward from several ruined structures, mainly commercial complexes and recreational areas unprotected by the barrier.

As she raised her head, she saw the blooms of light behind the clouds.

Each flash illuminated the sky briefly, followed by thunderous reverberations that rolled across the sky. "Senior is still fighting that demon; I don’t know why Bellial’s stamina or demonic energy is not running out at all. It’s common knowledge that illusions consume both energy and mental power."

Kevin heard her and looked up for the first time, realizing that they were on the academy premises already. His eyes lingered on the flashes in the sky.

Boom

An explosion drew everyone’s attention on the battlefield; the clouds in the sky parted, and a figure beamed towards the ground.

Crash!!!!

It crashed on the ground with great force. The impact sent fragments of stone flying in every direction as the ground cracked beneath the pressure. A shallow crater formed instantly, dust and broken marble scattering outward like a violent wave.

Andrea turned towards the figure that had just been thrown to the ground and realized that it was Demon Lord Bellial’s figure that had just crashed.

It sat in the rubble, and coughing noises sounded. Dark smoke curled faintly from his body as he pushed aside fragments of broken stone.

Another figure descended from the sky; it was a fox-kin lady with nine tails on her back. She descended in a tall and poised posture; her long white hair with crimson locks flowed with the wind.

Her white fox ears had a hint of a similar crimson at the edges. Behind her, a fan of 9 large fluffy tails that were snow-white with vivid red tips spread outward, like each had a will of its own.

Her gown was made of layered silk and golden fabric; the creases trailed like mist around her legs.

She descended as though she were here to enforce her will on mortals, serene and untouchable. The air around her rippled faintly with spiritual energy, bending lightly under her presence.

Andrea bowed. "Senior, I have no words; if not for you, I would have been in deep trouble."

The lady looked at her and smiled. "I have told you countless times, Andrea!! "You should call me Senior Sister!! Drop the formalities already," she spoke with a light gentle smile.

Andrea’s eyes flickered for a moment; in that split second, the crashed figure of Belial chuckled. The sound was mocking, like it was amused by a small mistake.

The lady looked at that figure with disgust, but before she could do anything, Andrea attacked.

Not Bellial, but her. Yes, Andrea attacked the nine-tailed foxkin she just thanked and called senior, completely taking her off guard.

"Bullshit, Senior Inari never talks like that; her tongue is the most venomous thing I have ever heard."

Andrea’s attack came suddenly. Sharp crystalline shards erupted from the ground and shot toward the foxkin with blistering speed.

The demon lord Belial that had crashed onto the ground slowly dissolved and turned into Inari, a wounded and tired Inari.

Meanwhile, the Inari that had just been attacked by Andrea turned into Demon Lord Bellial’s figure.

"Tsk, you bastard of a lizard, Andrea, I raised you for this day!! so that you could badmouth me in such a situation in front of this shitty, holeless bastard!!"

Inari shouted as she tried to stand up from the crashed cavity in the earth; meanwhile, Bellial screamed, "Graaah!" You insects are so pesky!!!"

"Fucker, it’s your mother who is an insect, and that too a sewage worm." Inari did not stop; she hadn’t been giving her tongue a break.

Her curses flowed like an endless stream, each insult was sharper than the last. Andrea imagined what Bellial’s ears must have heard the whole time he was fighting her, and she could not help but shiver just thinking about it.

Still, Andrea couldn’t deny Inari’s help had been crucial for her.

Bellial couldn’t take it anymore; no matter how cool-headed he was, he was slowly losing his mind under Inari’s curses, and his illusions were being hindered by Inari’s words.

He moved immediately, making up his mind to end Inari’s life.

Andrea realized this and moved to intercept him; several crystals shot towards him, blocking his path, but were smashed by a wave of his hands. The demonic energy bursting from his palms shattered the crystal barrage like fragile glass.

Andrea tried to block, but Bellial immediately activated an illusion, distracting her only for a split second.

Andrea lost the momentum and crashed into a building irritatingly. The stone wall cracked as her body slammed through it, fragments of brick and dust exploded outwards.

Bellial had just removed the building from her sight using illusion and had shown her space to intercept him, making a fool of her.

Meanwhile, Inari, who looked at Bellial approaching her, closed her eyes.

This is how Inari had fought at first; she was a spiritual energy user, in fact, the best spiritual energy user in the whole Aris and also the matriarch of the fox clan.

Countering Bellial was simple for her; she had to close her eyes and just use spiritual energy against him, engaging him for so many hours. No vision will lead to no illusion, or so she had thought.

But unfortunately she had left her ears unchecked, and Bellial, after realizing that his visual illusions were ineffective, had turned to using auditory illusions.

At that point in time he had caught her off guard, making her crash on the ground in such a bad state.

Inari got a valuable lesson, and right now she covered her ears with two of her furry tails while she closed her eyes and used her spiritual sense to track Bellial.

The soft fur of her tails pressed firmly against her ears, sealing them completely of any noise.

At the decisive moment when Bellial was about to attack, Inari raised her hand and clenched it.

Bellial stopped in mid-air; he felt like his neck was being hung by a massive coil. Invisible spiritual energy tightened around his throat like a crushing serpent.

"Yes, keep him stationary," Andrea shouted, hoping to land an attack on Bellial, but she overlooked the fact that Inari’s two tails covered her ears.

Andrea launched a barrage of sharp crystals towards Bellial’s figure, but his figure got trashed on the ground by Inari at a very critical moment, missing the sharp crystals, which then flew towards Inari.

Sensing the approaching projectiles, Inari lost focus from Bellial and used spiritual energy to shield herself.

"Andrea!!!" Inari shouted, "I will kill you, bitch!!!"

"Oh shit!!" Andreaa, meanwhile, clenched her head in frustration as she saw Bellial’s figure getting released from Inari’s grasp.

In the next moment, she didn’t even see anything coming, yet she felt like her head was hit by an asteroid.

The impact was brutal, and Bellial had gone for her head.

The air exploded around her as an invisible wave of demonic force struck her body and hurled her across the battlefield. The ground shattered where she landed, carving a trench through broken stone.

Inari closed her eyes and ears again hurriedly as she saw Andreaa in danger, but before she could utilize her spiritual energy, a palm of demonic energy slammed into her, sending her flying for a distance before she crashed against a reinforced wall.

Bellial’s figure appeared in front of Inari.

"You curse a lot; now I will make you regret every curse of yours," Bellial sneered and moved towards her, a sword emitting strong demonic energy appeared in his hand.

Andrea, meanwhile, lay stunned at a distance. She didn’t know what had hit her, but she felt like she almost had a concussion. The world spun around her as she struggled to focus.

She was sure it was an attack filled with demonic energy; she felt the toxicity in the wound on her head.

"Oh shit!!, senior is in trouble!!" she murmured, looking at Bellial walking towards Inari with the intention to kill her.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 219. Kevin’s Struggle
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

a few minutes earlier....

"I need to plant this array." Kevin told Andrea, who was looking grimly at the crashed figure in front of her. The blueprint of the formation still rested in his hands; the parchment was being handled by him like a precious gemstone.

Andrea nodded. "Be quick; we don’t have much time," she said with a grim expression.

Kevin looked at her with a strange expression. "Why are you panicking? The senior is literally beating the shit out of Bellial." he saaid pointing towards Bellial’s crashed figure.

Andrea turned to look at him grimly. "If only things were so simple," she said.

Kevin did not understand what she was trying to say; he looked at the sky and saw Astral Warden Inari descending from the sky. Her nine tails spread behind her like a fan of white flames as she pushed Bellial toward the ground.

"Look, she is right there."

Andrea looked up and spoke in a hurry.

"Kevin, you need to hurry up!!" she nudged him repeatedly before disappearing towards Inari’s figure.

Kevin, still unconvinced, created some distance and watched the interaction from afar. Soon the colors on his face disappeared as he saw Andrea attacking Inari, and in the next moment Inari’s figure shifted into demon lord Bellial’s.

"Oh no!!" Kevin realized the actual scenario and turned to sprint inside the premises in order to place the formation.

The moment he started running, the battlefield erupted around him. Demons poured through walls and burning pathways, their grotesque forms crawling over broken stone like swarms of insects; they had gained a renewed sense of motivation after witnessing that Bellial had just been fooling around with the enemies and was not actually defeated.

Meanwhile Kevin dodged the demons attacking him and trying to group against him swiftly and moved towards the barrier in a hurry.

On his shoes, several runes flared up, accelerating his pace and pushing his body forward like a launched arrow.

He dodged most of the demons that tried to stop him. Clawed hands swiped through empty air as he slipped past them; his body moved in sharp zigzag motions that left afterimages behind.

Soon he reached the opposite side of the premises, where General Marcus was single-handedly handling hundreds of demons while controlling the academy’s weapons, mainly chains of judgment, with some other commanders of the Celestial army.

Golden chains lashed through the air like living serpents, each swing crushed several demons at once that had earlier been chasing after Kevin.

Marcus floated slightly above the ground, his hands moving continuously as if conducting a deadly orchestra to which golden serpents danced on his commands.

As he saw Kevin running into the inner perimeter, Marcus assisted him and cleared a pathway. A massive chain slammed into the ground before Kevin, smashing a cluster of demons into pulp and opening a clear corridor.

Marcus had no idea where he was rushing to, but still, he decided to assist nonetheless. Kevin was an asset to the academy and the celestial army; they could never afford to lose him.

Kevin blazed through at full speed, and after a few seconds he finally reached the core section where the main formation of the academy was situated.

As soon as he arrived, a sight shocked him. Five academy professors are pouring dissolvents and energy-disrupting materials on the floor. The glowing rune lines on the ground were being smeared and damaged deliberately.

Kevin’s jaw tightened. "What the hell are you doing!!!"

The individuals halted what they were doing and turned to look at him; in that moment, Kevin saw it: blood-red eyes, not like a vampire but a demon.

Demonic energy flared around these professors; there was no need for them to say anything; it was clear what their purpose was and whose spies they were.

"Tsk, the principal should have done a thorough checkup!!!"

Kevin didn’t understand why they were not able to detect spies even after they checked for brain seeding. The realization annoyed Kevin more than he knew.

Meanwhile, the leader of the spies spoke to his aides. "You can calm down; it’s just Kevin. Even though his cultivation is strong, this fragile elf has no affinity; he just knows how to make runes, not fight."

Kevin, who heard this from a distance, raised his brows but did not add anything; he just waved his hands, and a light stone appeared in his hands.

He clenched the light stone and focused while his eyes closed.

The cultists standing in front of him wondered, "Boss, did he just freeze seeing death so close?"

The boss of these cultists did not reply. His eyes remained fixed on Kevin; suddenly he felt something strange. The silence around him became unsettled, and suddenly, the leader moved to attack Kevin, fearing what he was trying to cook up.

But suddenly a blinding flash of light stunned him as well as his minions behind him.

Boom!!

Light elemental energy exploded into the surroundings; the cult members braced themselves. Meanwhile, they saw their leader was flung back a few meters away.

As the smoke dispersed in front of their squinting eyes, a figure emerged.

Kevin’s shirtless form came into sight, and what they saw instilled horror into them.

"I-impossible!!!" one of them spoke.

Kevin’s whole upper torso was covered with runes, runes that were etched on him, on his limbs, chest, back, and neck. Each rune glowed fiercely, burning with concentrated light energy that flowed across his muscles like streams of molten gold.

For the enemies, it was unheard of. Carving runes on the body was abnormal in all senses; nobody had tried to do so, and nobody had the courage to play with death.

Except Kevin.

Kevin did not wait for any show; he quickly appeared in front of each cult member one by one and smashed!

His body moved like a blur.

He smashed heads like watermelons with his bare hands.

Bones cracked violently under his punches as the runes across his body pulsed brighter with each movement.

Light elemental energy moved around his whole body, elevating his combat abilities to a godly level.

One by one these spies lost their lives and were smashed into pulp under his punches.

A few moments passed as he made sure there was no one left in the perimeter.

A few seconds later Kevin deactivated the runes on his body and flopped to the ground exhausted. Smoke emerged from the deactivated runes that appeared like black tattoos on his body.

His muscles trembled uncontrollably as the surge of power faded away. With a moment’s wait, he vomited a mouthful of blood while his eyes released blood at the edge of his lashes; he desperately tried to catch a breath.

He collapsed near a wall and supported himself; clearly, the use of these runes had taken a toll on his body. His chest rose and fell violently while his lungs struggled to pull in air.

He desperately wanted to lose consciousness, but he could not afford to.

If he gave in now, Andrea might die, he told himself. Andrea’s earlier words about sacrificing herself rang in his mind, keeping him awake.

He took out a potion and chugged it down forcefully, at first a health potion, then a stamina potion, then mana recovery potions. He took every concoction he could that he thought would help him move.

The mixture of potions burned down his throat like fire. By the end of those few hellish minutes, he gained an extended period of consciousness that was traded for a piercing headache.

Still, he moved.

At first he replenished the still-running barrier array with new, stronger lightstones before he moved on to planting the array given by the Eternal Sovereign; this was the last hope in his mind that could turn the tables in this battle.

With great effort and with a bleeding mouth and eyes, Kevin moved and securely placed the array beside the main array.

A moment later he neatly placed the 10,000 light stones on the several nodes of the array.

Each stone clicked into its slot with a faint glow.

"Please work," Kevin muttered before resting a palm on one of the nodes and pushing mana in a pattern that would activate the formation.

Kevin’s mana depleted rapidly, yet he still held on until the array hummed and glowed, indicating activation. Light began to travel through the runic lines slowly like streams of a river.

Kevin smiled and took a few steps back before closing his eyes and letting himself collapse on the ground under the weight of gravity.

He was too exhausted and injured to even sit down or find a safe place to collapse.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 220. Chickens (demons) on the chopping board - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"You curse a lot; now I will make you regret every curse of yours," Bellial spoke and moved towards Inari with the intention to take her life.

Andrea, still stunned by the last attack, struggled to get up. Her vision blurred and doubled as she pushed herself off the shattered ground. Something was sticking to her head, something alive like a parasite. It pulsed faintly, burrowing near her temple as if feeding on her mind.

She tried to shake her head, but the demonic creature clung stubbornly, sending waves of stabbing pain through her skull.

When almost all her hope was lost, a huge sound blared across the battlefield.

Bellial stopped in his tracks as he heard the unsettling sound. At the center of the academy, beyond the light barrier that had been restricting the demon army’s path.

In the core area, a huge light beam shot out towards the sky, punching through the clouds.

The beam rose like a pillar connecting heaven and earth; its brilliance was so intense that the clouds around it dissolved into glowing mist, although the beam’s endpoints were still covered by the blanket of clouds.

Bellial, stunned at the huge commotion, turned around. His glistening face was without eyes and reflected the golden beam; his breath quieted down, and hints of quiet shivering appeared in his fingers.

Almost all the demons in the whole perimeter of the academy were stunned, seeing the influx of so much light energy radiating from the core area of the academy.

Some of the weaker demons instinctively recoiled, their grotesque forms shrinking back as if their bodies recognized the deadly purity of the energy.

As if immediately, the huge boom that had stunned everyone died down, and silence reigned in the academy. a stunning and unsettling silence before another sound, much nastier and sinister, for the demons.

Roaaaaaaar!!!!!

A massive roar shook the whole Aris continent. The sound rolled across mountains, forests, and cities like the cry of a primordial being awakening from slumber; even thousands of miles away, the whole northern arctic shook by the roar.

The clouds parted violently, and a massive halo appeared, a figure made entirely of light elemental energy.

Golden wings spread wide behind the armored knight’s halo; each feather was blazing with molten light. Streams of radiant particles drifted from the wings like falling sparks from a divine forge.

A circular ring halo burned above the helm like a second sun suspended in the air.

Its armor radiated piercing golden light that made any demonic being here uncomfortable, its surface covered with faintly shifting runic patterns that flowed like liquid sunlight across the plates.

In both of his hands, the knight held long, luminous blades pointed downward as he fell toward the battlefield.

The blades were enormous, each longer than a tower, and their edges hummed with violent light energy that crackled and rippled through the air.

Demons tried to swarm away from that spot. Panic spread through their ranks like wildfire. Creatures that moments ago had fought fearlessly now scrambled in every direction, clawing over one another to escape the descending judgment.

Marcus, meanwhile, stopped. Along with him, the celestial army and the academy professors stopped and looked in the sky with reverence.

The scariest part was not the light and the size, but the head of the knight.

Not a head, actually; there were four heads on his neck, a ring of four heads covered by full-face helms without even eyeholes, making it look just like the holier and more ferocious version of Bellial.

Each helmet faced a different direction, silently watching every corner of the battlefield as if no movement could escape their awareness.

The knight struck the ground like divine judgment. Stone and debris shattered outward along with the crushed pulp of demons.

The ground cracked beneath the impact, and the floating island itself trembled under the arrival of this radiant colossus.

A shockwave of light burst from the left blade as it pierced the earth. The blade sank into the stone like a knife through butter.

The shockwaves spread to the whole battlefield, stunning the demons immobile. The demons that were closest to the swords were disintegrated instantly as their bodies started unraveling into ash before they could even scream.

Their flesh dissolved into drifting motes of grey dust, leaving behind only empty shadows that faded seconds later.

For the first time in their lives, Celestial army officers saw demons running away for their lives, struggling to get a chance to live.

Grotesque winged creatures screeched as they fled into the sky, only to be struck by waves of light that burned holes through their bodies.

A massive horned demonic beast turned and ran, their hooves tearing trenches into the ground as instinct forced them away from the radiance.

The knight then slammed the right blade into the ground, and another shockwave erupted, this time much more ferociously.

The waves of light elemental energy slammed everything in a vast area.

The ground glowed briefly as the energy spread outward in expanding rings.

It even reached Bellial, hitting him with a slam, and his body thrashed and fell to the ground a few meters away.

Bellial clenched his chest and started breathing heavily. His demonic aura flickered violently as if struggling against the oppressive purity surrounding the battlefield.

Meanwhile, Andrea, who had been struggling to stand up, suddenly felt her headache multiply several times before something squirmed and creaked in her head, and a flopping sound was heard by her.

Finally, relief spread all over her body, and her eyes opened properly for the first time after the most recent hit she had taken; she chugged a few bottles of potion and looked at the ground.

She saw a weird and small gooey demonic creature with tentacles squirming and struggling on the ground as if it were taking its last breaths.

The parasite shriveled under the flood of light energy saturating the battlefield.

Finally, it died, and Andrea quickly placed the creature’s corpse in a jar before throwing it in her space ring.

Meanwhile, the massive knight took another step, and the floating island quaked in the air. The sheer weight of its presence distorted the air around it.

Andrea lost her footing but still managed to turn around.

All she saw was a huge leg and blades before her gaze moved upward, and she scanned the whole halo of the colossus knight that had just descended.

"Holy..." she murmured in surprise; she wondered whether this was really something a formation could do. She had to contact Kevin and learn how to control this halo before it mistakenly killed her own people.

But then the knight moved one of his swords and swung it low as if sweeping the whole battlefield. The motion was slow and appeared to be lazy relative to the knight’s size, yet terrifying for the small creatures below.

Andrea, alarmed, quickly took to the sky, fearing friendly fire, but then she saw it happening. The blade just passed through the others, while the demons just got cut in half.

Their bodies split apart instantly, clean lines of light slicing through armor, flesh, and bone as if they were nothing but paper. A major portion of the battlefield was swept like this, as if this knight were killing pests.

Hundreds of demons collapsed in pieces, their bodies dissolving into ash before they even touched the ground. The battlefield cleared in seconds, leaving only drifting smoke and fading light where swarms of demons had stood moments earlier.

Andrea remained surprised for a moment, but then regained her composure. She needed to make sure Bellial didn’t try to escape.

After all, she was pretty sure that this massive knight halo would be able to kill a demon lord today.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 221. Chickens (demons) on the chopping board - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Inari remained collapsed on the ground until she felt the ground trembling under her. At first it was distant, like a low vibration traveling through the floating island beneath her. Then the tremors grew stronger; each tremble came heavier than the last.

The huge impacts of the colossal knight’s stomps woke her up. Her eyelids slowly opened, heavily and sluggishly. The world swam into focus through a haze of pain and exhaustion; she felt something digging into her head, something alive like a parasite.

As a few waves of golden shockwaves passed, the parasitic creature came off her head and crawled away before it could be hit even more.

Relief washed her up as she felt the pain leaving her body. Now her eyes opened up; she saw the sky was dim while a cascade of golden energy swirled in the air. The entire battlefield was drenched in radiant light mana. Golden light energy particles fell like snowflakes from the sky.

A few miles away, she saw a humongous halo of a golden knight slaughtering demons. The sight left her shocked and sent a chill up her spine.

"When did the academy get such means?? Why didn’t they use it earlier???" Several more questions popped up in her head as she looked at the colossal figure.

Suddenly Andrea appeared by her side and spoke, "Senior Inari, I need your help!!"

"Ugh!! What?" Inari clutched her head, but Andrea did not give her time; she immediately poured a bunch of potions on her head and some stamina potions in her mouth. The liquid splashed across Inari’s face and down her throat before she could even protest.

Andrea then picked her up and flew into the sky.

"Mghmm!!" Inari released muffled noises as Andrea’s potions almost choked her; her eyes widened as she found herself being piggybacked by Andrea.

Wind rushed past them as Andrea shot upward through the battlefield smoke.

A few moments later Andrea pointed at a portion on the ground. "Look, this bastard Bellial is trying to escape. Stop him, senior Inari, and bind him with your spiritual energy; let me kill him properly."

"Brat, release me first and let me fly on my own," Inari spoke, clapping Andrea’s shoulders hurriedly.

Andrea released Inari in the air and let her float.

Inari used her spiritual energy to float in the air before she looked down at Berllial with a sneer. "Khekh, bastard, your end is near."

In the forest below, Bellial was hurriedly trying to pen up an abyssal portal and escape. Dark mist gathered around his hands as he tried to tear open a gateway through space.

Even though he had no mouth, eyes, nose, or any other crease on his face, just his hurried moments revealed his desperation to leave and the fear he was experiencing.

Bellial waved his hands, and his position got camouflaged by an illusion; he took a sigh of relief and shifted positions by a few miles while keeping the illusion intact.

The forest distorted slightly as his illusion wrapped around him like a veil.

Yet a wave of light energy erupted again as the knight moved his other sword in a distant slaughter of Bellial’s demon generals.

The light wave tore through the illusion. He tried to set the illusion again, but this time the amount of light energy infused in the air hindered and stopped any illusion from activating.

Almost instantly Andrea’s and Inari’s figures loomed over him again.

This time not fearing his illusions, Inari just froze Bellial in the air.

Invisible coils of spiritual energy tightened around him, locking his limbs and suspending him helplessly above the ground.

Meanwhile, Andrea conjured a sequence of huge, sharp crystals.

Several crystal spikes grew from the ground like a bed of death and pierced his back.

One of them was conjured a few meters above him in the sky, started spinning, and shot towards Bellial, pinning him down to the ground against the sharp surface of the crystal bed from his stomach, almost making a mess of it. The crystal impaled through his body and buried deep into the earth beneath him.

Another crystal landed above, shattering the previous one, going further deep, and spilling its organs out.

Bellial screamed and squirmed under Andrea’s wrath.

Andrea, meanwhile, transformed partially into her dragon form as crystal scales, sharp, piercing claws, and ferocious fangs appeared.

Her nails elongated into curved claws while shimmering crystal scales crawled across her arms and shoulders.

She appeared beside Bellial while Inari restricted any movement from him.

Andrea’s other hand dug into the already cleaved stomach.

Like putting her hands in a bag searching for her key, Andrea searched Berllial’s chest cavity; there was no mercy, and his screams were ignored completely.

Warm demonic blood coated her claws as she tore deeper into his body.

Andrea searched Bellial’s demon core with the intention of destroying it and cutting off any chance of his revival ever in the future.

"Argghhhh!!!!" Bellial screamed and convulsed blood, but Andrea did not stop her gruesome ways. Several moments passed, but nothing came into her grasp.

There was no core at all in the whole body.

"Kheukk!!" Bellial spat a mouthful of blood before speaking. "Have you even killed a true-born demon before??" he chuckled.

"We demons are not limited and weak like you; we are built different, much more distant from your pathetic imaginations," Bellial spat and laughed with an evil expression as if relishing the sense of defeat on Andrea’s face, while half of his body remained split and splattered on the ground.

Andrea looked at him with wide eyes; a frustrating realization occurred to her.

She had never killed a demon lord; only the prime astral wardens had achieved that feat. Only they knew how to kill one.

"Hehehe!! What happened, Miss Principal? Do you not even know how to kill a true born?? Heheheh, too bad it seems like being an Astral Warden is a joke nowadays!!!" Bellial laughed again.

Andrea’s eyes went bloodshot. Her breathing grew heavier as anger and helplessness clashed inside her chest.

She had fought for hours. She had bled in this battle; she had risked her life countless times. Yet now, at the final moment, she realized she did not even know how to finish the enemy.

The humiliation and helplessness burned worse than any wound.

She did not want to lose after coming so far, yet she had no idea what to do, and she doubted the halo of the knight, who did not seem to have any consciousness of its own, would know.

Bellial kept laughing at her face while her frustration kept piling up.

His laughter became so irritating for her that in an instant Andrea’s claws sharpened, and she struck with her claws before tearing apart his limbs one by one.

Bones cracked as she ripped his arm away.

"Who allowed you to laugh, you worm?" she roared as her eyes glowed with anger.

Her claws traced Belial’s whole body and slit every piece of flesh she could find. Blood sprayed across the ground as her fury finally erupted.

"Fucker, die!!!" she screamed before slashing at his face.

"Andrea!!!" Inari’s voice sounded from behind.

Andrea, covered in demonic blood, stopped and looked back at her. Inari pointed towards a certain direction with a distressed expression.

Andrea turned to look at the direction she pointed to, only to find the colossal knight’s halo walking slowly towards them. Each step of the massive construct crushed stone and left glowing footprints of light behind.

He had wiped out every demon in a matter of half an hour. Now the only demonic presence he sensed was from the direction he was walking to, to end his quest and quench his thirst for demon blood.

Andrea, meanwhile, remained puzzled. She knew for a fact that if Bellial died so simply, then he would be able to revive unless his core was not destroyed.

That was the only way to kill a being above the true divinity realm of cultivation. Even though this knight might erase the current form of Bellial, if his core is not destroyed on time, then he will only revive again and regain his strength in almost a few years.

It won’t be long before he rebuilds an army and attacks them again with a much greater force.

And that thought burned in Andrea’s chest, making her restless.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 222. Global Warming
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"So did they accept our offer??" William asked Maris, who had now returned to the domain of infinite;

Her spacecraft had taken off and landed from outside the Riverdale Empire’s forest since the domain of infinite only allowed her to leave through the same entry point where she came from.

She could only leave from her mansion in the imperial capital and walk to the forest secretly, as this was a covert mission from her lord’s side.

Yet she was sure she did not leave any traces behind; even if she did, no one in the empire was perceptive enough, and moreover, Riverdale was going through a slight internal restructuring.

The reason for that was sitting in front of her.

"Take a seat," William told the kneeling figure of Maris inside his hut. He had long decided to drop formalities that seemed good at the start, but now they seemed useless.

Maris complied and sat in a chair at a distance before speaking. "Yes, my lord, Andrea has taken up your offer," Maris replied politely.

"Hmm, good job," William nodded as if thinking about something in distant memory.

Maris looked at him; there was no expression of joy or celebration on his face.

"My lord, congratulations on ending a demon lord’s reign of tyranny; it’s truly your grace that the academy would be able to kill Demon Lord Bellial today," she spoke with the intention to make him cheer up and see some satisfaction in his eyes. After all, she would feel useful and capable if her lord was satisfied with her performance and contribution.

Her voice carried genuine excitement; to her, the event marked something historic.

A demon lord’s fall was never an ordinary occurrence.

William looked at her and shook his head. "No, he isn’t dying anytime soon," he said with silent pursed lips.

All the happiness and fulfillment on Maris’s face disappeared; earlier, she had been truly excited to hear the news and rumours of Bellial’s death when she returned to Riverdale’s capital, and knowing about how she was involved in the whole execution made her thrilled.

She would also be a milestone in her journey from a weak disposable pawn to a powerful individual, as her lord had promised her.

But right now, William was denying that possibility. Maris went silent; she was stunned.

She had gotten some idea earlier about what that array that she had delivered to the academy with her own hands could do in a battle, yet William was now telling her that it wasn’t possible.

"My lord, can I ask the reason behind your words?" Maris bowed and asked with a grim expression; she did not dare express her displeasure.

"The reason I am saying he won’t die is not that I don’t want him to die, but because it’s impossible to do so right now. Demon lords are true-born demons and children of the abyss; their cores are kept separate from their bodies inside the abyss, mostly near the womb from which they were born. That’s the safest place."

Maris looked at him with shock. "By wombs, you mean their mothers??"

William shook his head. "No, only midborn and lowborn demons are born from two parents like all of us; demon lords are not beings born to parents; they are born from embryos of the abyss. The places where these embryos sprout are called ’abyss wombs.’

These places are the most important and sensitive possessions a demon lord or any trueborn has.

He protects it at all costs and mostly keeps their cores near a womb, which starts their revival process instantly. If the core is stored somewhere else entirely separate from the womb, then until the core is placed inside the womb, the revival process won’t start.

William looked at Maris and let his words sink in properly before continuing after a few minutes.

"Even the most powerful beings on Aris are unaware of this fact; only a few individuals at the top know, and today junior Astral Warden Andrea will realize that being an astral warden has long lost its significance and has become a playground of personal interests."

"But my lord, how did people in the past kill demon lords??" Maris asked, looking at William. She could not digest the fact that people at the top would keep this fact to themselves.

To be honest, she never expected that she would be divulged about such secrets so casually by her lord. She knew her lord was mysterious, but knowing the most sensitive stuff about demons that most of the strongest beings on Aris didn’t know meant that her lord’s wisdom surpassed these beings.

William replied to her question.

"Some of the astral wardens, mainly some of the primarchs who killed demon lords managed to destroy their cores for good, while some just killed them superficially on the surface and did not erase their existences entirely, they just managed to get hold of these demon lords’ cores somehow, preventing them from reviving but also keeping the possibility of their revival," William spoke with a sigh.

Maris kept spiraling inside her mind with the questions that kept piling up. "So... why would they ever try to keep those cores intact and not destroy them?" she asked with a frown.

William looked at her with a serious expression. "If you think that the top priority of the prime astral wardens is to erase the demons, then you are mistaken, Maris."

He was about to continue, but he suddenly stopped. "Let’s continue this conversation later." He smiled lightly.

"I need to go out; it seems like we have created a lot of commotion outside." William chuckled and disappeared, leaving Mris alone with even more questions than before.

Meanwhile, William’s figure appeared outside in his camp’s barracks, and he heard a loud commotion from outside. Without waiting for anything, he walked outside and saw cadets and officers had come out of their barracks and training sessions into the open.

Everyone stood under the dark sky and collectively looked at a golden spark in a direction that shone like the sun and created unsettling howls. The winds were moving restlessly as if a storm was approaching.

William saw all this; to him, the scene felt as if the world was ending.

[The Demon annihilator has been summoned, the array is pouring the energy of light stones into the halo.]

[The immense energy of such high purity light stones is infuencing the atmosphere of the whole of Aris, it seems it will be raining soon above your camp]

"Just tell me that we are not going to be the reason for the end of this world right??" William asked with a twitching expression.

[No, we will just be responsible for more global warming :D]

"(┬┬﹏┬┬) bro, you need to be more serious at times, tell me whether heavenly Will is gonna interfere??"

[Unless the demon annhilator tries to break open a pathway to the abyss which seems quite possible, its bloodthirst for demons unmatched after all]

"Well, thank me, I purchased these Demon annihilator summoning formations in those two years of selling chocolates, too bad I only have 3 more left." William shook his head in relief.

"now i just hope it kills as much demons as possible, wish i could see it from my own eyes"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 223. I was blind - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Fucker, die!!!"

Andrea screamed with raw fury and desperation bleeding into her every syllable.

"Andrea!!!" Inari’s urgent voice sounded from behind, calling her out of panic.

Andrea, covered in demonic blood, stopped and looked back at Inari. Her chest rose and fell rapidly as she tried to steady her breathing.

Inari pointed towards a certain direction with a distressed expression. Her face had lost its calm, replaced by clear unease as her golden eyes reflected something enormous in the distance.

Andrea turned to look at the direction she pointed to, only to find the colossal knight’s halo walking slowly towards them.

The ground trembled faintly with every movement of the halo; cracks spread across the already devastated terrain.

"Shit, it’s coming to kill Bellial!!" Andrea muttered as she looked at the colossal being.

Her voice carried a mixture of exhaustion, dread, and grim satisfaction. Even in the middle of chaos, she understood exactly what the knight had appeared for.

Inari immediately appeared beside Andrea.

"Let’s move; there is only so much we can do right now," she said while giving a second glance at Bellial’s body.

Her gaze lingered for only a moment, but within that brief glance lay centuries of experience and quiet understanding. Even someone as powerful as her knew when the battlefield had moved beyond mortal control.

"We can only make sure that this impotent dog gets killed in the most gruesome way for now," she said with faint anger lingering in her words.

Her voice was calm, but the subtle tightening of her fingers revealed the frustration she was holding back.

She was frustrated too; just a few days ago she had been calmly resting in her domain while teaching her disciple assisting her in a breakthrough.

Suddenly she received a distress letter from Andrea.

Not just her; almost every Astral Warden had gotten it. Andrea had utilized emergency communication crystals to contact every member of the council.

Yet out of the 20 junior astral wardens, only she had come to help Andrea; the rest only had excuses.

Above the juniors were 7 elders in the Astral Warden Council, and above them were four primarchs.

For elders and primarchs to make a move in a conflict unrelated to their respective domains, there was supposed to be a voting process where the primarchs held the most voting power and juniors retained the least of it.

The system had been designed to maintain order and prevent reckless intervention in worldly affairs, but over time it had become a convenient excuse for inaction.

When a week ago a call for assistance had been made, it was concluded that only junior astral wardens may assist Andrea; the way the voting process had been conducted was very hard on the academy.

It was decided that the elders and primarchs were not supposed to intervene at all. Even then, out of 20 junior astral wardens, only Inari had responded.

Andrea knew this, but her mind had been too occupied saving the academy to do anything about it, yet now she realized that she wasn’t even informed about the method of killing a demon lord.

The realization slowly sank into her mind like poison spreading through veins.

The attitude, which earlier seemed unbothered to her, now seemed suspicious. The primarchs had not even cared when the academy they themselves had established was on the verge of going to ruins.

The thought gnawed at her like a festering wound.

Her eyes burned with frustration and fury; this was not the first time such a thing had happened.

Memories she had buried long ago began resurfacing with painful clarity.

15 years ago, when her sister Anasthasia was suffering from a crisis and her family was on the verge of ruin, these astral wardens, instead of helping them out, had chosen silence, and years later they had welcomed the Pope as a part of the elders.

The hypocrisy of it all twisted in her chest like a knife. As the countless instances of their partiality as well as silence repeated and layered in her mind, Andrea’s heart burned with fury. She felt angered and betrayed.

Every memory added another spark to the growing fire inside her.

Her bloodshot eyes looked at the colossus knight slamming his foot on the mangled body of Bellial, who flashed a smile to Andrea before becoming paste.

The demon lord’s grin was grotesque, filled with mockery even in the face of death, as if he found amusement in the chaos he had unleashed in Andrea’s mind.

Then the massive foot descended.

With a wet, crushing explosion, the demon lord’s body was flattened, splattering dark blood across the shattered ground.

As soon as Bellial died, the knight smashed his sword on the ground and roared again towards the sky as if challenging the natural order of this world.

He took the other sword and slashed it into the sky, cleaving the space in an attempt to open a pathway to more demons. The motion was so immense that the air seemed to tear apart under the blade.

The whole island shook, and clouds started rumbling.

Dark masses of clouds began spiraling above the battlefield, drawn together by invisible forces responding to the knight’s challenge.

"Andrea, let’s move away!!! It’s the heavens; the knight is fighting the heavens!!!" Inari nudged the silent Andrea, who nodded quietly and moved away with her.

Even beings as powerful as them knew that staying within the range of such a clash would mean certain destruction.

Rumble!!

The clouds thundered violently, rolling across the sky like an ocean in a storm.

And a purple beam shot forth from the sky towards the knight.

The beam descended like divine judgment, carrying immense pressure that bent the air around it.

ROAAAAARR!!!

ROAAAAARR!!!

ROAAAAARR!!!

ROAAAAARR!!!

This time the knight roared from his four heads; his roar spread throughout the Aris, inflicting fear and dread among the common populace.

Across distant towns and villages, people froze in terror as the unnatural roar echoed through the sky. The purple beam clashed with the knight’s sword, who, in return, pushed further.

The collision created an explosion of light that illuminated the entire island, casting enormous shadows across the burning battlefield.

And from there started an exchange of severe attacks between them.

Blades of divine light slashed upward while bolts of heavenly energy rained down, turning the sky into a battlefield between godlike forces.

And that remained until the formation running in the depth of the academy premises lost all its energy, and the 10,000 light stones got discharged due to overload.

The whole area started burning under immense heat, and the temporary array broke down. Cracks spread through the magical circuit lines, and one by one the glowing light stones shattered like glass.

In an instant the demon annihilator’s summoning got canceled, and his figure got disintegrated into light particles.

The colossal knight slowly dissolved; its massive form broke apart into countless glowing fragments that scattered into the air like falling stars.

The heavens rumbled for a moment, declaring their victory before disappearing.

Soon the storm clouds dispersed, leaving behind only destruction and demonic corpses. Cold winds roared above, bringing signs of rain that would quench the thirst of the fallen warriors.
