Chapter 224. I was blind - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Holy Sun Empire...

Imperial Palace.

At the top of the palace in a grand crystal-lit hall, Pope Winston was overseeing a ritual; in front of him, a bright altar was raised, and a sword floated on top while rotating slowly like a projection.

The hall itself was vast and majestic, its walls made of polished white stone engraved with golden scripture praising the Light God Lux.

On the hilt of the sword, a bright yellow crystal cross remained embedded and pulsed like a beating heart, making the sword appear like a living being.

Winston looked at the 100 people in front of him, normal brainwashed citizens of Holy Sun, people with dreams, people with a life, kneeling in front of him with hope and reverence.

Their faces were filled with devotion and faith, eyes shining with the belief that they were about to receive divine blessings.

With cold eyes, he raised his staff over their heads and said, "Be ready to accept the embrace of the light god Lux, O’ chosen ones. You will be in the heavens soon, the place of eternal peace and rest. Be proud, for you are the most pure-hearted souls handpicked by Lux himself."

With that, he slammed the staff on the floor, and several formations got activated. Golden runes ignited across the floor, spreading outward like blooming flowers made of light.

The arrays moved and drew energy from the bodies of the kneeling figures.

For the people, their breath turned heavy, and their blood ran cold; before they knew it, they lost consciousness. The skin on their bodies started aging, their hair fell and disintegrated, and their eyeballs fell off as if life were being drawn out of them.

The horrifying transformation happened in complete silence.

Winston looked at the process coldly; only a few individuals were present in the room. They were the pope’s trusted cardinals, the only people in the whole Aris who knew about his true deeds.

Even though they stood silently, their expressions were carefully controlled.

As the process of absorption was over, the arrays that were rotating clockwise changed their rotations to counter it and started emitting threads of light energy.

The sword on the altar caught those wisps as if powering itself on them.

Before long, the blade of the sword elongated, and a few more yellow gems, much more powerful and radiant than normal light stones, appeared, embedded in the sword.

Soon, the process was over, and silence reigned in the ritual hall.

"Prepare the next batch," Winston turned his head slightly to the side and ordered.

The cardinals standing behind him nodded silently as if too used to the horrific scene they had just witnessed. The cardinals moved out of the hall, leaving Winston alone in silence.

BOOM!!!

Suddenly, tremors erupted across the Aris, and even the pope felt it standing on the highest floor of the palace. The sky darkened suddenly.

Winston’s eyes widened with alarm, and he hurriedly walked towards a window. His gaze looked at the bright star-like light shining in the far west of his empire.

"What the..."

Suddenly, something pulsed in the periphery of his vision.

Winston turned his head only to see the divine weapon of Lux spinning faster and pulsing with light as if getting excited about something.

Winston’s breath caught, and his head turned towards the source of this phenomenon, wondering what just happened out of the blue that earned a reaction from a divine weapon.

***

Five days later...

The world had not yet recovered from the chaos of the battle. The academy was still being rebuilt, wounded warriors were still being treated, and rumors about the final battle between the demon lord and a colossal being had spread across the continent like wildfire.

Yet Andrea had not rested even once during those five days. While the outside world healed, her thoughts had only grown colder.

"So you are saying you want to quit your position as an astral warden??" A sound reverberated across a dark hall.

The hall was enormous, almost cathedral-like in scale, but instead of stained glass and holy symbols, it was filled with cold marble pillars and stone carvings depicting the heaven’s council’s history.

Andrea stood facing the wall in front of her; meanwhile, the voice that had just sounded came from one of the four thrones resting in a line several feet above where she stood.

The thrones were carved from black stone, massive structures embedded into the elevated platform like immovable monuments.

One had to raise his face to look at those figures; the difference in elevations of both Andrea and the primarchs was a symbolic setting.

The raised platform symbolized their authority - those above judged, and those below obeyed.

It remained a custom from countless years; however, today, Andrea felt the difference in these elevations too glaring.

The physical height suddenly felt less like a tradition and more like arrogance carved into architecture.

She did not raise her head and meet their eyes.

The shadows of the primarchs stretched across the floor, long and oppressive, yet Andrea kept her gaze fixed forward as if those shadows did not exist.

She did not want to; she did not bow when she arrived today.

That alone had already caused subtle ripples of tension within the hall.

Normally, every Astral Warden would bow upon entering this sacred chamber, acknowledging the authority of the Primarchs.

Her posture was straight, and even her shoulders refused to bow in front of the immense pressure they were being put through.

Invisible pressure pressed down on her like a mountain, as a result of the combined aura of the Primarchs.

Yet Andrea remained unmoved.

"Yes, Primarch," Andrea spoke with a cold expression that refused to betray what was going on through her mind.

Her voice was steady, calm, and completely devoid of hesitation.

But beneath that calm exterior, memories and realizations from the past five days had already solidified into something unshakable.

"Why??" another voice sounded from one of the seats.

For a brief moment, silence filled the hall again.

"I was blind; fighting Bellial made me realize that I was shackled by my decisions. I realized that I did not deserve this position. I want to leave the council and just fulfill my duties as the principal of the academy."

Andrea spoke; she meant something entirely different, but she was sure that these people would mistake her words to be self-loathing when, in actuality, they meant the complete opposite.

In truth, her words were not born from weakness but from clarity.

For years, she had believed the council represented justice and balance.

The battle with Bellial had shattered that illusion.

"There is no need to blame yourself; I think you should take some time and think about it." Another primarch spoke from behind the dark.

The figure speaking was barely visible, hidden behind layers of darkness and faint starlight radiating from the throne.

To the outside world, the Primarchs were revered as near-divine beings, those who were just a step away from being gods.

Yet to Andrea now, they only looked like silhouettes hiding from accountability.

"I’ve had five days to come to this decision, Primarchs; I think I am sure about this," Andrea said.

The primarchs went quiet for a moment. The silence that followed was heavy and unnatural, stretching across the hall like an invisible veil.

Andrea knew they were communicating mentally.

A few moments later, she felt the primarchs shift on their seats.

The movement was subtle, but the pressure in the room changed slightly, and her shoulders relaxed.

"Fine, you are freed from the responsibility of an Astral Warden; from this day on, your decisions will either not concern the heaven’s council at all or will completely go against it," a primarch declared.

The words carried the weight of final judgment. For countless individuals, being dismissed from the Astral Wardens would mean disgrace, but for Andrea, it was freedom from something that had lost all meaning and relevance.

Andrea just nodded silently and took her leave.

She did not look back.

From today, she will do everything in her power and expose the flaws and schemes running deep among these people who refused to protect their own people.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 225. leave the rest to fate
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

10 days after the academy’s crisis ended...

The world had slowly begun to settle after the chaos that had shaken the entire continent.

Rumors of the battle against the demon lord and the academy’s success in repelling the demons had spread through every empire and city.

People whispered about divine knights descending from the heavens, while many debated the implications of such an event.

Yet beneath the surface of this temporary calm, tensions were quietly rising across Aris.

Riverdale Empire, Imperial Palace...

Inside one of the chambers of the palace that overlooked the sprawling capital below, two men casually sat across from each other like old friends.

"So the eternal sovereign told you to stop your attack, and you decided to comply?" Klaus’s voice rang through Aurelius’s chambers while his face showed squinted eyes that refused to believe what he was hearing.

The chamber was filled with towering bookshelves, military maps pinned across the walls, and stacks of documents scattered across the large desk where Aurelius had been working tirelessly for the past several days.

"That’s not the full picture; he informed me that my plan was leaked by someone to the cult and that they were now laying a trap for my army. The eternal sovereign warned me and asked me to clear out traitors living under my own nose before going for the ones outside the empire," Aurelius said.

As he spoke, his gaze remained fixed on the map spread across the table. She had been trying to come up with a solution to draw the cultists out of their base and kill them instead of rushing inside and walking into the trap that had been laid for them.

Aurelius had no idea what the cults had prepared, but he did not want to test the Eternal Sovereign’s claims and risk losing his men and reputation.

Klaus’s eyes narrowed.

The normally carefree warrior leaned slightly forward, his sharp gaze studying Aurelius as if trying to determine whether the emperor had lost his mind.

"And you just believed him??"

"I intend to clear my record as an emperor, not ruin it even more than it already has by risking lives of my men, if there is even a fraction of truth to eternal sovreign’s claim then it’s worth waiting my plan out, after all the whole point of striking so fast was to take them offgaurd, since that objective is out of question i will have to think of something else" Aurelius sighed and replied while loosening his posture a bit.

The exhaustion in his voice was difficult to hide.

For days, he had barely slept, juggling political pressure, public outrage, and the growing threat of the cults in public perception.

"That’s so unlike you!! Where is your temper, dude?" Klaus complained as he looked at the lifeless Aurelius in front of him.

He gestured dramatically with his hands, clearly frustrated with the emperor’s uncharacteristically calm behavior.

"Klaus!! Are you here to help or bicker??" Aurelius asked, tired of Klaus’s shenanigans.

He rubbed the bridge of his nose, trying to suppress the growing headache forming behind his temples.

"Tsk, again, where is the aggression???" Klaus spat.

He leaned back against a nearby table, crossing his arms with a dissatisfied expression.

"How will you burn those cultist bastards if you can’t even be decisive?"

Aurelius looked at him.

His expression remained composed, but there was a faint flicker of irritation in his eyes.

"You were the first person on Aris to believe in Eternal Sovereign, and it turns out he was right in your case, so just shut up when I am doing the same."

Klaus made an annoyed expression when he saw that Aurelius had a genuine point.

His lips twisted slightly as if he had just been forced to swallow a bitter pill.

He was really the first person to believe in Eternal Sovereign.

"Tsk, I guess you are right," he said while folding his arms before giving him a look.

"You know, if you had been more aware of the kidnappings, you at least could have avoided all the hate you are getting from the public, which is mainly pointed at your ignorance," Klaus said.

Aurelius gritted his teeth.

The mention of the kidnappings immediately darkened his expression.

"Tsk, the minister of law enforcement was either really dumb or maybe he was just a part of Daniel’s faction from the start."

The words came out colder than before. Behind the emperor’s calm demeanor, frustration simmered quietly.

Klaus looked at Aurelius.

"I would have had him executed till now."

Aurelius heard Klaus’s careless comment.

"I can, but if I do it, then Daniel will just get another reason to get people against me; he would declare me a tyrant."

The political situation inside the empire was already fragile. Every decision Aurelius made was being scrutinized by nobles, ministers, and the public alike.

Klaus just sighed.

"If only your father were still alive!" he said before looking at the ceiling of the chamber.

"Well, he still left a great lesson for me."

"What lesson??" Aurelius left what he was doing and looked at Klaus.

"You know, having only one kid and self-control," Klaus replied, making Aurilius’s face twitch; he just decided to ignore Klaus’s mocking comments.

The corner of Klaus’s lips curled upward in amusement.

Suddenly, the door to his study was knocked on. Aurelius looked at the butler who had just arrived.

The elderly man stood respectfully at the doorway, dressed in the traditional uniform of the imperial household.

"My lord, you have visitors," the butler bowed in front of Aurelius and spoke.

Aurelius gave a look at Klaus and then nodded.

"Send them here."

A few minutes passed, and two figures entered the room. The heavy wooden doors opened slowly, revealing two familiar silhouettes.

Both Klaus and Aurelius stood up.

"Miss Andrea!!" both exclaimed at the same time.

Andrea, with Kevin behind her, entered Aurelius’s chambers.

Her presence immediately changed the atmosphere of the room.

Even without releasing any aura, the authority surrounding her as the principal of the academy and formerly an Astral Warden was impossible to ignore.

Klaus froze.

For a brief moment, the usually fearless warrior looked like a child caught misbehaving.

"I will take my leave," he said before bowing at her.

Andrea nodded with a smile, and in an instant, Klaus’s figure disappeared. He was embarrassed about the way he had been sitting earlier when these two had just entered the room.

Aurelius offered them a seat and sat across from them with a straight posture.

Despite being the ruler of an empire, he still felt a certain level of respect and caution toward someone like Andrea.

"I hope the academy is recovering well from the battle it has gone through; Riverdale is ready to provide any assistance to the academy," Aurelius spoke, albeit a bit nervously, at the sudden arrival of Principal Andrea.

The battle that had ended ten days ago had shaken the entire continent. Helping the academy rebuild would also help stabilize public perception and establish a good relationship with them.

"I thank you for your kind words, but we are not here for your assistance, Aurelius," Andrea spoke directly to the point.

"We are here to provide our assistance."

Aurelius’s brow furrowed. The unexpected statement caught him completely off guard.

"Sorry, but may I ask what you mean by that?" he asked, puzzled by Andrea’s words.

"I mean to say that we are here to assist you in attacking the Clayman cult’s base," Andrea said.

"What?? Why?" Aurelius stood up in shock.

The chair behind him scraped slightly against the marble floor.

"Who even told you about that??" he asked.

The information had been truly limited only to the dukes; if one of them told the academy, it raised questions about their loyalty.

Aurelius was shocked to his core that the information had traveled so fast.

But before he could make any further assumptions, Andrea cut him off.

"We were informed by the eternal sovereign; he wants us to get involved in this," Andrea spoke.

Aurilues felt another wave of shock pass through him.

"May I ask why you are complying with the eternal sovereign? I don’t think you owe him anything."

He had to ask this.

"It’s none of your business, Aurelius; we have an understanding between us, and that’s all you need to know for now," Andrea spoke without hiding her intentions.

Her gaze remained steady.

There was no room for negotiation in her tone.

"Anyways, when are you going to attack?" she asked straightforwardly.

Aurelius looked at both Kevin and Andrea.

The eternal sovereign had made these two people move and gotten them involved in his plan. Aurelius couldn’t understand why the eternal sovereign was helping him so much. He wanted to understand his intentions.

Andrea, as if reading Aurelius’s thoughts, spoke lightly.

"It will be good if you focus on things that are in your hands and leave the rest to fate; trust me, that’s the way forward," she said with a smile.

Chapter 226. Opening up to Seraphine - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Sylvaris Empire...

Within the inner palace complex, one particular chamber remained unusually quiet during the early hours of the morning.

Soft sunlight slipped through the tall crystal windows, casting golden streaks across silk curtains and polished marble floors.

"SERAPHINE!!!!!!!" an angry female voice echoed through Seraphine’s chamber.

The shout shattered the peaceful silence like thunder ripping through peacefull clouds.

Servants passing through nearby corridors immediately froze, knowing very well whose voice had just erupted through the palace halls.

Sera, who was sleeping on her bed, woke up with a flinch; her eyes opened up so quickly she did not even have time to register her surroundings.

Her body jolted upright instinctively, her heart suddenly started racing as if she had been dragged out of a battlefield alarm rather than a peaceful sleep.

Soon the surroundings settled.

The soft curtains swayed lightly from the open balcony doors, and the faint scent of morning flowers drifted into the room.

"Mom!! What happened? Are we under attack?? Is there any trouble?" Her voice carried genuine panic as she scanned the room quickly.

Selena sylvaris, who was just standing at the side of her bed, looked at Sera and asked while flashing a book in front of her like it was some forbidden artifact.

Seraphine’s eyes fluttered for a moment, then her cheeks became red due to embarrassment.

The realization struck her like lightning. She looked down and could not meet her mother’s gaze.

Last night she must have forgotten to keep her novel back in her storage ring.

Now she knew she messed up big time as she saw Selena gritting her teeth.

Her fingers tightened slightly around the book as if she was restraining herself from tearing it apart on the spot.

"Who gave you this??"

Sera remained silent; she didn’t know what to do; it was the first time she was being scolded by her mom.

Her mind raced frantically for an excuse, but none came. Sera just kept looking down and did not meet her eyes.

"Look at me; you are not getting away with this!" Selena shouted while her face was red from embarrassment.

Her voice echoed loudly through the chamber.

Just reading the title made her understand that this book was filled with ideas she did not want Seraphine to have at such a delicate age.

The cover alone was enough to make the normally composed empress feel like her blood pressure had doubled.

"Was it your maid? Should I get her executed for exposing you to indecency?" Selena asked.

For the first time, Seraphine tensed up and looked at her. Her wide eyes immediately filled with panic.

"No!! "Mom, it’s just a gift from Katherine!!" she spoke meekly and awkwardly.

Her voice came out hurried and nervous, the words tumbling out before she could stop them.

"What?? You mean Kathrine Nightreign!! The daughter of Viviane Nightreign??" Selena asked Seraphine.

Her eyes widened slightly, and a dangerous spark flickered within them.

It was almost as if nodding to this question might unlock an expression on Selena’s face that Sera had never seen.

"That bitch!! She is trying to manipulate my daughter!!"

Selena confirmed the facts from Seraphine’s silence.

Her anger exploded to a whole new level. A wave of curses followed for a few hours before Selena finally cooled down.

Servants passing through nearby corridors silently prayed that the empress’s rage would eventually settle and won’t shift on them.

Heaving with heavy breaths, Selena looked at Seraphine.

"We need to have a serious talk about this, Sera. Get ready and come to my study."

Selena said before walking away with Sera’s novel in her hands.

The door to her chapbers slammed behind her with a firm thud.

As soon as Selena left, Seraphine took a deep breath and sat back relaxed. Her entire body sank into the soft mattress as the tension drained from her shoulders.

"What a hassle !!" she said feeling a headache.

"Aishh!! I want to return already and meet William, if not for this," Sera said as she looked at a small black tattoo on her wrist before sighing.

"Sera!! Can you hear me??" Suddenly William’s voice rang in her head.

Slowly Sera’s sleepy expression turned surprised. The way the colors on her face changed would have made William laugh for hours.

Sera looked around before clutching her head.

"Ugh!! Were dreams not enough already? I am hearing his voice when I am awake too!!"

She rubbed her temples as if trying to chase the voice away.

"Sera, I know you are hearing my voice. Please reply. Are you pissed about something?!!"

As if instinctively, Sera spoke in her head.

"No!! Why would I be pissed off!!"

Her reply came out reflexively before she even realized what she was doing.

"Thank heavens, you replied," William’s voice turned soft while Sera turned shocked.

Was William really talking to her???

"William!! Are you really there?!!" she asked with an alarmed expression.

On the other end, William chuckled.

"Yes, do you want to see me now??"

"What!!"

Sera quickly stood up and started searching for every corner of her chamber, even the outside balcony and every place she believed William could be hiding.

She checked behind curtains, under tables, and even leaned over the balcony railing to scan the palace gardens below.

"I can’t find you here!! Are you using that invisibility robe of yours?" Sera asked curiously.

"Hahaha!! Seems like my Sera started searching for me." William chuckled.

Sera pouted before crossing her arms. Her cheeks puffed slightly in annoyance.

"Of course you dumbo!!!"

William did not let her continue.

"ok, follow the instructions I am about to give to you, and we will be seeing each other."

His voice suddenly carried a hint of seriousness.

"Alright," Sera said with her brows raised.

"Focus on the link through which I am talking to you; try to feel it!!" William spoke.

"There is a link??" Sera asked, surprised.

"Just do as I say, Sera, focus!!!" William reiterated.

Sera went silent and closed her eyes as she tried to feel the link, and surprisingly, she was able to do that in an instant.

It felt like a faint thread connecting her to somewhere far away.

"Pour your mana into the mark and will for yourself to appear in front of me."

Sera took a deep breath and focused, willing to be at William’s side.

Suddenly a warm breeze brushed past her skin, and the temperature became much warmer than before.

A pair of hands cupped her face. She opened her eyes only to find William’s soft, loving eyes looking at her.

Sera’s breaths hitched. Her heart pounded loudly in her chest.

She knew it!!

This was not a dream, not at all; this was reality.

"I missed you," William spoke softly before hugging her tight.

His arms wrapped around her gently, pulling her into a warm embrace that immediately made the distance of the past days disappear.

Sera returned the affection by holding William close.

Her fingers tightened slightly on his clothes as if reassuring herself that he was truly there.

"I missed you too," Sera spoke lightly before lightly landing a peck on his cheeks.

She then looked around only to see a maple tree above them and surrounding that seemed too familiar yet unrecognizable for her.

The leaves of the maple tree glowed with warm shades of red and orange as sunlight filtered through them.

A soft wind rustled the branches, scattering a few leaves across the grassy ground.

She could not remember when she was here last time, but she was sure she was here before.

There was something nostalgic about the place, like a half-forgotten memory waiting to be recalled.

She turned to look at William curiously.

"William!! "What place is this?? And how did you bring me here?" she asked, her mind racing.

The way William had called her was too mysterious.

Even though she was sure that William was real and not a dream, she still found it hard to believe.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 227. Opening up to Seraphine - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"William!! "What place is this?? And how did you bring me here?" Sera’s curious voice rang.

Her voice carried both amazement and confusion as her eyes darted around the surroundings.

A soft breeze passed through the maple leaves above them, creating a gentle rustling sound that filled the silence between them.

William sighed; it was time to tell Seraphine about some things he had hesitated to do before, but now that he had decided to be truthful to those he cared about, he decided to come clean.

For a long time, he had kept several secrets buried within himself. The system, the domain, the mark, and many other mysteries surrounding him were things he had carefully hidden from the world.

But Seraphine was different.

"This place is a domain of infinity; it is a personal domain that I use," William said before elaborating further, explaining the features of the domain to her.

His voice remained calm as he described the strange rules of this place, the way time moved differently, the way the environment could be altered according to his will, and the countless possibilities the domain offered.

He spoke slowly, making sure Sera could understand everything without being in a hurry.

He did not worry, and neither did the system stop him.

According to the system, Sera was already bound to the mark of destiny; she could not betray William even if she wanted to.

William never doubted betrayal from Sera anyway; the only worry he had was convincing the system, which surprisingly never objected.

Even now, the system remained completely silent.

"Whoa!! Really!! 1 day outside is equivalent to a year here??" Sera asked, amazed by what William said.

Her eyes sparkled brightly, reflecting pure excitement.

Strangely, William was feeling proud looking at Sera’s shiny eyes, like a child showing off his toys to his crush.

"Yes!! And that’s not the end; there are more features, you know, hehe!!" William spoke, and till now his nose’s bridge tightened with pride.

There was a faint smugness in his tone as if he had just revealed the most impressive thing in his collection.

Sera looked into the sky.

The sky of the domain stretched endlessly above them, a calm blue expanse that seemed far larger than any sky she had ever seen.

"Ah, I wish I could see the whole expanse from above," she said with a smile.

Her voice carried a dreamy tone as she imagined what the vast domain might look like from the sky.

William, who stood behind her, flashed a grin. A mischievous idea instantly formed in his mind.

"Will-waait!!" she said as she felt William’s hand on her waist; she did not mind, but was flustered because the touch was unexpected.

Her cheeks flushed slightly as his hand rested firmly around her waist.

The sudden closeness made her heartbeat quicken.

"Just trust me," he whispered softly before pulling her closer to him. His voice was gentle and teasing.

"Climb on my feet," he said.

Sera nodded with a blush and climbed to her feet; she was here barefoot after all; she hadn’t expected to meet William the first thing after leaving her bed.

Her toes curled slightly as they touched the top of his feet.

She was turned away from his face, so there was nothing she could hold onto. She just held his hands that were wrapped around her waist

William slowly floated into the sky.

The ground beneath them gradually moved farther away as the wind brushed gently against their bodies.

Sera flustered, "Will, how are we flying??" Her voice carried both excitement and nervousness.

"Don’t panic!!" Will said as he raised the altitude.

The maple tree below them slowly shrank as they ascended higher.

Sera had never taken a flight herself, only shallow floats with her spirits, but the altitude William was floating at was already several feet above the ground, making Sera nervous.

The wind now moved faster around them, gently tugging at her hair.

"Oh shit!! William, don’t let me fall from here!!!" Sera panicked.

Her fingers instinctively clutched his clothes tighter. William chuckled and tightened his grip on her waist.

He pulled her and rested her head against his chest. "Calm down, Sera!! I would never let you get hurt."

His voice carried a reassuring warmth.

Sera looked down at the serene landscape; the fear slowly dissolved as she felt William’s warmth behind her.

The vast domain stretched endlessly below them, rolling grasslands, scattered forests, and distant hills glowing under soft sunlight.

She cupped his face.

Her fingers brushed gently across his cheek.

"Thank you," she said.

William replied with a peck on her cheeks while they slowly floated around the vast domain.

The moment felt strangely peaceful.

"By the way, I feel like I have been here before. Did you bring me here in the past?"

William pursed his lips. He had expected this question eventually.

"Remember the time you got kidnapped during the mission a few months back?"

"Yeah," William could not see Sera’s expression since her face was facing away from him, but if one looked carefully, they would see the smile on Sera’s face had disappeared; a premonition rose in her heart, and her eyes flickered fast, indicating her inner turmoil.

Memories of that day resurfaced in her mind.

"Actually, I was informed of your kidnapping the same day by someone, and then out of worry, I started searching and finally found you. Then, after saving you from kidnappers, I brought you here to spend some time." William said in a steady tone, realizing that Sera’s breathing and heart rate suddenly dimmed, like she was holding her breath.

He knew he was going to get a reaction, but he was prepared.

"That day you were under the effect of a severe dosage of sedatives," William spoke as he started rubbing her shoulders and hands from behind.

"That was the day of our first kiss," William concluded with a sigh.

Sera remained stunned for a moment before she turned around in mid-air while balancing her steps on William’s.

Seeing this, William turned cold.

Wasn’t she scared of this height just now? he thought.

He looked at Sera, who was now a bit distressed as she looked at William.

"So I was holding myself back for nothing!! Why did you tell me so late, you dummy!!!"

Sera screamed and punched him lightly on his chest. Before grabbing his face and planting a deep kiss on his lips.

William’s heart beat like a drum watching Sera’s behavior; she was not the meek, shy Sera that he had pictured in his mind.

Her boldness completely caught him off guard.

Several moments passed as Sera did not let William go; only now did they end their kiss and take deep breaths that they were holding.

William looked at Sera’s satisfied expression as if she had a meal after going hungry for several months.

There was an unmistakable happiness glowing on her face. She hugged him tightly after that.

"So from now on we will meet often, right?? No matter where we are!!"

William nodded; from inside, he was thankful that Sera was not asking about the white raven who had saved her in front of the world.

William wouldn’t hide it if Seraphine asked him, but he still wanted his Sera to stay away from the mess she was not a part of.

Although how long William would be able to hide that fact from her was a question of its own.

Suddenly, Sera pointed at something below. Her eyes lit up with curiosity again.

"Oh, look!! "There is a small house down there!!" Sera said.

From above, the small wooden house looked cozy, sitting quietly beneath the shade of several tall trees.

"Did you make it to pass free time, or do you often do your cultivation in there?" Seraphine looked at William excitedly.

"Let’s go there!!" she said.

William looked at the house with an awkward laugh.

The cheerful excitement on Sera’s face contrasted heavily with the growing nervousness in his heart.

He had just been thinking about avoiding divulging the information about his identity as eternal sovereign to Sera.

It seems like he did not have a choice now; Sera would see Lia lying in there, and William would have to go through a questionnaire.

Maybe Sera would suspect him of cheating on her and would get pissed off at him; maybe she would stop talking to him.

William sighed as he looked at Sera’s cheerful gaze. He was ready to go through this.

After all, Sera was his woman, the love of his life, and someone he would protect and provide for.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 228. Opening up to Seraphine - 3
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Let’s go." William nodded.

The decision came out calmly, but inside his chest, there was a faint tension that he could not completely hide.

His gaze briefly drifted toward the small house in the distance before returning to Seraphine.

Sera sensed the change of expressions on his face but did not show it; she understood that there was something in that house that William was too insecure to show her.

Her eyes softened slightly as she observed him quietly. She had known William long enough to recognize when something weighed on his mind.

She nodded and let William lead her there; she wanted to see with her own eyes what was bothering her so much.

A few moments later, William arrived in front of the hut with Sera.

The small wooden structure looked humble compared to the vastness of the domain. It stood quietly beneath a cluster of tall trees; its roof was covered with moss, and the faint scent of herbs was drifting from the windows.

Maris came out, making William surprised.

The door creaked slightly as it opened, and a familiar figure stepped outside carrying a small bag.

"Maris, what are you doing here?" Will asked; he had long given her her own house to live in.

He hadn’t expected anyone else to be here today, especially not Maris.

Maris looked at Seraphine, who looked back at her expressionlessly.

For a brief moment, the two women simply studied each other curiously.

"Greetings, my lord." She then bowed towards William.

"I was here to refill the rations; Barash, Ronin, and Amorphous already finished everything you gave a few days ago."

"Oh!!"

William almost forgot to give them more tasteful food as he was occupied with other stuff; he nodded silently and then pointed at Sera.

"She is Seraphine; I think I have told you about her earlier."

Maris’s eyes widened, and she bowed deeply.

"Oh, forgive me for not greeting you, Miss Sera."

Her bow this time was much deeper, almost touching the ground.

Sera’s composure cracked as a soft smile appeared.

"It’s all right, Maris; we didn’t know each other."

Her voice was gentle, and the tension that had briefly formed between them quickly dissolved.

Maris nodded.

"You may not know this servant, Miss Sera, but I know about you; you are our future mistress," she said.

Sera blinked slightly at the direct statement.

"My lord cares about you deeply; even though he may not show it, he always talks about you and misses you!!"

Maris spoke, making Sera blush and give William a sideways look filled with happiness and satisfaction.

Her cheeks warmed noticeably as she glanced at William with sparkling eyes.

William was reeling inside; he knew he had earned another breathtaking, deep kiss from Sera.

He silently showed a thumbs-up to Maris and decided to reward her later. Maris then bowed again and took her leave.

Her footsteps slowly faded as she walked away from the hut.

Sera turned to see her disappearing figure before turning to William. She nudged him and looked at him with a smile.

"So miss me often, huh?" she chuckled.

Her teasing tone made William feel slightly embarrassed. Then her brows rose, and she spoke with an evil smile.

"How about I leave my mark on you as well!!"

William giggled. "You have been getting naughty!!"

Sera giggled. "I have a surprise for you later!!"

Her eyes sparkled with mischief.

"Oh!!" William’s eyes lit up. "What is it??"

His curiosity immediately spiked.

"Surprises are not supposed to be revealed before time," Sera spoke before leaving him behind and walking towards the entrance of the house.

Her steps were light and confident as she approached the door.

Williams’ expression soured again as he saw her enter. His chest tightened slightly as he imagined the moment that was about to come.

With a sigh, he entered a few moments later only to see Sera standing near Lia’s peaceful figure.

The room inside the hut was quiet and dimly lit. A faint medicinal scent filled the air, and Lia lay on a bed in the center of the room; her body was pale and unmoving.

She looked almost like someone peacefully asleep.

Sera turned around to look at him; their eyes met. William tried to remain as stoic as possible, but Seraphine sensed something in them.

She came forward to stand in front of him, her hands cupping his face, and with a soft expression, she asked,

"Who is she?"

William felt the touch of her hands, and strangely, he thought he would not be able to lie; her touch wouldn’t let him, nor did he want to.

"She is Lia, Maximus’s lover," he said with a quiet sigh.

Seraphine’s eyes widened at the revelation.

"What!!"

A rare emotion of relief appeared on her face, but then she frowned.

"What is she doing here? Wasn’t she supposed to be kidnapped?"

"I managed to save her a few days ago before I saved you from the cultists."

Sera looked at him as if she could not believe what he was saying.

"I need you to tell me the whole picture, not one-line answers that don’t help other than only deepening my worry," she spoke with finality.

William looked at her lovingly before closing the distance between them.

"Sera, what I am about to tell you is one of my greatest secrets. I hope the amount of trust I am showing is enough testament to my loyalty to you."

Sera combed his hair before cupping his face.

"I never doubted your loyalty, William; I just want to know you better so that I can help you better. I don’t want to know everything you do; I just want to know enough to make sure you are safe and happy. That’s all I want."

She said before kissing him gently, William looked at her with a relieved expression.

"Sera, I am the eternal sovereign."

"Huh??"

Sera looked at him with an expression that refused to believe him at first.

But then, with the intensity she saw on his face, she slowly realized how true he was in his claim.

She then remembered the time when she was kidnapped; people had told her that an emissary of the eternal sovereign had saved her and handed her back to her mother.

She realized that it could only be William if he had truly saved her.

She then looked at Lia’s figure.

She remembered how Maximus had gotten letters from the eternal sovereign; she remembered William leaving before them for the mission.

So many events flashed in her mind one by one, reinforcing William’s claim.

The reality slowly settled as she got lost in her thoughts.

Suddenly, she felt something warm; she realized it was William hugging her while patting her head.

Eventually, under the warmth, Sera’s mind calmed; she realized that it did not matter what identity or mission William carried; she had decided to live with William for life; nothing was going to change between them after this day.

Her heart felt warm from the fact that William had finally decided to open up to her and reveal such a huge secret about him.

She decided not to ask any more questions.

She believed in herself and the choices she made; she was sure William would never disappoint her in any way.

Her head turned up to look at him as she rested her chin on his chest.

"I love you!!" she said.

"I love you till infinity," William replied with a rare smile.

Suddenly, Sera spoke after a few minutes of silence between them.

"So how are you going to win Lia over??" Sera asked.

"Huh??"

It was William’s turn to get shocked; he stuttered and asked,

"What do you mean??!!"

Sera squinted her eyes.

"Well, obviously, it seems you are going to make Lia your second wife, not that I mind you taking in more wives, but let me tell you beforehand, I will be the leader of all your harem."

"I was your first after all, hmph!!, and I will be screening you women; you will need my approval before joining the harem, hehe!"

William almost got his brain fried by what he was hearing Seraphine speak.

He tried to check her temperature and wondered if she was alright.

"What happened, William??" she asked.

William sighed.

"First of all, Lia isn’t going to be my second wife; I am just keeping her here because she is on the verge of death. If I don’t find a cure for her, she will die, and I have a reason to save her that is definitely not love and affection.

Second, are you telling me that you are alright about me marrying other women!!!??"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 229. Opening up to Seraphine - 4
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Are you telling me that you are alright about me marrying other women!!??" William asked with a flustered expression on his face.

His voice rose slightly in disbelief, his brows furrowed as he searched her face for any hint of teasing or hidden resentment. His grip on her shoulders tightened.

Seraphine looked at him with tight brows. "Yeah, I think that’s not uncommon." Sera spoke as she hugged him, "Also, it will be easier for me to handle you."

Her tone was casual, almost too casual, but the way she leaned into him revealed a quiet confidence like she had already thought through this far more than he had.

"Am I difficult to handle right now??" William asked with a visible frown. His pride took a small hit as he tilted his head, genuinely puzzled.

Sera just chuckled and looked at him with a gaze that only deepened his curiosity. There was something playful, something knowing in her eyes that made his heart beat a little faster.

Shaking his head, William could only sigh.

"Let’s go out; I have something to tell you," he said.

His tone turned more serious; the earlier confusion was now replaced with a resolve.

"Oh," Sera got curious about what he wanted to tell her; she followed his lead as William held her hands. Their fingers intertwined naturally, as if it were something they had done countless times before.

He brought her out and made her sit on the stairs of the hut.

The wooden steps were slightly warm from the sunlight, and a soft breeze carried the scent of the surrounding greenery.

William stood in front of Seraphine and caressed her cheeks and hair like he was trying to memorize every detail of her.

"Sera, I don’t know how this sounds, but since you say that you are open about me marrying another woman, there is someone I want you to know about."

Sera gasped, "Damn you, scoundrel!! Was Lia not enough!! You already have another woman in your sights." Her reaction was exaggerated, but there was a faint sharpness hidden beneath her tone.

William rubbed his temples, hearing her. "Sera, I am serious; I don’t love Lia."

Sera just chuckled.

"I know, I am just messing with you!!" Her laughter lightened the mood instantly.

William gave her a look.

"Are you really sure about me taking in other women, or are you just testing me?? Well, I am already about to fail your test," he sighed.

There was a hint of vulnerability in his voice now. Sera looked at William with a pursed smile; she did not say anything and just looked at him, all restless and flustered.

She saw him getting on his knees in front of her and matched their eye level. He then clasped her hands, to her amusement.

The gesture was unexpectedly sincere.

"Sera, you will always remain my first love, and I will love you forever; even death won’t do us apart," he said and reiterated again.

Sera chuckled. "Tsk, who is this woman that is making you so restless, Willy? You know I am getting a bit jealous now."

William shook her head. "No, it’s not about that woman right now; it’s about us. I want to make sure that you never forget what I am saying. I love you and will love you forever," William said.

This time his tone was steadier and firm; he even came forward and planted kisses all over Sera’s face.

Each touch was filled with warmth and urgency, as if he were trying to express everything words couldn’t carry.

"Uff!! If you only do that every hour, I will be satisfied," Sera muttered as she felt his kisses on her neck.

Her voice softened; her earlier teasing was now turning into something more affectionate. She then held her face and brought it up.

"It’s alright; I can take it. No need to go out of your way and try to make me happy; I was never sad to begin with," Sera reassured William again.

Her gaze steadied him. William nodded and came to sit behind her, taking her in her arms.

He rubbed her hands and started telling her about Tamasya.

He told her about the first time he met Tamsya, how she became his master, and how her heart broke when he had told Tamsya about them and their first kiss.

"So you couldn’t keep our kiss a secret, huh?" Sera looked at William with squinted eyes.

A teasing glint returned to her expression.

"Are you mad??" he asked with a light expression; he was feeling a burden come off his shoulder now that he had told Sera about Tamasya.

"I am not mad, actually. I am a bit happy that you told her about me beforehand so it won’t be difficult when I claim the first wife spot, hmph!!" Sera spoke lightly.

William held her cheeks and pulled them a bit, making her yelp

"Arhhh!!" she moaned in surprise, making William chuckle.

"Willy, you are getting bolder and bolder!!" Sera stood up and turned to look at him with a pout, her hands crossed together, and her face showed an artificial angry expression that William knew was just there to mess with him.

William couldn’t help but pull her closer and hug her waist; he rested his head on her and whispered, "Thank you!!!"

Sera looked down at him; she caressed his head lightly and smiled subtly. The warmth between them deepened at this point.

"So when will I meet my junior sister?" Sera asked.

William, who was resting his head on her waist, lost his smile. "I haven’t proposed to her," he said.

"Oh!!" Sera ruffled his blue hair and played with his locks. "I think you should do it sooner than later; don’t make her wait."

"Really??"

"Yes," Sera spoke while her gaze remained fixed on him.

"Ugh!! You don’t know what you have done for me, Sera!!" William exclaimed and suddenly stood up. His earlier tension completely replaced by excitement.

He picked Sera up in the air from her waist down.

"Oh, oii!! Stop, William!!"

He did not listen; he just swung her in circles lightly.

"Hahaha!!" Sera giggled as she felt what he was doing; her hair fluttered with the wind.

And before both of them knew it, William just lost his balance and fell to the ground with Sera.

Both of them looked at each other and giggled. A wave of nostalgia washed over them.

William often used to swing her this way when he used to meet her in Riverdale.

With time they had forgotten the innocence they once shared. But now, after the acceptance Sera had shown for William, he could not help but fall in love with her even more.

On the ground, William rolled towards the Sera and came above her, his hands locked firmly, pinning her hands on the ground. Sera blushed and her hands clasped together as she realized she had no way of escape.

William locked his eyes on hers. The world around them seemed to fade for a moment.

Sera gulped with hesitation; her mind was churning whether she should let William or not.

Without waiting, William plunged; he kissed her cheeks, her neck, and her forehead before going for her soft lips.

The moment was intense yet filled with affection rather than urgency.

Sera’s breath became heavy. "Will...mphh!" She was about to say something, but words did not come from her mouth.

William kept the intense kisses and smoches on, making Sera react softly. His hands now held the sides of her neck as he buried his face near her shoulder.

Before long, the moment deepened; William moved down her neck just as he reached a bit lower than her nape.

Boom!!!

An explosive slam hit William’s chest out of nowhere. The sudden impact shattered the atmosphere instantly.

"William!!" Sera gasped shockingly as she saw William being thrown a few meters away like a rag doll.

Her eyes widened in shock, and she clasped her mouth. Her gaze landed on her nape, where ancient marks pulsed with faint green energy.

The marks glowed, reacting violently as if protecting something sacred.

She covered her loosened robes before running toward William, who was stunned by the impact.

Sera regretted not having self-control. The immense pleasure she had been feeling blinded her from reality; she had forgotten to tell William about her physique.

Chapter 230. Morgan’s Bet
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"William!!" Sera gasped shockingly as she saw William being thrown a few meters away like a rag doll.

Meanwhile, William collapsed on the ground and looked at the sky in shock; he had not expected a sudden wave of energy hitting him out of nowhere.

He looked at the sky with weird emotions filled in his heart.

[I remember being called horny by someone. Just saying...]

William remained stunned on the ground, a small detail from the novel refreshed in his mind that he had earlier overlooked due to its insignificance compared to the rest of the events.

Sera came to his side and shook him with a worried expression. "William, are you alright!!" Now as Sera’s figure came into his view, he noticed the faint black tattoos glowing with green energy around them; his suspicion got confirmed.

William chuckled at the situation and shook his head, thinking how he had almost lost control over himself. Sera worried about William misunderstanding the situation, and to avoid getting him to think that she had consciously attacked him, she hurriedly started explaining.

"William, it’s my physique; it’s partially awakened, and it resists physical intimacy until you are approved by the world tree. I ..."

Sera was about to elaborate when William just stopped her and sealed her lips with his hands. "No need to explain, Sera; I understand. Sorry I got excited earlier even though you tried to warn me," he spoke with a sigh.

Sera’s shoulders slouched, feeling bad about the whole situation. She was confused about why the kisses had not been resisted, but something a bit deeper did.

"Let’s not delve into it now," William told Sera and stood up from the ground. Sera looked at William and decided to not inform him about the trial for now; she did not want him to have more burdens, unaware that he already knew about it.

William and Sera walked around the domain for a bit before Sera decided to say farewell. William informed her that he would contact her after a week.

Sera pouted; she knew in five days the semester would end at the academy and holidays would start. Everyone will have free time; she hoped to see William more often but knew that it would only happen a week later.

William reassured her again and again. He planted a few kisses, squarely avoiding the green symbols over her body. Sera chuckled at his attempt and caressed him lovingly before lovingly saying goodbyes.

"Oh, by the way, where is your surprise??" William asked

Seraphine turned and looked at him with a smirk. "Let it remain a surprise for next time," she giggled.

"Oh!! For a moment I thought the way you slammed me before was your surprise." William joked with a chuckle, making Sera lose her smile.

"Tsk, you scoundrel!!!" she pinched Will lightly on the stomach before turning to leave the realm.

William remained there laughing for a few moments before taking a deep breath.

[Are you ready now?]

William nodded. "Before that, I need to reward Maris for her good work."

William appeared beside Maris in the domain and gifted her stocks of delicious foods, a personal TV of her own, and several other things that left her bowing and thanking him for hours.

William, meanwhile, started making preparations for the conquest in a few days.

In another corner of the continent...

In the third-year base camp...

"Are you both sure of your claims, Arwen and Edward??" Morgan looked at both of them sitting in front of him; a few minutes earlier, both of them had entered his study and had started claiming things that left his brows raised.

"Yes, Professor, I am absolutely sure about this; William is a mole, a spy planted by the demon lords themselves." Arwen reiterated his statement again.

Morgan looked at both of them before taking a deep breath; he took off his goggles and rested them on the table before leaning back on his chair.

It was getting hard for him to believe what these two were saying, but the exactly same claim coming from two students was at least a matter worth looking into.

He looked at the icicle filled with demonic energy and the demonic brain seed that these two had kept in front of him in the name of proof. Then his gaze looked up at the two of them.

"I still can’t find your claims solid," Morgan said. "If I even want to touch William, I need very solid proof about his truth; he has no normal backing, and if you two really think your allegations have weight, then bring me solid proof."

Edward looked puzzled at Arwen; meanwhile, Arwen turned to him and just nodded, giving him a reassurance-filled smile. Edward raised his brows; he did not understand what Arwen meant.

The past few weeks every time he had talked to Arwen, the guy had just murmured things out of nowhere that made no sense.

Edward at first found it strange, but then he just reasoned that Arwen must be getting similar dreams like he was.

William must be haunting him too.

Arwen looked at Morgan. "Professor, I take a vow under the name of heavens that I was targeted by a shapeshifter demon a few months back on the academy premises; the same demon gave me these things."

Morgan’s eyes widened; he looked at Arwen like he was a maniac, but then the vow he took did not get the heavens to react. It meant that what he claimed was true.

Arwen looked at Morgan seriously under the shocked gaze of Edwards. "Professor, I guess my statement is enough to start an investigation."

Morgan, still cautious, asked Arwen, "Why did you not take William’s name??"

Arwen shook his head. "Because I am not sure if that demon’s real identity is William Kaiser or if he has just taken the form of an innocent individual, the shapeshifting powers possessed by that demon are so deceiving, and I don’t want to risk using the wrong phrasing in my vow."

Arwen remained content in his mind. He had already established the fact in the professor’s mind while also keeping the demon at his side satisfied.

When the timing is right and William is out of his way, he would just expose Edward as the actual demon by manipulating the facts and playing with words.

This plan was something he had come up with in the time of a few months after arriving on the base camp.

Arwen was a bit proud about his wits; he thought he was playing ahead of both the demon and William.

Morgan looked at Arwen with renewed intensity and then spoke. "Principal Andrea and General Marcus won’t agree to start an investigation on William."

His words made Edward and Arwen frown; they realized that William was even backed by the principal and General Marcus.

Arwen was now more sure than ever about letting William die under the demon’s hands; otherwise, he won’t be able to claim Seraphine as his.

"Fortunately, there is someone who will be able to help us," Morgan said before taking out a paper and a quill. He started writing a letter.

"Wait for a few months till the next academy trials; if you come in contact with the demon again, contact me as soon as possible. Here, take these," Morgan said before handing them two scrolls.

"Tear them, and I will be alerted; use them only in life-threatening situations." Morgan spoke while moving the quill against the letter.

Before long, he dismissed both of them.

Since the academy was not running like usual, Morgan wasn’t worried about the demon making a mess; he trusted the colonel of the camp where the first years were posted.

He was sure no demon could ever escape the eyes of Draven. Till that person arrives at the academy, Morgan would wait patiently.

Arwen and Edward came out of his office, blank. They wondered on whom Professor Morgan was betting.

Chapter 231. Honey Trapping
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

No man’s land, north of the Skybreak Empire...

"The Emperor didn’t attack the way you informed us." In a hall as dark as night, two figures sat in front of each other.

The one being addressed remained static on his seat without reacting, wearing a loose dark robe that hid his features and silhouette completely. He remained silent when questioned by the man in front.

"Are you sure you still have a hold over your subjects?" The man who questioned was scrawny yet tall and lean in build, with slouched shoulders.

His face was sharp with a pointed jawline, colorless white skin, and intense, glowing red demonic eyes. A sinister smile rested on his lips, framed by thick, long hair tied in a ponytail, while he questioned the man in front.

The one being questioned raised his hands, indicating to the questioner to stop. "Are the preparations for Aurelius’s welcome finished?"

"What the hell do you think we have been doing for the past week while leaving all our operations aside??" the front man, who was now being questioned, replied in a grainy voice.

"Aurelius will attack for sure; he has just postponed the raid for a few days. Maybe he was stopped by his loyal dogs," the robed informant of the Clayman cult told the Clayman cult’s leader, Agrath, a vampire.

Agrath grimaced, looking at the robed figure. "You expect us to stay static like this for an impending attack that we are not even sure will arrive? What if Aurelius doesn’t attack at all?? and we keep taking financial losses like we are doing now."

"Sigh, are you dumb?? Didn’t I say he would be attacking soon?" the man said with a cold sigh.

Agrath sneered, "Before calling me dumb, you should question your own competence." Now I see the reason why Aurelius is sitting on the throne of Riverdale, and you are begging for help from your enemies."

"Isn’t that true, Crown Prince Daniel Dalton? "Oh, my bad. I forgot to add ’former,’" Agrath spoke with a mocking expression.

"Are you done?" Daniel didn’t get flustered or angry by the mocking words; his tone remained ice-cold, the same as previously.

Agrath didn’t stop. "I wish I had never gotten someone traitorous like you among my men," he said.

Suddenly, the doors to the chamber opened up. A beastkin female servant with cat-like features entered the hall to serve drinks and desserts.

Agrath and Daniel stopped their bickering in front of her. Agrath noticed the features of the servant, and his lustful gaze washed over her like a predator eyeing his prey.

"What is this?" Suddenly, Daniel’s voice sounded as he curiously looked at a sleek piece of dark brown bar kept along with the rest of the desserts. He had never seen something like this before, even in the royal palace.

Agrath’s gaze moved from the servant’s hips towards the thing kept on the plate in front of them; he looked at the catkin-female servant, expecting an explanation.

It was his first time looking at such a thing, too.

The female servant smiled and enthusiastically introduced the item. "My lord, this is a chocolate; it has an extremely intense nutty and creamy flavor with a hint of bitterness that complements its sweet taste. A crate of these was retrieved from the inventory of a young merchant traveling south. Our agent Budeen had sent a few crates of these to the headquarters to get them checked."

She held the tray close and looked into Agrath’s eyes with a gentle smile. "Recently, Junior Elder Grimlock ran a few tests and concluded that this item was a harmless dessert; he was amazed by the taste and instructed us to add this to the dessert inventory."

Agrath nodded; his lustful eyes were now focused on the servant’s ample chest.

"I have never seen you before!!" Agrath spoke as he checked her out again properly.

"My lord, I was brought in with the new batch of slaves." The cat spoke with a shy expression, and her tail wagged furiously as if she were nervous.

Agrath suddenly felt a wave of excitement in his chest, looking at the innocent and meek woman in front of him. He wanted to ravage this cat’s kin, and slowly he made a silent decision to call her to his chambers later to have fun.

"Hmm!!" Agrath nodded, finally looking away. "What’s your name?"

The female catkin blushed and bowed suddenly. "My name is Mika, my lord."

He got another flash of her cleavage as Mika bowed in front of him.

He nodded. "Mika, serve us the chocolate."

Mika nodded excitedly. "Yes, my lord."

She moved delicately and sliced the bar of chocolate with a knife.

Now, even Daniel noticed where the intense gaze of Agrath was focused on.

Mika moved lightly and came forward in front of Agrath. She came a bit closer than necessary and leaned in front of him. "My lord," she said, holding the plate in front of his face and looking into his eyes.

Agrath didn’t even look at the chocolate; his gaze was focused somewhere else. He didn’t even realize how many pieces he had eaten till now from her hands.

He loved being fed by her.

Thanks to the short maid uniform, Daniel was lost in another heavenly scene that appeared in front of him as Mika leaned towards Agrath. He slowly felt intoxicated by the sight in front of him; his mouth watered, and he didn’t even know it.

Mika moved after serving a few pieces to Agrath; she walked towards Daniel and leaned in a bit close; her seductive gaze was now looking at Daniel. Her tight cleavage was on full show.

Daniel clenched the armrest; he controlled his instincts. Similar to Agrath, he too was never able to realize how many pieces of chocolate he had just been fed by her hands.

After serving both men, Mika straightened and, holding the trays, she left the room with a seductive walk, drawing both Agrath’s and Daniel’s gaze. Her hips swayed, making both men, who were hardened by experiences, lose their focus.

Only when Mika left did the two come back out of their stupors and focus on the discussions ahead.

Meanwhile, Mika came out of the meeting hall, and her shoulders slouched while her posture relaxed. She sighed before finding a silent corner for herself.

Looking around, after Mika confirmed that there was no one looking at her, she changed forms; the seductive cat-kin lady transformed into a rough, manly silhouette of Grimlock.

"Tsk!! Dammit, I feel like I was touched by them," he gritted his teeth.

With another wave of disgust for the two men, he disappeared and appeared inside the domain of the infinite.

William waited for him inside the domain. He asked with a smile. "Is it done?" he asked.

"Yes, my lord," Grimlock said before kneeling, "both of them ate about ten bars of chocolate."

William nodded with satisfaction. "Told you the aphrodisiacs were really potent. Now go take a bath; otherwise, Amorphous might come after you if you carry that scent around him."

Grimlock nodded with a grim expression. William sensed the unease. "What happened?? You look worried?"

Grimlock said with a sigh, "My lord, please don’t make me do this honey trap thing again, I..." He hesitated and stuttered.

"You what??" William asked only to make Grimlock shiver.

"My lord," Grimlock slammed his head on the floor. "Please punish me severely, but don’t give me a task like this again!!" he said.

William was taken aback, looking at how smooth the whole thing went; he really wanted to use Grimlock in these types of tasks. It seems like he would need to send Barash and Ronnin for the task now.

William just nodded and dismissed Grimlock; he promised a reward for his sacrifices once the mission was over.

After Grimlock left, William sighed.

[hehe!!]

William looked into the empty air. "What’s so funny?"

[Nothing much; I just never thought turning horny would help you come up with such crazy ideas.]

William sneered; the system was too shameless.

He had just been thinking about how he had lost self-control with Seraphine earlier, and that thought had given him inspiration in cooking up a way to honeytrap both the Clayman cult’s leader and Daniel to eat his blood at the same time.

Luckily, Grimlock had shapeshifter and natural actor talents. A few hours of making him watch television taught him how to act like a seductive maid.

William sighed; he didn’t know what to say. He was a bit embarrassed about this, to be honest.

Chapter 232. Prove Me Wrong - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

2 days later...

William stood inside the domain of infinity; he had been diligent with his cultivation for the past months; now soon his hard work would be tested during the attack on the Clayman cult.

But it had been almost three months now since he had taken any cultivation and other training feedback from his bonds.

"Show me my status screen," William asked the system with a calm expression.

————⁜⁜⁜————

Name: William Kaiser

Race: Human (98%), True-Fire Dragon (2%)

Age: 15

Mana Rank: SS

Spiritual Rank: SS

Demonic Rank: S+

Path: Eternal Sovereign (Stage 1: Bending Mortality)

———— TALENTS ————

Lawless Adaptation (Supreme)

———— SHARED TALENTS ————

Ruler of Eternal Darkness (SSS) (Shared via Tamasya)

Shapeshifter (SS) (from Amorphous)

———— SPELLS ————

Blizzard, Glacial Bind, Ice Spear (platinum)

———— BONDS ————

Ethan

Maximus Sinclair

Leila Ravenclaw

Seraphine (Mark of Destiny)

Sunchaser / "Sunny" (Mark of Comrade/Spirit Contract)

Emily (Mark of Servant)

Slaves: 834

———— ITEMS ————

Armament Nexus (Soul Weapon):

Crimson Aeon Blade (Divine)

————⁜⁜⁜————

Current SP: 45,000

————⁜⁜⁜————

William looked at the SS ranks in mana and spiritual energy with a sigh. His gaze lingered on those letters a bit longer, as if expecting them to change just by staring harder.

He wondered something while looking at his bonds as he asked the system about it.

"Can’t I make Sera cultivate inside the domain of infinity?? And then take in all the cultivation as feedback? Won’t that be super efficient??" he asked.

Introducing others to the domain was out of the question; he only trusted Tamasya and Seraphine enough.

Even the thought of letting others step into this place felt like exposing his most guarded secret.

If you manage to place a mark of soulmate on both of them, then sure, it’s possible. For now, the answer is no.]

"Why?? What will the system do if I try to force this now?" There was a hint of impatience in his voice.

[Nothing much as a penalty, but the cultivation gains of yours will equalize to what you get normally.]

Even if you make Sera cultivate inside the domain of infinity right now, the cultivation feedback would just be reduced to what you get normally in a day, and the effect of time dilation would be canceled out, and unless you manage to place the mark of soulmate on her, that limitation will remain imposed.

William frowned slightly.

"Why is the mark of a soulmate so special? Won’t I get it when I marry her?" William asked.

The concept felt both simple and complicated at the same time.

[No, even if two individuals are married, that doesn’t make them soulmates; you need to fulfill special conditions or pass some trials together that test you as soulmates to get one.]

William pondered on those words for a moment but then looked at the description of the mark of a soulmate.

His eyes narrowed slightly as he focused.

————⁜⁜⁜————

Mark of Soulmate

Placement conditions: The mark of destiny evolves in case a woman is recognized as the true soulmate of the host.

————⁜⁜⁜————

The words "true soulmate," which William had been ignoring in the past, now stood out to him the most; he finally understood what the system wanted to say.

Those two words carried a depth far beyond mere relationships.

Being married wasn’t enough; their bond had to be recognized by the system for it to grant the mark of a soulmate.

"Well, I guess I will understand with time what it means to be a soulmate." William spoke with a slight smile.

There was no frustration in his tone, only quiet acceptance. He had learned to not rush and panic in the matters of love.

Especially after the calm Seraphine had shown and resolved the insecurities festering in his heart for months in a few moments.

That memory alone brought a faint warmth to his expression.

He realized that he had been needlessly getting tense about things that were not in his hands.

"Let’s move ahead; provide me the cultivation feedback of the past 3 months," William instructed the system.

His focus returned instantly. In an instant the system complied: "Understood, host."

Within a few moments, waves of mana and spiritual energy flowed into William’s cores.

The sensation was overwhelming.

It felt as if rivers of energy were being poured into him, filling every inch of his being. His cultivations elevated by a huge margin, though not enough to cross the SSS-ranked threshold.

In total, he had gotten a bit over 500 days of average cultivation in mana.

Even then it was not enough to break through; he knew that he had not even reached halfway there.

William grimaced; his mana capacity, though increased by a bit, was still at SS rank.

The frustration surfaced briefly.

"System, can you estimate how many days’ worth of cultivation I need to break through from my current number of binders??"

[Host, you still need about 2000 days worth to cross to the SSS rank with just your binds cultivating; that’s roughly 11 months. If you also keep cultivating yourself diligently, the time can be brought down to roughly over 9 months.]

William sighed.

The number felt heavy, yet not impossible.

"I need to bind more people; let’s hope to do that in the coming month, unless my free purchase is used for an emergency."

William then instructed the system for the training feedback that his binds were doing.

The system complied, and William was rewarded 100X gains for all the techniques and other forms of training.

The results of gravity training were generous, and William’s limit rose from the latest 80g to 100g in a matter of instant.

His body trembled slightly as it adapted to the sudden surge in physical capability.

William also noticed that Ethan had started training in the third form of the tempest sword technique.

William only now paid attention to Ethan’s pace. In the book, Ethan had started training the third form of the tempest sword much sooner.

A faint crease formed on his forehead.

William knew that Ethan was bound to get a huge burst of power after he got recognized by the storm element.

In the book, Ethan had awakened the element with the help of a storm stone gifted to him by the storm dragon after he got first place in the world academy trials.

But now since William had intervened, Ethan had gotten a mythic weapon instead, which was good on its own but not compared to an affinity.

"Hopefully he strolls around the storm dragon’s domain and awakens the storm; otherwise it will be too difficult for him ahead," William spoke before leaving the domain.

William appeared in the outside world; he appeared inside the washroom.

This was the only place where even the strongest wouldn’t pry their eyes open and also the place from where William preferred to disappear into his domain.

The quiet isolation of the space gave him a sense of secrecy.

William came out and walked into his barracks; inside, an officer was waiting for him.

The officer turned to look at him. William saluted the senior.

The man nodded `and said, "Colonel Draven has summoned you to his office."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 233. Prove Me Wrong - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

William looked at Colonel Draven’s figure, who was sitting on his chair and looking at a letter in front of him.

The room carried a suffocating stillness, broken only by the faint rustling of parchment as Draven’s fingers occasionally adjusted their grip on the letter.

Sunlight filtered through the tall windows behind him, casting long, slanted shadows across the wooden floor.

A few minutes had passed in silence while William sat in front of Draven and looked at him with a neutral expression. The ticking of a distant wall clock sounded loudly, contrasting with the silent atmosphere.

Draven looked between the letter and William’s face; his sharp eyes were trying to connect the words written before him with the boy sitting calmly across the desk.

After a few rounds of introspection, Draven finally spoke. "Recently some allegations against you have been raised." Draven spoke with a steady voice, "Allegations of you being a demon spy."

He said while searching for even a speck of hesitation in William’s eyes. His gaze sharpened as he intended to pick apart the smallest flicker of deceit or fear.

All he saw was a sub-zero expression unreactive to the statement, as if the words had no power over William at all.

"Are you not scared?" Draven asked, leaning forward slightly.

William looked at Draven with an unwavering gaze. "There is no reason to be scared for me, Sir Draven. I am sure as long as everyone sticks to facts, I will be able to prove that the person throwing such allegations was wrong with quite ease."

His voice carried self-confidence and a firm belief in his own position. There was no rush in his tone, nor was he being overly defensive.

Draven looked at William’s confidence. To be honest, he was quite taken aback when he had received a letter from Professor Morgan, his old friend at the academy, warning him about William Kaiser being a demon.

He found it hard to believe, but then again, as the colonel of the celestial army, he ought to confirm with his own eyes. Duty stood above personal trust, no matter how long-standing the friendship.

Now that he had summoned William in front of him, he could not find any hesitation or fear in his eyes, yet Draven had no solid proof of William’s innocence. That was what troubled him the most, the absence of both guilt and evidence.

In his heart he wanted William to be guilty; after all, he had never seen a genius like William ever in his life.

The weight of responsibility pressed down on him, reminding him that a single wrong judgment could ruin a life or allow a hidden threat to pass unchecked. He had to keep his personal biases aside in such a matter.

"This is a really sensitive matter, William; if word gets out, then it will really impact your reputation, even if later you are proved to be not guilty."

His voice softened slightly, no longer just that of a colonel but of someone aware of the consequences of such an accusation.

"That is why I would like you to prove this letter wrong." Draven looked at William, his gaze firm. "I would like you to prove me wrong right now so I can file a case and then close it for good with proof enough to not rattle you again about such things. You will get immunity from similar allegations in the future since you would have proved yourself once," Draven said.

William, meanwhile, was smiling from inside, though none of it reached his face.

Edward and Arwen had walked right into his trap. Their impatience and hostility had led them exactly where he wanted. With a nod, William raised his hand slowly.

He conjured a flicker of light elemental particles in his hands.

The air around his palm shimmered faintly before a soft glow emerged; it was pure, radiant, and gentle.

The light danced like tiny stars gathered into his grasp.

Draven looked at William with a shocked expression and stood up from his seat. His chair scraped harshly against the floor, breaking the stillness.

He walked forward closer to confirm he was seeing things right.

It was the light element; William Kaiser was wielding the light element.

On his official records, William was reported to be a dual affinity user of ice and darkness, making him rare even among geniuses across the continent. That alone had already placed him in an elite category of dual elemental users.

Now he was seeing another element being handled by the same student.

Were the heavens not fair enough anymore? Were they favoring William??

Draven’s heartbeat fastened, thudding loudly in his chest as disbelief and astonishment clashed within him. His trained composure wavered for a brief moment. He could not understand what he should do now.

"William, you have kept the ability to use the light element a secret from everyone, right??" he asked William, his voice slightly strained.

"No, sir, I was admitted to the academy through special recommendations; my affinities and talents were never officially recorded," William spoke.

Draven sweated, a thin layer of moisture forming on his forehead. So that meant that what he had seen was just the data collected by observation rather than proper data recorded by artifacts. The realization made things both clearer and more complicated at the same time.

Colonel Draven spoke, his tone regaining some of its firmness. "William, even though your proof is solid enough to never let someone suspect you for a lifetime, I still believe that you should keep your Light element a secret; consider it advice from this old man."

A triple elemental user was rarely seen across ages; if the world knew about this, then Draven could not even imagine the amount of shit William might have to go through.

He wanted William to not get affected by such titles so soon in life, as they affected one more negatively than in a positive manner.

Being a rare genius meant better resources, protection, and a future. While being an anomalous, never-before-seen genius was just a curse in his opinion. These people became the center of attention, and that also included attention from the enemies.

The way he spoke to William, he could tell that there was sincerity in his words now.
