Draven then sighed, "Forget about filing a case for immunity; I will just find another way. I don’t want to push you to a heavenly oath now that you have proved me wrong."

Light was one of the few elements that the demon race was unable to wield; it was said to have purification properties and be a bane for demons. The very presence of such energy symbolized purity and alignment to natural order.

The demonic energy weakened in contact with light energy, and thus there was no demon in existence ever born able to wield the light element.

It was an element fundamentally incompatible with demons.

The same was the case with other light-derived elements, like the crystal element wielded by Principal Andrea and the super rare life element wielded by the Sylvaris empire’s ancestor. These were powers associated with creation, purity, and balance.

Such elements could never be wielded by the demons since they were too corrupted and impure for them.

William nodded, accepting the reasoning without resistance. "I am fine with that, sir, but I would like to file a court-martial against the person who accuses me of being a demon," he said.

There was a subtle sharpness beneath his calm tone now, a quiet intent that hinted he had no intention of letting the matter pass so easily.

Draven looked at him and shook his head, his expression turning firm once more. "That might not be possible, since the sender did not ask me to specifically file a case against you but just intended to warn me; then that means he is not officially advocating to file a case against you," Draven said with a hard expression.

"You can just relax, and in the future if someone files such a case, I will personally assist you to court-martial them," Draven said with a firm promise sealed in his words.

William sighed, a controlled release of breath, as if letting go of something trivial. "Alright, sir, as you say," he spoke.

Draven nodded and excused William. As the door closed behind him, the colonel leaned back and sighed in relief; the weight on his shoulders lifted.

He thanked himself for not revealing Morgan’s name; otherwise, he would have been in unnecessary trouble if William had chosen to file a case. The consequences of such a revelation now seemed far more dangerous in hindsight.

He took out a blank page and started writing a reply to Morgan about William’s innocence. Naturally he could not reveal that William had shown ability to wield light elements since that was better kept a secret. Drawen just termed it his personal investigation, which concluded with proving William was not a demon.

Meanwhile, William came out of his office with a smirk on his face. He knew that both Arwen and Edward weren’t going to give up so soon.

He knew that the more these two would try to mess with him, the more severely things would bite them back, much harder than before.

He was looking forward to the mess both of them were going to get themselves into.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 234. Andrea’s Visit
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Tamasya’s domain...

Far above the normal lands of Aris, a silver carriage towed by flying unicorns arrived in the sky of Tamasya’s domain.

The hooves of the 2 unicorns moved in the air as if pushing back the air to fly forward. Their manes flowed like flags, and their wings flapped slowly, making them cruise in the sky.

A driver with a mask and metal wings on its back drove the shiny magical carriage as it flew around and found its way among the clouds. It left behind a soft trail of shimmering particles as it advanced.

Tamasya walked outside from the front door as she sensed the arrival with shadow manifestations wearing servant uniforms lined up behind her.

As the carriage came to a halt, the faceless, metal-winged man came to the front, boarding off the driver’s seat, and walked to the small gate of the carriage before opening it. His movements were refined like a butler.

Tamasya’s expression cheered as she saw Andrea unboarding the carriage. A warmth spread across her usually composed face; her eyes lightened up with genuine relief.

She calmly took two steps forward and welcomed Andrea, who walked towards her and gave her a tight hug. The embrace was firm, filled with unspoken concern, relief, and a bond that had endured far more than ordinary friendships.

"You can’t imagine the relief I am feeling to see you safe and sound," Tamasya said with an emotional intensity. Her voice carried a slight tremor, betraying how much she had worried. She and Yue had insisted on getting involved in the fight against Bellial, but Andrea had denied it.

Tamasya still had divine seals sealing her cultivation and powers, making her vulnerable; moreover, only very few people on the continent were informed about her return. The restrictions weighed heavily on her and made her more of a liability than an asset for Andrea.

Meanwhile, Yue’s powers were not even combat-oriented, so she became more or less useless in a fight.

These hard truths were something Andrea had to speak up about to their faces to stop them from being emotional and impractical in these hard times. Though harsh, those words had come from a place of care.

Andrea rubbed Tamasya’s back while hugging. "As you see, I am alright," she said with a chuckle, trying to lighten the atmosphere.

"Let’s go inside; I have a lot of things to talk about," Tamasya said, stepping back slightly but still holding Andrea’s arm as if reluctant to let go too soon.

Andrea shook her head, her expression turning a bit serious. "No, I have a few things to take care of, so I will have to take my leave."

"What?? But you just got over a huge battle; I doubt you have to hurry right now. At least take a breather," Tamasya said with a concerned expression, her brows knitting together.

Andrea just shook her head. "Well, you see, there is a boatload of mess about to happen in Riverdale."

Tamasya looked at Andrea, surprised. "You mean the thing that Yue told us about Aurelius throwing a fit??"

Andrea, in reply, just sighed, her shoulders dropping slightly. "Yeah, he is going to attack the Clayman cult headquarters."

Tamasya looked a bit surprised. "Really??"

"Yeah, I have to assist him with that," Andrea spoke.

Tamasya shook her head. "Why? You don’t owe him anything. You just left your position as an astral warden, so you are not obliged to take orders from the council anymore. Just ignore them."

Andrea shook her head. "It wasn’t the council that ordered me; since Aurelius has kept this matter a top secret, there is no involvement from the council."

Tamasya’s brows furrowed. "Are you doing this for Yue??" she asked, confused as to why Andrea was getting involved in these matters.

Andrea shook her head. "No, Tammy, Yue would never bother us for such matters; instead, it is part of a deal I made," she said with a sigh.

"A deal??" Tamasya asked, looking at Andrea with curiosity, her eyes narrowing slightly.

Andrea nodded. "During the fight against Bellial, we were provided assistance by the Eternal Sovereign; otherwise, due to a shortage of light stones, the Academy would have been reduced to ruins," she spoke in a grim tone.

Tamasya’s eyes widened in shock. "What really???" she gasped as she looked at the disappointed expression on Andrea’s face that refused to lie.

She sighed, the weight of the revelation settling in. "Yes, Tamasya, the demons have become a hundred times harder to kill than before. If not for the Eternal Sovereign providing us with light stone and a very powerful formation, then we would have been left in shambles."

Andrea then looked at her with emotions that she had been suppressing trying to rise up again. "You were right about the council; it’s a good thing that you never joined them." Andrea said, "The primarchs don’t care about us at all; they seem to be playing their own game."

Tamasya sighed. "I’ve always said it, Andrea; those who accept shackles imposed by others are already dead inside."

Her gaze drifted briefly to the horizon, as if recalling countless decisions and paths she had chosen not to take.

"Those primarchs may look tough to us; not going to lie, they are really strong, but they are still not anywhere equal to gods, the beings who can wipe us with a single wave of their hands, those who live beyond the heavens."

Andrea nodded silently with a bit of weight on her. "I have decided to find my own way, just like you, Tammy," she said with a small, subtle smile, one that showed both her determination and the uncertainty.

Tamasya rested a hand on her shoulders and pressed them firmly. "I know you will be able to."

"We will start by saving Annie," Andrea said, as she clenched her fist with determination.

"We sure will do," Tamasya replied with hope-filled eyes; a rare softness appeared in her expression.

A brief silence filled between them, not awkward but heavy enough.

"So what was the deal??" Tamasya asked, changing the topic.

Andrea looked at the ground cluelessly for a few moments before replying, "Basically I need to assist Eternal Sovereign’s organization 3 times whenever he asks." Well, there are some conditions that protect my interests, but yeah, that’s pretty much the crux of it. He asked me to assist Aurelius as the 1st wish."

Tamasya nodded. "Well, as long as the deal doesn’t turn toxic," she spoke lightly, though a faint hint of concern lingered beneath her tone.

Andrea then also described her encounter with the Golden Raven and the powerful array with which she came so close to killing Belial, yet remained helpless since she wasn’t informed about how to kill demon lords.

Her shoulders slouched and her expression became grim as she recalled her helplessness. The memory seemed to replay vividly in her mind; at that time, the moment of near victory had slipped away in a few seconds.

Tamasya consoled her, placing a reassuring hand on her shoulder.

Soon Andrea looked at her pocket watch and spoke, "It’s time; I need to leave."

Tamasya pouted, her lips curving slightly downward. "You should have stayed."

Andrea chuckled. "Work calls," she said before walking towards the carriage and entering it. The metallic figure once again took position, and the carriage hummed faintly with magical energy.

As she was about to leave, as if remembering something, Andrea opened the door again and peeked her head out, looking at Tamasya.

"Oh shit!! I completely forgot about it!!" Andrea spoke, her eyes widening slightly.

Tamasya, who was about to leave, turned towards Andrea; she wondered why Andrea was smiling suddenly.

"I have a special present for you, hehe!!" she chuckled; a mischievous glint flashed in her eyes.

Before Tamasya could ask, Andrea closed the door of her carriage and waved goodbye.

"See you soon, Tammy. Enjoy the gift; don’t forget to thank me later!!" Andrea said with a cheerful smile and a wink before the carriage floated on the ground and took off for the sky.

The unicorns leapt forward, their wings spreading wide as they ascended gracefully, disappearing into the clouds.

Tamasya looked at the carriage phasing away in the sky, wondering what surprise Andrea was talking about.

"Did you miss me?"

Suddenly a voice too familiar to her ears entered her senses. The tone carried a teasing warmth, one she could recognize instantly.

Tamasya immediately looked down towards the spot where the carriage had been standing moments ago.

William’s figure came to view.

He was carrying a charming and gentle smile and a bouquet of dark red carnations.

He was dressed in the same black leather armored dress that he had worn the first time he had met Tamasya in the prison of gods.

"Wish me luck," he spoke in his mind.

[Go win her over, host!!!]

The air between both of them was charged with anticipation.

William looked into Tamasya’s shocked eyes; with a smile, he walked forward.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 235. Fresh Start - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Did you miss me??" William asked for the second time as he came forward and closed the distance between himself and Tamasya. His voice carried a soft and familiar teasing tone, but beneath it lingered a subtle nervousness that even he could not fully suppress.

Tamasya came out of her stupor and took a breath; her eyes, which had been fixed on William, shifted towards the ground; she did not meet his eyes.

His sudden appearance had caught her off guard, leaving her momentarily defenseless in a way she rarely ever was.

One of her hands raised to the side, and she put a few locks of hair that remained in front of her eyes behind her ears. The small, almost habitual action betrayed the storm of emotions she was trying to contain within herself.

She chose not to reply to William again.

But today, William was not here to get flustered. He extended his hands and presented her with the bouquet of red carnations. Back on earth, these flowers were considered the symbol of deep love. Their deep crimson petals seemed almost alive under the light, carrying an intensity that matched the feelings he held for her.

"These are for you," he said. He did not use the designatory word "master" in his sentences anymore; today, he was here to cross that line. The absence of that single word changed the entire tone of his sentences.

Tamasya took the bouquet from his hands; she then turned around, making William face her back while she blushed, her heart beating fast, filled with embarrassment and awkwardness. Her fingers tightened slightly around the stems.

Her lips were tightly pressed together; she was trying hard not to reveal her emotional fluctuations on her face. The effort was visible in the subtle tension of her jaw and the stillness of her posture.

But as she turned around, her eyes bloomed shiny and cheerful, even though her stiff face didn’t move. She was happy.

She walked towards the entrance holding the big bouquet. The flowers contrasted beautifully against her dark attire, making the moment feel almost surreal.

"Come inside," she said before William followed her with a smile. He noticed the faint reddish hue of blush on her ears and knew he had been able to make her happy with those flowers and his presence.

William felt a sense of relief. With this, he knew that in her heart, Tamasya was not holding any bitterness for him. This meant he could do what he had come here for.

William also noticed the outfit change; Tamasya used to wear a simple, loose black robe, or a combat-ready outfit that William had seen her wearing in the prison of gods.

But right now, he saw the change of dress, which just amplified her feminine features and made her look undescribably stunning, which, in his opinion, made her look leagues above how he had always imagined her in his mind.

The dress clung gracefully to her form, decorated with faint silver accents that shimmered whenever light touched them. It was dangerously captivating.

He wondered where the idea for the outfit change came from.

He didn’t mind the change at all; in fact, he thanked the person responsible because William was certainly several times more excited to propose to Tamasya in such beautiful clothes.

His cheeks blushed as he saw her exposed back and slim waist from behind. Her hourglass figure was much more pronounced, unlike the earlier loose robes of hers. Every step of her elegant walk just crushed William’s heart with excitement and nervousness as well.

"Stop gawking!" Tamasya’s stern voice rang, making William shocked and embarrassed. He wondered if Tamasya had eyes at the back of her head.

Meanwhile, Tamasya giggled in her mind; Shadow was reading every microexpression on William’s face before telling Tamasya about it.

<"He seems flustered now that you caught him">

Tamasya found it hard to control her laughter, but she still sustained it, biting down on the urge to let it spill out completely. Meanwhile, William now just rubbed the back of his head and looked to the side, trying to regain whatever composure he had left.

They were now walking inside a dark corridor only lit by sunlight coming from a distant window, with the silver accents and white crystals on her pitch-black dress, while Tamasya was leading him to her room.

"Sorry, I was just wondering about your new dress," William said with a sigh.

"Why??" With a sudden jerk, Tamasya turned around with a stern expression, leaving William off guard. "Do you think I can’t wear new dresses?" she asked with an elevated tone that just made her look cuter in William’s opinion.

"No!! That’s not what I meant; you can wear as many dresses as you want." William raised his hands lightly and took a defensive posture as if a hungry lioness had pounced on him.

"So!! Do I not look good enough...?" Tamasya squinted her eyes and leaned forward a bit while resting her hands on her waist.

"No!! No, absolutely not!! You look...." William was about to say something when he suddenly gulped, checking out Tamasya from head to toe for the nth time.

He didn’t know if words would be enough to appreciate her beauty.

"I look... what??" Tamasya didn’t let the matter rest. By now, even William realized that she was messing with him.

He felt like she was testing him, whether he was really suited to handle someone like her. He couldn’t explain it, but his instincts just told him everything Tamasya wanted.

*Surprise me, take me off guard, and impress me if you dare*.

"You look..." William said, but gave a breathy pause in between the words while his eyes looked at Tamasya, telling her silently that he had accepted her challenge.

He walked closer to her and stood at a breath’s distance away from her; he looked down at Tamasya’s eyes, who reached the level of his shoulders; his voice turned breathy.

"You look... so beautiful that my words betrayed me the moment I tried to speak. With whatever fragile pieces of life left inside of me were stolen by my own eyes that were condemned to fall for you again and again, and still unable to explain to the rest of me what they’ve just witnessed."

He exhaled softly in surrender.

"I feel like if I dare confine your grace within words, I’d be punished by the heavens for it... as if something this breathtaking was never meant to be reduced to words. How could I ever be arrogant enough to try?"

His gaze lingered on her as he breathed a sigh and shook his head lightly as if he had been defeated by her beauty.

"It would’ve been easier... if you were just pretty."

A faint, helpless smile formed on his lips.

"But you had to be... you."

"Do you have any idea what you did to my heart, it’s beating like it’s being suffocated by something only you can fill..."

"That’s what you did."

The air between them stilled like something sacred was happening.

"And when you asked me if you weren’t beautiful enough..."

His voice lowered as his gaze shifted from her eyes to her lips.

"...you accused me of a crime."

"The crime of not closing this distance... of not kissing you and proving just how wrong you are to ever doubt yourself."

William said with an expression that could kill maidens from a heart attack; his eyes refused to waver while looking at her.

[Kiss her!!!!] The system said, but was ignored.

Tamasya, meanwhile, looked up at him, her eyes opened wide, as well as her mouth. She had been masking her blush and emotions that had been threatening to break the dam in her heart.

William, with his words, had been able to break those shackles, and her face was now as red as the flowers in her hand.

She looked lost in her stupor as if not being able to face the reality that William had just said what he had said. William looked at her with a satisfied expression. At last, he had been able to draw out the warmth he had been trying to.

He had meant everything he had said earlier, although he won’t let things just die down here; he will make Tamasya fall in love again. And that wouldn’t be achieved by just a few words.

He then broke the silence between them with a chuckle: "Just joking, you just looked cute."

Tamasya’s expression changed once again and turned into a pissed one. "You scoundrel!!, do you think you can mess with me!!!" She was breathing heavily before, while feeling surreal, but now she was pissed at William for making her lose the silent battle that she had started.

She hit him lightly while William held her hands, a cute physical and verbal banter started between them, William broke her expression again, this time tickling her neck.

A few spurts of giggles came out of her mouth like a melody she had forgotten she could make.

To William, they meant relief of heart.

One thing the now giggling figure of Tamasya did not realise among those tickles and light curses was that William had slowly dissolved the freezing awkwardness between them, and they were back to bickering the way they used to before.

The invisible wall between them had crumbled.

It was perfect for William; he will be able to make Tamasya fall in love with him again now.

Even if she loved him already, he still wanted to make her fall over him again. He did not want his love to be associated with the painful memories of him breaking her heart earlier.

This time, he wanted laughter to replace silence, warmth to replace distance, and choice to replace regret.

It was his attempt at a fresh, brand-new start.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 236. Fresh Start - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Tamasya set the flowers in a vase by the side of her bed. The deep red carnations spread a faint, sweet fragrance through the room. She adjusted them carefully, trying to delay the inevitable need to turn around.

William leaned against a wall near the entrance, smiling as he crossed his hands and looked at her figure. His posture was relaxed, but his gaze was anything but casual; it looked at her, trying to etch her image in his mind forever.

He had just been like this for quite a few minutes now.

<"He just keeps looking at you,"> Shadow spoke in Tamasya’s mind while she set the flowers.

Tamasya’s heart beat wildly.

"What do I do now? What do I say?? He’s been so silent. He just came and made me flustered by his poetic words, and now his gaze is all over me. What happened to his lover?? Did she leave him?? Or did he leave her for me?"

<"Why don’t you ask him??">

Tamasya bit her lip; that was not something she wanted to ask so soon. The mere thought of voicing those questions made her chest tighten.

Finally, she straightened as the flowers were set. She turned and looked at William with a mild smile, one she carefully crafted to hide the storm within.

William came out of his stupor as his eyes met hers. "Let’s go to the kitchen," he said with a wave of his hands.

"But..." Tamasya was about to say she was not hungry.

"I am hungry," William said, removing the need for her to add anything else. He straightened on his feet and let Tamasya walk beside him as they both walked towards the kitchen.

The silence between them was no longer awkward, but it wasn’t comfortable either. It carried tension, anticipation, and something unspoken that refused to settle.

"How has the breaking of seals been going on?" he asked.

Tamasya looked at William, surprised. How did you know??

"Principal Andrea told me," he said.

Tamasya remained shocked; a bad premonition rose in her heart. Did Yue tell Andrea about her feelings for William? Her thoughts began racing again. The memory of Andrea’s playful wink, her teasing words before leaving, all came crashing back.

As she overthought this, William looked at her, noticing how she had ignored his question again.

He just shook his head and figured he would make her speak eventually.

The kitchen came into sight.

It was spacious yet cozy, the marble counter reflected light softly, and the silence here felt more intimate than anywhere else.

"Let me cook something with the chocolates," William said as he opened the drawer where he had earlier stocked entire crates of chocolate for her.

Opening her drawer, all he saw was emptiness.

He looked at Tamasya with a surprised expression, who had now come out of her thoughts with the sound of the drawer opening.

"You could have just told me to send you more chocolates," William asked her, turning his head to the side.

"I..." Tamasya didn’t have anything to add to this; she had been too prideful to ask for newer chocolates. Her fingers curled slightly, betraying her discomfort.

William sighed. "You really like chocolates that much??"

Tamasya bit her lips before she said in a very soft tone, "They help with the pain of breaking seals, also other types of pain..." William immediately understood what she was talking about.

"How many days ago did the chocolates finish off?" he asked, stepping close.

Tamasya looked at the drawer. "A few months."

William frowned. "So you were facing the pain of breaking seals for the past months without any chocolates??" Tamasya didn’t reply and avoided matching his gaze.

William moved his hands and ran them lightly through her hair, making her shiver. The touch was gentle and almost apologetic.

"I am sorry," he said with a gulp; a pang of guilt arose in his mind.

"Well, it will be alright now; I will help you break those seals," he said while the back of his hands caressed her temples.

"Let me cook something good for you!! made of chocolates," he added with a light smile.

Tamasya felt his touch and couldn’t understand the reason behind his boldness; she wanted to know what made him change so badly. What happened to the careful William she knew earlier?

She liked everything that was happening, yet she felt she had a lot of layers to peel to truly reach his heart.

"What am I even thinking? We are not lovers that I would be curious about him!!" she told herself suddenly.

<"What’s wrong with you!!, even I wanted to mate with him when he said those lines about your beauty">

"He was not serious!!" Tamasya told Shadow, embarrassed, about her shamelessness.

Suddenly, Tamasya felt herself being lifted from the waist.

"What!!" She got flustered as she looked at William picking her up before making her sit on the kitchen slab by his side as he returned to whisking the cake batter he was preparing.

The motion had been so natural, so effortless, as if he had every right to do it.

"Why do you seem so lost?" he asked as if he had not just touched her so intimately.

<"Yeah, I doubt he wasn’t serious.">

"I have a lot of things to tell you," William suddenly said as he saw himself getting ignored for the fourth time, "but I want you to stop ignoring me like this," he said with a pout that he had learned to make from Tamasya herself.

"I am not ignoring you!!" Tamasya spoke as if she were being wrongly accused. "What do you expect me to say about all those cheesy lines?! You just threw at me, who taught you this stuff??"

William kept focused on his cake, ignoring Tamasya this time, making her seethe and restless.

"Why are you ignoring me now!!" Tamasya leaned forward while sitting on the slab beside him, demanding answers from him.

William sighed and took a small amount of melted chocolate in his fingers before smearing it on the tip of her nose lightly.

Tamasya gasped, "What are you doing!!" She tried to wipe the chocolate off her nose, but William held her hands and stopped her from doing so.

He chuckled and came forward. "I think my words about your beauty were sincere. I take back what I said about me joking; I was not, actually."

Tamasya forgot she had something on her nose for a moment.

"What are you saying?? Are you out of your mind!!! Do you have a fever or something?" Tamasya asked with a gasp and checked his temperature.

William looked at her seriously for a moment. "You know!! I came here to achieve something!! Something that I thought would demand patience from my side, I was ready to wait for it, grind for it, and eventually cross the line that’s been keeping us apart."

His voice lowered, gaining weight with every word.

"But your silence makes me more impatient; every second feels so slow, as if I will be too late if I hesitate right now," William spoke while Tamasya remained frozen.

She could not pretend not to confront this anymore. Her thoughts blurred, her heartbeat was deafening.

Before she could think about anything, she found William’s face so close to her that she forgot herself being awake.

William moved his hands and cupped her face before he lightly moved it forward and planted a peck on the small amount of chocolate smeared on the bridge of her nose, wiping it off the tip of his tongue.

Tamasya’s face was now burning; her mind had gone completely blank.

"I am here to make you mine, forever. I am here to make you fall in love with me again."

William did not hold himself back anymore; he sealed Tamasya’s parted lips with a deep kiss. Filling her with the taste of chocolate.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 237. Fresh start - 3
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Once William and Tamasya started kissing, they didn’t stop; they couldn’t. Tamasya lost control over the emotions she had been holding.

William had fanned a flame that even he could not control now. Only now did William realize that Tamasya was not just in love; she was hungry, hungry for it, and he liked every part of it but still wanted to know what made her like this.

He didn’t know how many hours had passed now. Tamasya was breathing heavily in his arms; her lips shivered now. She looked at William with moist eyes. William was a bit surprised.

"Oh!! Why are you crying??" William asked softly and leaned his head a bit sideways, unable to understand. Tamasya’s face was red; her eyes were puffy as tears left her eyes.

Tamasya didn’t reply; she just buried her face in his chest as if a big relief had washed over her. She cried; her sobs were loud. William felt the air flushed by the smell of Tamsya’s tears; he did not ask anything else.

A few minutes passed.

Tamasya’s sobs had now died down; William’s clothes were soaked and warm. He had been caressing the back of her head. Tamasya refused to come out of the embrace now.

Her hands clenched his back, as if she let go now, the dream would end forever.

William gulped. The invincible image of Tamasya that she had portrayed in front of him was crumbling down. William was finally looking at her more humanly than ever. failed to understand where this pent-up stress and frustration was boiling over into relief.

He loved the fact that Tamasya was feeling relieved, and the tears were an indicator of that, but he also realized how little each of them actually knew about each other.

All this time, they had known each other, but they had never even shared their origins with each other. It was time to change that, and he would take the first step today.

He gently caressed Tamasya’s hair and picked her from the slab in a princess carry.

Tamasya, surprised by the sudden shift, raised her head that was buried in Williams’ chest.

William noticed the scattered mascara on her face, which made her look even more angelic than ever. He smiled. "Let’s go somewhere," he said before taking Tamasya with him inside the domain of Infinity.

Both of them appeared in the vast grassy expanse of the infinite domain. William looked at Tamasya in his arms with a smile as he looked at her, looking around curiously.

"Is this your domain??" Tamasya asked curiously.

"It was," William said, smiling. "Now it’s ours."

He then laid her on the ground. Tamasya came forward a bit and looked around the whole expanse; she could not tell where it ended or where it even started.

Before she could say anything, she noticed William hugging her from behind.

"I want to have a conversation about us," William said before planting a kiss on her cheeks from behind.

Tamasya turned around and looked at him with a smile.

A few minutes later, Tamasya and William sat near a river. The serene flow of water was calming to hear for both of them.

William broke the silence between them. "I realized this late and in awkward ways that I never expected, but the day you stopped looking into my eyes, your silence felt like a punishment for my heart, and your absence was jarring every second," William said as he rubbed her arms.

Tamasya had a complex expression on her face now. "What about the other girl you loved?"

William turned his head to look at her. "I still love her, and just like you, I can’t live without her."

Tamasya’s expression turned even more worried now, "So she is still your Woman??"

"Yes," William replied firmly.

"Then why??" Tamasya looked at William as if she had been wronged.

"Because her being my wife doesn’t mean that you can’t be my woman too, I want you both to be my wives," William said with certain confidence.

He was nervous in his heart; he had no idea how Tamasya would take it.

"What are you talking about?? Are you out of your mind? Does that girl even know that you are here, kissing your master all over??" Tamasya looked shocked at William’s straightforwardness.

"Yes, she knows that I love her, and she also knows how much I love you, Tamasya," William said firmly. He came forward. "That is why I want you to be my life partner," he said before holding her hands.

Tamasaya looked forward with a heavy expression now. She wanted to marry William, but the thought of another woman with him was a bit difficult for her.

"Can’t you make a choice between us??" Tamasya asked

"There is no choice; life without even one of you will be a mess for me," William said before he stood up.

"I am not a very great man, Tamasya. The only thing I know is that I will always protect you, care for you, and have you all for myself," he said, leaning forward.

"I have never said this before, but even if you reject me today, I won’t be giving up on us," William said while he searched her eyes.

"I didn’t think I was a very selfish man, but you proved me wrong. After realizing my love for you, I just know that I would never let any other man even come close to you. I want to have you all for myself, and I will make sure you are mine even if I have to go against fate itself."

William cupped Tamasya’s face. "So please be mine; otherwise, I will be forced to challenge fate," he said before planting a kiss on her lips.

Tamasya blushed, her eyes widened at William’s words; to be honest, she was also just holding herself right now; she wanted to leap into his arms and seal the promise of love.

A few seconds passed; William gave her space to think. He just lay beside her on the grassy ground to relax, waiting patiently for Tamasya to make a decision.

Meanwhile, in Tamsya’s mind, a storm unfolded.

<"You must take the position of first wife no matter what. I advise you to start the mating ritual immediately; take off your clothes and seduce him!!" Shadow spoke in a hurried voice.">

"Fuck, what the hell are you talking about, Shadow!!" Tamasya exclaimed.

<"I want to help you gain an upper hand in the power dynamics; someone like your mate won’t stop at only 2 women. You should claim the position of leader in the harem before the other elf.">

"Why are you assuming that I have already said yes to him??"

<"I mean, if you reject him at this point, it’s stupid, Tamasya. You are making scenarios about sharing your mate with other women, but I am sure the way your mate looks at you, he will never make you feel that you are competing for his attention and love.">

Tamasya understood what she was talking about; whenever Tamasya looked at William, all the complaints and grievances left her mind immediately, and the only emotion that was left was a deep and immense longing to be forever with him.

to wake up by his side daily and shower him with kisses, protect him from other vixens, and...

<"have some children with him">

"Huh??"

<"More children are equal to more power in the harem">

"Shadow, just shut up for a moment!!" Tamasya exclaimed before taking a sigh.

Her eyes focused, and she realized she had been sitting under the sound of a flowing river, completely lost in her thoughts.

She looked to the side only to see William, who had fallen asleep with a relaxed expression on his face. A subtle smile etched his face as he already knew that she belonged to him.

Tamasya smiled and came close to him; she waved her hands in front of his closed eyes to check whether he was really asleep. After shaking him lightly, she came to the conclusion that William had really drifted to sleep.

Tamasya took a deep breath before she lay by his side, turning over towards him, looking at his face.

Within a few minutes, even Tamsya felt a bit sleepy and eventually drifted to sleep, not before hugging him as her hands rested on his chest while her head rested in his arms.

Both of them lay there peacefully; a few butterflies fluttered around them as if they were flowers.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 238. Oracle of Pandemonium
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Tamasya’s eyes opened up; she found herself back in her room. For a brief second, her mind floated between reality and the fading sleepiness, making her vision a bit hazy.

Sitting up, she looked around only to see William sitting on the couch in front of her. The room was quiet, eerily so, save for the faint rustle of fabric as she shifted slightly.

Tamasya breathed heavily; she remembered falling asleep beside him. Her chest rose and fell unevenly, as if her body were trying to catch up. A faint warmth crept up her cheeks at the memory.

William looked at her. "Good morning," he said with a subtle smile. His voice was steady and calm, but anything but peaceful to her.

Tamasya blinked her eyes for a moment and then nodded. Her lashes fluttered as she tried to steady her thoughts.

William walked to her and handed her some water to drink to make her a bit active.

Only now did Tamasya notice his outfit was different; it was a blue robe decorated with golden accents, and a cape-like cloth hung over his shoulders and made him look like he was wearing a ceremonial robe.

"Why are you wearing such a dress?" she asked, looking at him from head to toe. Her gaze lingered longer than usual, noticing the sharpness in his posture, the way he carried himself like someone about to step into a battlefield.

"I need to leave in a few hours." William said, sitting back on the couch, "I’ve prepared breakfast for you." Stay put until I come back; don’t go anywhere," William said with a smile.

The smile was gentle, but it didn’t quite reach his eyes.

Tamasya looked at him with a frown. A faint crease formed between her brows as unease settled in her chest. "Where are you going?" She asked him.

William looked at her with a subtle smile. "There was something I was not able to address for a long time; finally, I have gotten the chance to take action during these holidays."

His tone was casual, but the weight behind his words lingered in the air like an unspoken warning.

Tamasya still did not understand what he was trying to say, but she still nodded and felt the need to say some things she had sought before sleeping, before doing anything else. Her fingers curled into the bedsheet unconsciously, betraying the storm of emotions she was holding back.

"About us..." Tamasya said before looking at her fidgeting hands. Her voice softened.

"I have made my decision," she said.

William smiled and stopped her with a gesture. "Wait, I would like to hear it after I come back from my mission." William said, "I need immense focus on the upcoming task. I don’t know if you will accept my proposal or reject it, but both answers will be too distracting for me, so I want you to save it for the time I return."

William knew the answer; he could see it in her eyes, yet he still held it for now. There was a quiet certainty in her gaze that told him everything, but knowing it and hearing it were two different things.

He wanted that magic moment to be surrounded by happiness and peace, which would be worth remembering, and not storms that were about to hit him.

He didn’t tell her, but he felt restless today; something big was coming, and he could feel it.

He could feel it; his heartbeats ran cold, and he was feeling nervous, not because of the upcoming Clayman cult’s conflict but because of something else he was unaware of. He was edgy.

He knew the coming days were going to be unexpected.

It had happened yesterday when he had heard several notifications ringing in his head. The sound had pierced through his sleep abruptly; each chime echoed loudly.

[Ding!!! Talent Lawless’s awakening has sensed massive disruptions in fate....]

[Ding!! The host is deemed worthy.]

[Ding!! Congratulations on being awarded the F(suppressed) rank talent ’Oracle of Pandemonium.’]

————⁜⁜⁜————

Oracle of Pandemonium (F) (Suppressed)

Eyes cannot see everything, even if they belong to the #%@ himself.

Effects:

Follow your heart when it tries to fly away from the cage of what is known to you.

[Locked ...]

————⁜⁜⁜————

Just reading the description had opened up so many questions in his mind.

The words felt so incomplete and cryptic.

He had not felt it at first, but the moment he had come out of the infinite domain carrying Tamasya in his arms, his smile had vanished; he had felt it immediately like a ticking time bomb, his heart beating, making him sweat rivers.

It wasn’t just fear; it was anticipation mixed with dread, a sensation that coiled tightly around his instincts. And the worst reason was that he didn’t even know why.

William was not scared, but his instincts as a swordsman were screaming, and his sovereign sword’s intent, if earlier it was a calm river, was now a raging threat, threatening to flood out and destroy everything around it just to protect its host from the invisible threat.

Williams’ brain had gone haywire by the name of the talent, the entity being referred to, and the rank; it was the first time he was being awarded such a low-level talent, and the mark at its side describing it as "suppressed" concerned him.

The entity whose name was censored somehow in the text, he felt it might be the unknown who had invaded his domain, yet he had no proof or clue about it.

What was the real rank of this talent? He had no idea about it, and the system had not given any answers; more like it could not.

William had called it several times, but the system had gone completely silent for a few minutes, as if it had shut down. That silence had been more terrifying than any answer.

Coming back, it had told William that this was a very normal thing and he didn’t need to worry about it.

[Yes, it was just monthly maintenance work of the supreme system,] the system’s voice sounded.

"Fuck off, don’t try to lie to me."

[sigh!! "I am sorry; I just can’t. I am doing everything for you. I can’t tell you things you are not supposed to know for your own safety.] system’s voice sounded defeated, which made William even more worried.

That is why he looked at Tamasya seriously now. "No matter what happens, don’t leave your domain at all!!" William wanted to just put her in his infinite domain and make sure she was safe every moment with him.

But the problem was that this so-called entity called "unknown" had invaded his domain. William felt that even if the unknown won’t do anything to Tamasya, what if something happened to him? Then what would happen to the domain and the beings inside it??

The system had told him the answer. If William dies, the infinite domain collapses completely with him.

Since his heart was beating wildly and something massive was ahead, he felt that she was the safest on her own island, which was laced with several protection arrays and defensive mechanisms, and more importantly, as Tamsya had told him before, Shadow herself protected the whole domain actively. Compared to everything else, this was the one place he could trust.

"But why??" Tamasya stood up, throwing bedsheets to the side. The sudden movement broke the stillness of the room.

She came forward and cupped his face. "William, what happened??" she asked with a serious look in her eyes. Her gaze searched his, trying to pierce through whatever he was hiding.

William shifted his head and lightly kissed her hand before holding it tight. "Don’t worry about it; I just don’t want you to leave the island; I am concerned about your safety."

He looked at her firmly. "Promise me you won’t leave the island and will stay put!" His gaze left no room for argument.

Tamasya looked at him intently before nodding. She could feel the tension in his voice, even if he refused to speak it aloud. "I promise."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 239. Reincarnation: My Sweetheart is a Goddess
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"What is this??" Tamasya asked as she looked at a vial filled with bright, swirling, glittery transparent liquid that looked otherworldly and shimmered with a celestial light.

"Essence of Eleftheira, to help you unlock the remaining seals," William said. He looked at the bottle; this vial had cost him his current monthly free purchase.

Even though it was a risky choice right now, at least it ensured Tamasya’s absolute safety. He was sure that if Tamasya regained her cultivation, then until the gods decided to descend on Aris, she would be untouchable.

Before getting sealed, Tamasya was a being in the goodhood stage of cultivation. Her cultivation rank was True Godhood, and she had just been on the cusp of reaching Absolute Godhood, where a being ascends to the realm of gods.

Reaching the absolute godhood stage of cultivation was only when a being was given the designation of a god; they were given a seat among the gods, and they either rule over elements or laws.

Naturally, there were a handful of more conditions that needed to be fulfilled to become a full-fledged god; it was not as simple as breaking through a rank.

As Will remembered how strong she actually was, his lips curled in happiness and pride. His soon-to-be wife was on the verge of becoming a god as well.

Will stood by her side as he noticed how carefully she was checking the vial out of curiosity.

"Construct a protective barrier around yourself and then just drink it," he said.

"Where did you get this??" Tamasya asked with large eyes filled with curiosity.

"Don’t ask me that." William replied expressionlessly, "Just drink this; it’s good for you."

"I know it’s good for me," she said with a stare, "but I should still know what it is that I am consuming. Is it a potion of sorts? I don’t want to drink something creepy like some insect juice or extracted fluids of some organism??"

William looked at her, cluelessly taken aback by what she was blabbering about; he looked at her weirdly for a few seconds before nodding. "It’s just a potion concocted from herbs."

Tamasya looked at him with a suspicious gaze. Yet she still just nodded. "Stand a few steps away."

William complied and created a few meters of distance from her. With his hands behind his back, he looked at her from a distance. Tamasya gave him a last look before erecting a dark shadow elemental barrier around herself.

William looked at her disappearing figure with a smile.

[Host, she is going to kill you!!]

William listened to the system’s warning.

————⁜⁜⁜————

Essence of Eleftheira (ancient rank)

Blood essence of Goddess Eleftheria, the goddess of freedom and liberty, that she extracted from her blood during her final moments for her daughter, who was sealed by the demon god beyond the Abyss.

If the essence hasn’t been used, it means her daughter is still sealed.

Effects: Drinking it can grant freedom from any type of seal, curse, or restrictions placed unfairly on the target.

————⁜⁜⁜————

"Who is going to kill me first, Eleftherira or Tamasya??" William asked with a smile that said he had just taken a risk with his life.

[maybe both]

"Sigh!!" William shook his head and looked ahead. The dark barrier shook. William knew the unsealing had started.

Inside the shadow barrier, Tamasya looked at the vial for a few more seconds.

<"He was right, this is just a potion,"> Shadow’s voice sounded in Tamasya’s mind.

She felt a bit weird listening to Shadow’s words; she mostly felt suspicious, but then just shook her head, reasoning that she had an overthinking mind.

Without waiting, Tamasya drank the liquid in the vial, swallowing the contents at once.

The liquid entered her mouth and felt cold and thick, also a bit metallic in taste, which Tamsya thought was due to bitter herbs in the potion.

As she emptied the vial in a single go, at first Tamasya felt completely normal, but a few minutes later she felt something burning in her stomach, then in her chest, before the sensation spread to her core.

"Ufhh!!" She released an involuntary sigh as the sensation inside her intensified with every moment. It was not painful but extremely uncomfortable.

She felt her ears go numb, and her nasal passage felt heavy and blocked, like she might start having a cold right now.

<"Sit down,"> Shadow instructed.

Tamasya did not wait and sat in the same posture as she used to while breaking seals or cultivating.

Several wisps of shadow suddenly hurried toward her as if trying to prevent something, but alas, they were too late.

Bang!!

It turns out Tamasya had not erected a strong enough barrier and had truly underestimated the impact of the essence.

After all, William had just marketed it by telling her that it was a potion to help unseal her cultivation, but he had failed to explain how potent the liquid really was.’

Tamasya had undermined its effect, and now William was going to realise this.

The sixth seal broke.

A wave of energy was released as the divine seal dispersed, and Tamasya felt a stinging pain, yet much less than when she was doing it herself.

Her cultivation jumped from pseudo-divinity to full-divinity.

"Urgh!!" she clenched her fists.

The wave of energy struck the barrier of shadow, shaking it in its place. But Tamasya was not even able to raise her head to look; she felt like puking; her guts were twisting.

A few seconds passed before the seventh seal broke, too.

This time, a whole stage was elevated; her cultivation jumped from full-divinity to pseudo-godhood.

The energy wave that was released was massive, like a detonation; it was released with Tamasya as its center.

The barrier stood no chance. It broke and slammed straight into the nearest targets, which were mainly the shadow borns and the clueless William, who was right now wondering if the two ladies he had wronged would hunt him down.

[Host!! watch out!!]

The energy hit Will like a tsunami before slamming him against the wall at the opposite end of the hall.

The furniture and other decor and ornament were shattered into pieces, but surprisingly, the building remained intact, as if it had always been built to sustain such pressures with ease.

William took a deep breath and looked at Tamasya, clenching her stomach and chest on the ground, completely unaware of the waves she released.

[Host, I advise you to enter the domain Infinity.]

William came out of the rubble and said, "No, I want to stay and make sure she is alright."

[Shadow is with her to take care of her; you should first look at yourself in the mirror.]

William took out a mirror, only to be left speechless; his forehead and nose were bleeding.

[The pain hasn’t registered because you have become insensitive to this pitiful amount of pain over the years of suffering much worse, but that doesn’t mean these injuries won’t have side effects. I advise you to enter for just five minutes; I will inform you when she is done.]

William looked towards Tamasya and bit his lips before nodding.

With that, he disappeared in the domain. Those five minutes were six hours for William inside, where he kept fidgeting and asking the system about the updates every minute.

Finally, the five-minute mark ended, and the system gave a green signal for William to come out.

William appeared to notice that the previous debris of home decor, utensils, paintings, and ornaments had turned to powdered dust now.

Tamasya kneeled on her two knees on the floor silently while looking at her hands, opening and closing them repeatedly.

She found it difficult to believe that the 4 strongest divine seals that were left on her were removed in an instant.

She felt her core was blazing like a star; she felt powerful again; she felt like she could conquer the world.

"Tamasya," she heard her name being called from behind; emotions filled her as she turned, stood up, and looked at William.

Before she could pounce on him and shower him with kisses.

<"STOP!!"> She was stopped by the urgent voice of Shadow.

<"Remember what happened to your mate last time you weren’t able to control your strength. If you don’t want to make a pulp out of him, it’s better to control yourself and get used to your newly returned strength first.">

"Oh shit!! You are right," Tamasya said.

She looked at William with an emotional smile; she wanted to express her happiness, yet she couldn’t.

Meanwhile, William wondered why she stood like a statue in front of him.

[She has no control of her strength; maybe she doesn’t want to see you get hurt,] the system said, making William realize the situation.

He just smiled. She could not move, but he could. He went forward and hugged her tight.

"Congratulations, my sweetheart," William said with a light chuckle before planting a kiss on her lips. While Tamasya just clenched her hands controlling her instincts while silently cursing William for almost catching her offgaurd.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 240. Blood of Solaris - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Several hours ago.

Inside the supreme system’s backend...

A vast, incomprehensible void stretched endlessly in all directions. It was neither space nor void, neither digital nor divine; it was something in between yet something beyond.

The system’s spirit. A shiny, white, orb-like astral projection flickered around an enclosed space, carrying out the normal functions of the supreme system and inspecting its own operations.

Golden sigils and mysterious symbols floated everywhere around it, forming intricate patterns and mechanisms. They rotated slowly, interlocking and separating in an endless sea, each carrying fragments of laws that governed reality itself.

The system spirit was now working around a small green sigil, which represented the mental link established by William. The sigil shimmered faintly, like a fragile thread connecting two vastly different planes.

It coordinated several visual and auditory inputs and calibrated everything again, ensuring smooth functioning.

At some point, William will have to do a large-scale teleportation of his Clayman cult’s slaves into the domain of infinity; the system ensured that nothing went wrong when William did that.

[Ding!! Administrator notification #0958: Talent Beloved of the Universe (Supreme) has been activated.

[Do you want to broadcast the notification?] [Y/N]

Suddenly, an urgent notification blared in front of the system spirit.

Its imaginary lips were bitten by its imaginary teeth in frustration. Even without a physical form, its irritation manifested clearly in the erratic flicker of its light.

"Argh!! Bitch, you are here again!! Do you not understand William will get in trouble this way!!"

[Ding!! Administrator notification #0959: Talent Beloved of the Universe (Supreme) has a message for the admin.]

[<{ Dear Orion, Whatever I do is for William’s own good, so can you please fuck off for a moment!!}>]

The system spirit whose name was Orion started thrashing around frantically. Its glow jittered wildly as it darted from one sigil to another like a panicked firefly trapped in a glass cage.

"Fuck, last time you summoned that giant’s ass-like eye, because of that douche bag entering the infinite domain and scaring the shit out of William, I became the scapegoat since I am answerable to him. Do you know he doesn’t even trust me like before since I could not tell him to whom that giant ass-like eye belonged?"

Its movements became more chaotic, colliding with floating sigils that distorted momentarily before stabilizing again.

[Ding!! Administrator notification #0959: Talent Beloved of the Universe (Supreme) is accessing the Supreme System’s Talent Pool.

[Ding!! Administrator notification #0960: Talent Beloved of the Universe (Supreme) is initiating a forced reboot of Absolute Adaptation.

[Ding!! Administrator notification #0961: Talent Beloved of the Universe (Supreme) has turned on the effect ’Lawless awakening.’

[Ding!! Administrator notification #0962: Talent effect Lawless awakening has sensed massive disruptions in fate....

[Ding!! Administrator notification #0963: The host is deemed worthy.]

[Ding!! Administrator notification #0964: Congratulations on being awarded the F (suppressed) rank talent ’Oracle of Pandemonium.’]

"What the fuck!!!" Orion spat, looking at the talent that had just been awarded to William. Its glow dimmed for a split second.

It clenched its imaginary temples and cried in frustration, "At this point, just kill me, you shitty woman (┬┬﹏┬┬)."

[Ding!! Administrator notification #0965: Talent Beloved of the Universe (Supreme) has a message for the administrator.]

[<{ Dear Orion, serve my hubby well, goodbye}>]

Orion looked at the last message from the universe; if he had a mouth, he would surely puke blood on the spot.

A silence spread after this notification sounded.

"How do I explain this??" Orion thought as his astral form stopped thrashing around in frustration. His glow steadied, though it now flickered with lingering anxiety. Reluctantly, he broadcast selective notifications. The decision felt like walking on a razor’s edge.

Orion’s gaze shifted to the sigil in the distance, which broadcast William’s POV and was a medium for them to communicate. The sigil glowed brighter than the others.

He saw William seeing the notification and demanding answers. Even from this abstract plane, William’s curiosity was palpable.

In a panicked manner, Orion muted the sigil to think of something in peace.

Unfortunately, the only lie he could come up with later was to tell William that it was quite normal and that he had been busy with the monthly maintenance of the system. The excuse formed reluctantly, weak even in his own mind.

Which William literally took as a bullshit excuse.

Orion could only sigh in defeat, weighed down by guilt. He wanted to tell William that his own wife was responsible for such things, but he could not.

***

At the same time...

Holy Sun Empire...

A vast empire bathed in divine radiance during the day now stood under a cold, watchful moon. The golden spires of its grand structures shimmered faintly in silver light.

Pope Winston walked into an underground entrance towards the cellars. A staff in his hands, adorned with light stones and golden robes with the church’s symbols and aesthetics.

His old yet straight figure walked alone under the shade of night diluted by the moonlight-filled sky.

The end of his staff knocked on the ground with each step. The sound echoed hollowly, each tap reverberating like a countdown. As he walked towards the entrance of the narrow passage.

The guard at the entrance bowed before opening the thick, black, metallic gate. The heavy gate groaned loudly as it opened.

Winston entered the passage silently and walked towards the deepest and most cruel prison of the Holy Sun Empire. The Iron Hell prison, where special captives were kept. The walls were lined with dark metal, etched with faint glowing runes that pulsed ominously.

A few minutes later, Wiston arrived in the deep underground chamber. The space opened into a massive cavern filled with darkness.

At the center was a deep pit of lava radiating heat unbearable by mortals. The molten surface bubbled violently, casting flickering orange light that danced across the walls like restless spirits.

Winston walked towards a particular cellar to the right of the entrance and stepped in front of the mithril prison. The metal shimmered faintly, unyielding and sacred, forged to contain beings far beyond ordinary strength.

A sight of a woman came into his sight; sapphire blue hair and eyes stared back at him. Even in such a place, her presence felt regal, untouched by the degradation around her.

Winston chuckled. "Have you been enjoying your stay here, Empress Anasthasia Luneia Solaris?" he asked with a mocking smile. His voice dripped with amusement, though his eyes held something sharper.

Anasthasia sat in her blood-stained white robe, heavy chains the size of serpents coiled around her neck and hands, weighing down on her.

She had her head raised lightly from the previous meditative posture and looked at Winston’s figure standing in front of her.

Her stare contained the faint reflections of lava; she looked at Winston as if he were about to die soon. There was no fear, only certainty.

"Oh!! What’s this newfound fire I see in your eyes? Winston sneered, "Did you forget how I defeated your man in front of the whole world?? Hahaha, I can assure you confidently that he begged like a dog for my mercy."

His laughter echoed across the chamber, distorted by the cavern into something almost monstrous.

Annasthasia looked at Winston with the same intensity. Her expression did not waver; her resolve remained unshaken.

"Winston, you should give up on trying to get into my head. I will tell you for the thousandth time, begging for mercy is not something those with the blood of the Solaris clan are capable of. There are only two choices for heirs of the Solaris blood on a battlefield."

Her voice was steady at first, but beneath it burned a rising inferno.

She said while raising her voice a bit, making sure Winston remembers it forever, "They either bring death to their enemies or they welcome it with their heads raised."

Her words struck the air like blades as if she were declaring a universal law.

Anne didn’t stop; her rage-filled voice echoed across the prison. The lava seemed to respond, bubbling more violently as if resonating with her fury.

"I saw your cowardice on the day when you tried to hide your defeat by killing a newborn; that’s your real face, pathetic swine with the soul of a demon."

Each word carried venom, years of suppressed anger pouring out without restraint.

She cursed in rage before stopping, her hands clenched around a small bracelet hidden behind her long hair. That small object, fragile compared to everything else, was the only thing she held onto, a silent anchor amidst the darkness.

The chamber fell into a tense silence as Anasthasia’s words finished.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 241. Blood of Solaris - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Winston’s expression was nowhere near the smile that had been etched on his face when he had arrived in front of Annasthasia. The earlier mockery had completely vanished, replaced by something far uglier and far more honest.

His face was filled with annoyance. The muscles along his jaw tightened; for fifteen years, he had tried to bend the will of this woman; he could not understand what was fueling her. What hope had he left to shatter to see defeat in her eyes?

In fact, if earlier her eyes felt calm, then right now they were fiery, as if something had reignited her spirit.

"Well, if you don’t want to believe me, it’s your choice, but don’t forget that your life is in my hands. Do you even know why I have not killed you till now?" he asked with a sinister expression on his face. He came close to the cellar bars and slammed them with his hands, looking at Annasthasia aggressively.

The metal groaned under his grip.

Anne looked at him with a stern yet unaffected expression. Her piercing gaze looked at him. "How would I know what goes in the mind of a swine?" she said with a sneer that refused any form of fear of death that Winston was suggesting.

Her voice carried quiet contempt, not even granting him the satisfaction of anger, only disdain.

Winston got even angrier now. His breathing grew heavier, his chest rising and falling as if he were barely containing himself.

"5000 years ago, there was a pest called Tamaysa, the last heir of the shadow clan that escaped from Lux’s hands, but my lord knew what you meant to that woman, so you should thank him that he told me to wait and keep you alive."

Winston said his expression slowly turned into a triumphant grin, hoping to break her mind. "Bitch, you are just bait for my lord’s prey," Winston shouted.

His voice echoed violently through the chamber, bouncing off the walls and mixing with the bubbling roar of lava.

Anne looked at him with the same intensity and fire in her eyes; her expression didn’t betray any emotion that suggested she knew about Tamasya’s return.

Winston, without saying anything, moved on the narrow path outside the cellar and walked towards the adjacent cellar. His long cape followed his trail and brushed against the floor.

Anne saw that; her eyes flickered with worry. Suddenly, she heard the gate of the adjacent cellar opening with a creaking sound.

The sound of chains clinking together sounded. Anne rustled and stood up under the heavy chains. Her eyes were wide; she could not understand what Winston was doing.

Her breath hitched as unease coiled tightly in her chest.

After a few minutes of chains clinking and opening noises. Winston’s footsteps sounded.

The sight of Winston walking slowly toward her cellar came into her sight.

In the dim orange glow, her gaze turned to look at the figure behind him, where he dragged a man with old features, sapphire blue hair, and eyes from the chains wound around his neck.

The man’s body hung limply, his feet scraping against the ground, leaving faint trails of parted dust behind.

Anne’s voice faltered. "Father!!" she shouted in panic. The word "tore" out of her throat, filled with disbelief and fear she could no longer suppress.

"Wait!! Where are you taking him!!" she shouted as she saw Winston dragging the weakened figure of her father.

Anne came forward and clenched the mythril bars of the cell. The cold metal bit into her palms, but she didn’t feel it; her entire focus was locked onto the frail figure being dragged away.

Winton turned around and looked at her with a crazed expression. He was finally feeling the satisfaction of seeing despair on the face of this woman who hadn’t moved her eyes for years.

His eyes gleamed unnaturally, like someone who had found twisted joy in another’s suffering. His hands raised the chains, and he looked at the almost half-dead figure of Anne’s father.

"I just want to see the lengths this man is willing to go to keep her daughter alive," he said, making Annesthasia panic.

Tilting his head and looking at Anne with crazed eyes, Winston chuckled and said, "I have come to know that recently the heir of the Shadow Clan is back on Aris; to make her come out of her rat hole, I need him."

His laughter was low and unsettling, echoing unnaturally in the cavern.

Anastasia heard that and was filled with frustration and rage. The name struck something deep within her, igniting a storm she could barely contain. "You will die the most gruesome death; I will make sure of that."

Her voice trembled not with fear, but with fury. This was not the first time she had seen Winston use baits like this; she had been a part of his scheme before, and she knew how vile a mind he had.

Winston just chuckled. "Don’t worry, Anastasia; I will make sure the heads of you, your family, and that heretic shadow born will be hanging on the capital gate for show, just like I did with your husband."

The words were spoken casually, as if describing something trivial, yet each one carried poison meant to break her. The last line was a lie, but Winston had repeated that lie over the years in front of Anne, making her believe that her man was really dead.

"De...ath...is..." Suddenly, the almost unconscious figure of Anne’s father mumbled something. His voice was faint, barely audible, like a dying ember struggling to remain lit. Anne quieted down while Winston looked at the lifeless man in his hands.

Even in that broken state, his presence carried a quiet weight.

"What?" he looked at him with a frown. Annoyance flickered across Winston’s face, quickly replacing his earlier amusement.

In a grainy and heavily breathy voice, the Patriarch of the Luneia clan spoke, "Death!! I see it in your stars," while looking Winston straight into his eyes with his dim eyes.

Winston got so irritated that he threw a punch straight at his face, rendering him unconscious. The impact echoed sharply, the sound sickening in its finality.

Looking at her father being hit, Anne shouted, "You coward! If you have the guts, then open my chains, and I will tell you how precious life is."

Winston left the carvings through the entrance, dragging Anne’s unconscious father with him while ignoring her curses. His figure disappeared slowly into the darkness, leaving behind a suffocating silence.

Every prisoner in the iron hell prison was given small doses of corrosive neurotoxins, making them almost limp and unfunctional. But that was not the case with Anne.

Since Winston had kept her alive under orders from Lux, the light god, to use her as bait for Tamasya to come out of her hiding.

Winston had just kept the rest of them alive to be killed beside Anne. Winston wanted her to witness these deaths of her clan members before dying.

It was a slow, deliberate cruelty designed to break her spirit.

As Winston left, Anne looked at her father’s figure being dragged and broke down into tears under the orange shade of lava. The light flickered across her face, illuminating the tears that streamed down uncontrollably.

Her body trembled, chains rattling softly as her strength faltered. She remained there thinking about what the future held for her; the bracelet in her hands was clenched tighter.

Her sister, her father, and her son were all in danger, and she did not know what to do.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 242. Finding Mole - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Aurelius sat in his study hearing reports from his trusted men, the commander of hounds called number 1 and his personal butler.

The figure of Andrea and Yue Qinglan sat on the couch at the other side of the room silently hearing the same reports. Their posture were too relaxed for the gravity of the situation.

The way they nonchalatly sat There indicated their senority and authority over Aurelius.

"Are you sure there is no one among these people involved with the cults?" Aurelius asked with a conflicted expression. His voice carried disbelief.

He had handed a list to number 1 a few days ago containing the names of almost all the ministers under him and commanders of the imperial army.

Yet the hounds had found no one involved with the cults after investigation. The sheer impossibility of it gnawed at him; either the enemy was far more careful than anticipated or something was being overlooked.

"Who are we missing?" Aurelius asked with tight brows, to which number 1 remained silent. The silence stretched with unspoken thoughts.

He wanted to suggest investigating the imperial family, but he did not dare to say it out loud, so he chose to stay quiet. The risk of even implying such suspicion was enough to end his life if misunderstood by the emperor.

Aurelius’s butler said, "Your majesty, you will have to decide the new date of attack soon; there is a lot of pressure on us since we didn’t inform everyone about the new date of attack."

His tone was polite, but beneath it lay urgency. The machinery of war could not remain idle forever.

Aurelius’s brows tightened even further in annoyance, but he did not say anything and just sat back.

His fingers tapped lightly against the armrest of his chair, a subtle sign of his growing frustration. The two ladies on the couch just stayed quiet and did not add anything.

A few minutes later he murmured, "Attacking again without finding the mole is like inviting death." He spoke before turning around and checking for records of other major people in the administration that could be investigated.

Kaboom!!

Suddenly an explosion sounded, and everyone in the room was alerted. The blast echoed violently through the castle, shaking the very foundations beneath their feet.

Andrea’s eyes widened a bit more than others as if she had heard it before. Recognition flashed across her face.

Immediately she stood up and walked outside. Her movements were swift and decisive, leaving no room for hesitation. Looking at her, so alerted, Yue decided to follow her. Aurelius and Hound number 1, along with the butler, also rushed to see what happened.

All of them came out of Aurelius’s chambers, located at the topmost spire of the castle, only to witness a huge black spaceship hovering in the sky several meters above them.

The sight was surreal an unnatural presence that did not belong to this world. Its massive structure blocked portions of the sky, casting a looming shadow over the castle.

Andrea’s widened eyes immediately recognized the object as the spacecraft in which the Golden Raven had arrived. A flicker of urgency crossed her expression.

She noticed how several soldiers were about to attack; she immediately shouted to stop, "Stop!!"

Her voice cut sharply through the chaos, commanding immediate attention.

Aurelius looked at her and repeated the same orders. While most of the archers managed to retract their bows, a single archer among them, by mistake under the shock of the massive metallic object, launched an arrow.

The arrow swished in the air before striking the metallic body of the spacecraft and turned to dust. It disintegrated instantly, as if the very concept of resistance had been rejected.

The hull of the spaceship opened up in the sky, and a figure jumped out of it.

A dark indigo blue cape fluttered in the sky; the man who had jumped did not float or even try to stop. He just fell under the force of gravity before landing in front of the archer with a thud. The impact sent fractures spider-webbing across the stone, dust rising into the air.

The one who had fired the arrow by mistake shivered as he saw a tall being with a face covered by a blue raven mask land in front of him.

The blue raven landed on the opposite tower where Aurelius and the rest were; he turned to look at that particular archer who had targeted him, and with an intentional look, he walked away.

That single glance was enough to drain the color from the archer’s face.

After a brief run-up, spiritual energy burst forth in his legs before he leaped from the edge of the tower and landed on the other side, where Aurelius was standing.

He came to stand in front of Andrea and Yue and nodded with acknowledgement. "I am Indigo, or you can also call me the Blue Raven. I am a humble servant of the Eternal Sovereign."

His voice was calm and controlled, carrying an authority that did not need to be declared.

Suddenly the huge spaceship turned back into the biomechanical transformation orb and landed on his hands. The massive structure condensed rapidly, folding into itself with impossible precision.

Andrea, Yue, Aureliusn and the others who had been alerted in the whole palace looked at the other worldly transformation with shock apperant in their eyes.

As soon as the spaceship disappeared, Andrea came out of her stupor and nodded at the blue raven.

She welcomed him inside; the blue raven nodded and walked behind her and Yue. Meanwhile, Aurelius, along with his butler and number 1, looked at each other confused before following the three.

The situation had shifted too quickly, leaving little room to process.

As they entered inside, they saw Andrea pouring tea for him, and their expressions froze. Especially Aurelius, it was not like he didn’t know who the eternal sovereign was, but the most difficult fact to accept was that someone like Andrea was showing him such respect.

The quiet act spoke volumes far more than words.

The Blue Raven turned and looked at Aurelius. "I hope you are not sick, Lord Emperor," he said.

The question felt odd to Aurelius; he could not decipher the intent behind the question.

"i am not" he replied in a confused tone.

The blue raven nodded before sipping tea from the cup offered to him. "Good, we will be attacking the cult in a few hours."

His tone made it sound less like a suggestion and more like an inevitability.

"What??" Aurelius, Number 1, the butler, even Andrea and Yue looked at him with shock, stopping his moments mid-track. The blue raven tilted his head and looked at them.

Their reactions hung in the air, heavy with disbelief.

"oh i apologise!! if my indentity is not clear, i am just a humble servant of lord eternal sovreign"

Andrea shook her head. "No, we know that already. It’s just so shocking that you came here so abruptly and told us to get ready," she said. Her tone remained composed, though the surprise was evident.

The blue raven looked at her intently before saying, "Miss Andrea, shock value matters a lot when it comes to operations like these."

The blue raven looked at him with a sigh and shook his head. There was a faint disappointment in that gesture.
