half an hour later...

In a tavern hall somewhere in the outer ring of the capital. The place was alive with noise, laughter, drunken chatter, and the clinking of mugs echoed against the walls.

The wooden tavern structure was darkened by years of smoke and spilled ale. The air was thick with the scent of cheap alcohol and roasted meat, creating an environment both lively and suffocating.

A cloaked figure stood in front of the main counter. His presence blended oddly into the chaos around him.

"This is the payment for the booze," the man said before passing a single bronze coin to the man across the counter. The tavern owner looked intently at the coin for a few seconds before picking it up.

The cloaked man left the tavern after paying without looking back. blending into the crowd.

Meanwhile, the tavern owner engaged with new customers for a few minutes before another man came up beside him and started helping him.

"I want you to take up your shift early today," the tavern owner said, looking at the man silently. The newly arrived figure looked at him a bit longer and then nodded before taking on the counter.

No further words were exchanged, yet an understanding passed silently between them.

The tavern owner opened the shelf below the counter and took out the bronze coin handed to him earlier; he had kept it separately among the silver coins.

From the back door of the tavern, the man entered the busy streets of the capital. The streets were still alive despite the early morning.

Unknown to him, faint energy was shuffling between the shadows of people around him, specifically following him. It moved imperceptibly, merging seamlessly with the darkness cast by flickering torches and moving bodies.

A few minutes passed, and the man kept on roaming around, taking detours and turning around narrow alleyways. His path seemed random, as if he was testing whether he was being followed.

The shadow still kept following him without losing the trail. It slipped across walls, stretched along cobblestones, and vanished into corners only to reappear again behind him.

Finally, looking back a few times, the man ensured nobody followed him. His eyes scanned carefully, lingering on every movement behind him.

After he was satisfied, he turned and walked straight to the inner circle of the city.

The shadow kept following him, jumping between objects and people. until the man started entering the core area of the city, where only the most secure merchants and highly esteemed noble families lived.

The environment changed drastically into cleaner streets, guarded entrances, and an oppressive silence that replaced the chaos of the outer ring.

The shadow followed him into the core area without hesitation. A few minutes later, the man stood in front of the Winter Duke’s mansion. The towering structure loomed over him.

The man was stopped by the guards standing at the entrance, but then suddenly he whispered something in the guards’ ears, and he was allowed entry without problem. The guards exchanged brief glances before stepping aside, their expressions tightening ever so slightly.

The looks of this commoner man contrasted with the noble, elegant surroundings, yet nobody stopped him; he straight walked up to barracks where the soldiers trained, and without waiting, he walked around them towards the stable where steeds of the Winter family were being taken care of.

Inside the stable, he met the keeper of that place and handed him the coin with a smile. "This is my first installment of the debt I took last month," he said before leaving the man with the copper coin.

The smell of hay and animals filled the air; the man cleaning the horses stood up, walked out of the stables, and entered the main palace from the front door. The shadow didn’t lose any trail and kept following the stablekeeper.

The man walked inside and directly met the main butler. "My lord," he bowed and presented the copper coin.

His posture was humble, but his movements carried a strange rhythm.

"My lord, yesterday I was handed out my income, but the officer gave out a defective copper in the sack of coins; it hasn’t been minted with the imperial seal. Now he won’t return this; please help me deal with this precarious situation, my lord."

His tone was carefully crafted, with just enough desperation to be believable.

The butler nodded and took the coin before taking out another copper coin and handing it to the stable keeper. "Thank you, my lord, may you..." The man was about to praise him, but the butler dismissed him instantly.

The man left promptly, while the Butler walked into Duke Winter’s study silently and shut the door behind him. With a silent gesture, he presented the coin to the duke.

Duke Winter had a tall, muscular figure, his white hair folded back into a ponytail, and a blue-white noble attire fitted tightly to his frame. His presence alone commanded respect, his sharp eyes reflecting years of experience.

The duke took the coin and broke it with a bit of strength; the coin broke like an eggshell from the center, revealing a small note.

Inside the note, Duke Winter read the contents carefully, and his brows rose. His expression shifted from calm composure to alert focus in an instant.

He looked at his butler and instructed, "Let’s go to the imperial palace; we need to inform him immediately," he said with a hurried expression. Urgency replaced the previously controlled atmosphere.

It was unknown to him that the shadow of a decorative vase at the corner of the room moved faintly.

After the two men left the room, the shadow rustled and left through a window of the room. It slipped out like a wisp of smoke, vanishing into the daylight.

In Aurelius’s study...

Almost an hour had passed, and everyone had been sitting quietly, waiting for someone. The silence had grown heavy, each passing second stretching longer than the last.

"Aren’t we wasting our time here waiting if we really have to attack in 3 hours?" Aurelius asked. His patience was wearing thin; his fingers tapped rhythmically against the armrest of his seat.

Blue Raven moved his head to look at him. "Patience is the most essential tool of a leader," he said, making Aurelius irritated. Who was he to teach him? Aurelius was a bit annoyed but didn’t say much.

Soon, a whisp of shadow flickered in front of them, only to form conjecture and take the shape of Number 1. The transformation was smooth yet eerie, the shadow condensing into a solid form.

Number 1 kneeled in front of Aurelius. His head lowered in respect, though his presence carried urgency.

"Speak Number 1, did you find anything suspicious about him?"

Number 1 gave a slight pause before speaking. "Yes, my lord, I did find something suspicious," he said, making everyone’s ears perk up, especially Aurelius, who found it hard to believe. His butler was way older than him and had served his father faithfully, and it had been the same with him.

"Are you sure??" Aurelius asked again. His voice carried disbelief.

Number 1 sighed in his mind; he knew this was going to be more difficult to explain than he had earlier anticipated.

Grimlock was now walking around him but still not close enough to look like he followed him. After all, he was just a junior elder.

Finally, under Grimlock’s instructions, Agrath (Amorphous) found the meeting room. The room was unfamiliar, but his confidence masked any hesitation.

"All the best," Grimlock spoke before standing in a corner a few meters away from the door of the meeting room.

He decided to assist Amourophous navigate the upcoming meeting, which was beyond his expectations and knowledge, as far as Grimlock knew.

There was supposed to be no upcoming meetings today.

That could only mean one thing, which was the presence of an uninvited guest.

Amorphus entered the room with a dizzy expression as if he were mildly high on certain substances; after all, that’s how Agrath was most of the time, smoking more than breathing.

The act was deliberately staged by Amourpohous as he had noticed how Agrath acted. Also, a sloppy exterior allowed room for inconsistencies in behavior without raising alarms.

"What do you see??" Grimlock asked hurriedly in his mind.

"What is a pheonix doing here??" Amourophous asked as if he had seen the most unexpected thing.

"Phoenix??" Grimlock asked with a shocked expression. There were no phoenixes working in the main headquarters of the Clayman cult; most were humans or more populated races like elves, vampires, and beastfolk.

Phoenixes were rarely seen in this part of the continent. Even if there were phoenixes in the cult, they were rare and mostly in bases located near the Ashfall Empire.

"Why are his eyes bloodshot?? I don’t remember cucking him ever??" Amourohous spoke, making Grimlock suddenly twitch.

"What is he wearing??" he asked.

"A rough robe like a beggar, or maybe he is a prisoner," Amorphus spoke in his mind while he walked towards his seat.

He slowly settled on his seat in a relaxed, laid-back posture, looking at the phoenix-kin man who seemed to want to kill him at any moment.

"Look at his forehead. Is there a golden head ornament worn by him?" Grimlock asked Amorphous.

"Yeah, he has one," Amorphus replied while silently looking at the man who was biting his lips silently now.

"A high-born phoenix," Grimlock’s voice sounded in Amorphus’s mind. That single identification raised the stakes of the room instantly.

"How many red rubies are engraved on that band?? " Grimlock asked

"I see nine slots, but one slot is empty on the right side; the rest are embedded with red rubies." Amourphous said he was getting confused now.

As someone who had spent most of his time in the Abyss and even when he arrived on Aris had spent most of his time in the Skybreak Empire, he knew less about the pheonixes as a race.

"Shit," Grimlock exclaimed.

"What happened??" Amorphous asked hurriedly.

"That must be a member of the imperial family that rules over the Ashfall Empire; the empty slot means he has died one time."

"Oh well, let’s see what this one has to say." Amourohous didn’t panic; he looked at the man looking at him with a hateful expression.

"Will you say something??" he asked, pretending to know him.

The phoenix man with sharp and young features gritted his teeth, his fists clenched together.

"My Celera was killed."

"Oh!!!" Amourohous couldn’t care less in actuality, but he still showed a shocked expression as if he had heard completely unexpected news.

Then his expression turned grim; clearly the man was grieving for this Celera; he just needed to show the right reactions to get all the context from this phoenix-kin.

Years of deception in the Skybreak Empire had made him a master at it, and the natural actor talent bestowed by William made his work easier than ever.

"What happened??" he asked with a grim expression. The man in front of him bit his lips, and a few drops of tears escaped his red eyes. The question opened a floodgate of tears.

"The Celestial army, Commander Damian, found out that she was a spy! That bastard, whore’s son, killed my Celera in such a cruel manner!!!" the man now cried at full volume.

"I didn’t even get to see her for the last time; she was thrown in the sewers after getting herself crushed and broken into pieces by that bastard!!" His grief turned into volatile rage slowly now.

"I searched the sewers of that place desperately day and night, only to find this," he said before taking out a skeletal bone of a hand; it was broken from a side.

He rubbed his face against its surface, making amorphous frown. The act of this man blurred the line between mourning and obsession.

"That bone belongs to a male; look at its width and proportions," he said.

"What??" the man in front of him asked with a frown. Agrath nodded with a serious expression, confirming he was not joking at all.

The phoenix’s eyes widened, and he threw the bone to the side abruptly. For a moment, anger replaced sorrow entirely.

His teeth gripped his lips tightly. He looked at amorphous "A member of your cult was killed so ruthlessly, and you have no clue??" Now the blame shifted outward, searching for accountability.

Amorphis realized the reason why this man had been staring at him hatefully. With a sigh he replied.

"The shadows of war are looming over my cult right now; the human emperor is about to attack us any day."

"Especially after our operations in the south got halted. The cult is struggling to get back on its feet; thousands of Clayman cult members died under the hands of the celestial army. It’s only natural that we overlooked Celera’s absence."

"But don’t worry. Now that we know what they did to her, we will give her all the respect and rights she deserved; after all, she martyred her life for the demon lord Clayman. I am sure he will compensate you properly."

Amourphous’s intention wasn’t to make this guy calm down; it was to inflame the anger in him even more.

By now he had realized that this woman had a romantic relationship with this royal phoenix, the way she caressed that bone.

Smash!!!

"FUCK!! YOUR COMPENSATION!! Do you think Astra Ashfall seeks some petty compensation from you?" the phoenix shouted and slammed the table in front of him, breaking it apart.

Amorphous’s eyes widened on the surface while he gleefully informed Grimlock that he had gotten the name out of this man’s mouth.

"Hmm, Astra Ashfall doesn’t ring a bell in my mind, but the Ashfall family is the ruling family of the Ashfall Empire. Well, at least we have something to report to Lord William now," Grimlock said.

"Try to get some more information out of his mouth." Grimlock suggested.

Amorphous, meanwhile, confronted the enraged Astra. "What do you seek, Astra??" he asked.

Astra’s sobs right now had turned muffled; he clenched a part of his face and started laughing maniacally.

"Khekke!! I want to see his head crushed; I want revenge; I want the head of Damian Cross, the commander of the Abyssal Extermination division."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 248. Buried past
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Arghh!!!! My lord, forgive me!!" A piercing shrill cry escaped the mouth of the old butler inside William’s moving ship.

Blue Raven, aka William, sat at the corner of the chamber with mana clouded around his ears. He looked at the mess happening in the center of the huge chamber.

Several hounds pinned the old butler to the floor while Number 1 brutally interrogated the poor old hag; he looked at the sliced fingers and poked out eyeballs on the floor.

And the irony is that the old hag hadn’t even confessed to any collusion with the demon cults; he had just agreed to be on the side of Daniel and betray Aurelius in his political missions and leak private decisions and policies to his brother.

William wondered whether the wailing and broken old butler even knew he was helping a traitor of the kingdom. The reason he had given right now was just his belief that Daniel was the rightful owner of the throne as the elder sibling.

William just wondered at this point why the old emperor never crowned Daniel even after declaring him the crown prince. No one knew the reason, and this lack of reason and abrupt change of decision was what made the choice of Aurelius as the emperor questionable in front of the world.

In the novel there was a certain excerpt where people in Riverdale talk about how the emperor’s mother used some forbidden demonic spell to control the old emperor.

Although the claim itself had no solid weight to it, William still wondered what had happened. The novel never explored the past history and politics of the continent and just remained stuck to the story forward with Ethan killing enemies, rising in ranks, and a classic hero journey.

Now that William looked at the characters in real life, this unexplained decision that he had never cared about before was just making him realize how it had left so many cracks behind.

The previous emperor left Daniel with an unfulfilled promise by making him crown prince but not the emperor, while he left Aurelius with an empire that was broken by the former decision.

Everything had done a 360-degree turn and come back to bite Aurelius in the back in the form of betrayal from his own butler and unrest among the public who doubted his leadership at every step of the story.

Finally, the cries of that man stopped; he lost his life under constant torture for the past half an hour.

William turned to look at Aurelius’s figure, who looked out of the window of the spacecraft silently; his figure did not even move an inch from the time he had been standing there, even after the butler died while apologizing to Aurelius in his last moments.

William guessed his thoughts; usually people become oddly silent when they are having flashbacks of past trauma or painful memories. There was clearly something that the novel didn’t mention, something that made these characters real.

With another second of waiting, he turned to look at the two ladies who were having a silent conversation using voice transmission; their silent looks at each other were proof that William’s assumption was not wrong.

The grim expressions on their faces made him feel that their conversation revolved around Aurelius and the imperial family’s past.

William felt the need to unearth the whole picture; it was just his curiosity fueled by the reason that he was now a part of this world now, so he needed a better idea about its history and politics for a better future.

"My lord, are you there?" Suddenly Grimlock’s voice sounded in Williams’s mind, diverting his attention.

"Yes, did something happen?" William asked, confused by Grimlock’s voice.

Grimlock then proceeded to explain the whole situation about Astra Ashfall’s arrival, his status, and his thirst for revenge.

William’s brows furrowed; the guy sounded like trouble right now.

Someone who was able to have multiple rebirths was actually trouble in the true sense because even if William managed to catch him and restrain him in some way, then he would just commit suicide and be reborn with the help of a divine rebirth embryo.

The exploitable factor thaat was ’fear of death’ became meaningless in case of high born pheonixes who had the privellage to use divine rebirth embryo.

William couldn’t deny it, the imperial family’s memebers of Ashfall empire really had a cheat code on them; 9 rebirths almost made them unkillable most of the time in battles.

Killing Astra was out of the question anyway, so William just gave Grimlock the usual orders for now.

"Turn into Mika and feed him some chocolates, and try to dismiss him in an hour; otherwise, I will just have to count him as collateral damage," William said.

"Don’t deviate from the plan; in fact, now Amorphus has a fair reason to present to all elders and hold them under one roof.

We will deal with that phoenix the same way we deal with other elders; if he tries to escape, we just let him," he spoke in a clear tone without any speck of doubt.

He was sure that even if Astra Ashfall escapes, his connection to that Spy Celera, could be exploited, or the fact that he was thirsting for revenge against Damian Cross could also be utilised according to how Will found suitable.

For how he would do so was a question he left unasnwered for now, since he had to focus on other important things.

"System, will the blood of mine that Astra would ingest through chochlates? Will it carry forward after rebirth??"

[No, unless you place a binder; that’s not possible.]

"Thought so," William spoke. At last he had to leave a few loose ends for the future unless an idea popped up in his mind in the next hour.

With a sigh he shifted his thoughts to the upcoming completion of the unknown’s mission. He wondered what the next subquest be after the current one is completed.

———⁜⁜⁜——————⁜⁜⁜———

[# Unknown’s Chain Quest]

[Sub-quest 1: Where is the veil??]

[Description: The Clayman cult’s leader knows the location of the veil.]

[Task: Enslave the cult leader and find the veil]

[Rewards: Sun-based God Spell]

———⁜⁜⁜——————⁜⁜⁜———

"Definitely excited for the new spell though," William said with a smile. He looked forward to it, although the effect of his new talent, Oracle of Pandemonium, still showed effects; the unrestful feeling and palpitations in his heart didn’t stop, meaning the impending crisis was still overhead.

"When will they stop??" William asked finally as he couldn’t control himself under the repeated beating.

[When you face the crisis, your heartbeats will turn normal, and you will feel utter silence.]

"Is this a crisis, an entity, or a phenomenon of some kind??"

[anything that will test you and change you]

hearing system’s cryptic words William chuckled. "Change me?? What’s left to be changed in me?? I’ve had worse experiences, and I don’t think I have anything left in me to change anymore other than becoming more and more unsure of my ability to fullfill my revenge properly."

The system remained silent, choosing not to reply, while William brushed it off and left his seat only to enter the control chamber of the ship and lock it before disappearing into the infinite domain; he had a new assignment for Maris.

[A/N - A few things to make clear]

- I never have or will nerf my protagonists.

- Yes, Will is still in a phase to grow and find more about himself, but trust me, everything will pertain to his goal of revenge and make him more focused towards it.

- I know the blood-boiling (or some might say ’edgy’) personality you read about in the start is slowly dissolving, that’s just what I wanted to do from the start. I want to make him focused practical, and selectively cold. Though that doesn’t mean he will stop kicking ass.

- I know that the William from the starting Chapters who didn’t care much about the massacre of Opera Town has changed a lot in some right ways, he has surely given up the idea of sticking to the script.

Although I would still like to know how you guys are feeling about MC’s personality in the comments.
