During his young age, the Miller family was still struggling to get on its feet, and everyone used to think that the Miller family would no longer be able to afford calling itself a merchant family.

But everything changed one day when a mysterious lady from a distant land arrived at their house asking for work; she was educated, and her aptitude for handling institutions was brilliant.

Over the period of five years, the lady from the distant lands worked hard with Lord Miller and helped the family stand on their feet. Bernard Miller looked up to the lady with respect because of her brilliance, and eventually that respect turned into a budding love between them.

Bernard was a cultivator in the mortal realm; hence, his age was almost reaching a hundred, while no one knew the age of the lady. Lord Miller did not seem to care about the age anyway.

By the end of the 7th year since her arrival, she was betrothed to Lord Bernard; only then did her name get famous among the locals of Opera Town.

She was Samantha Miller, the lady of the Miller house, a harbinger of prosperity to her husband.

17 years ago, on a moonlit night, 100 black-robed assassins surrounded the Miller estate from all sides; they were sent by the business rivals of the Miller family to remove them from the equation of Central Land’s business.

Especially to kill Samantha.

Late at night, every assassin was stealthily waiting to strike the manor at a single signal from their master.

Creak. Suddenly, these assassins noticed a lady opening the main door of the mansion and walking into the garden with an ignorant expression; in her arms, she carried a newborn baby girl.

The assassin leader immediately recognized Samantha Miller; in an instant, he ordered three of his men to proceed and retrieve her head as proof of the task.

In a single second, three assassins turned into silver streaks of lightning, and all three of them appeared at the same time, surrounding Lady Miller from all sides.

Their daggers shone in midair, and they did not even wait for themselves to land on the ground. They swung the blades in sync; even if the target managed to parry one blade, there was still the second one coming for her. If she managed to block even that, then there was a third.

The assassin leader was meticulous for every possibility; he himself remained ready to strike at any moment.

A sound of metal cleaving flesh sounded, and for a moment the assassin leader smirked, thinking his mission was a success, but then his eyes cheated his brain; he saw the bodies of the three assassins cleaved in half.

He immediately regained his composure and ordered everyone to attack together; the rest of the 97 assassins threw their daggers and arrows, some of which were aimed at the baby girl as well.

But before the projectiles could even reach the target, 96 heads touched the ground again, creating sounds similar to 100 watermelons falling on the ground for a moment.

Meanwhile, the assassin leader looked at Samantha Miller standing in front of him with a cold expression; suddenly, the baby woke up.

Weeehhh!! Weeehh!!

The little girl let out a cry. Samantha comforted the baby with light taps and shakes while the assassin leader’s legs shook at the same time.

As the baby came to rest, Samantha looked up at the assassin leader, and with a smile that couldn’t be classified as gentle, she planted a knife on his forehead, pinning the blade deep into his brain through the skull.

The blood splashes were stopped by a mana barrier, and with that, Samantha left the garden, letting the 100 bodies be an example to not mess with the Miller family.

The next morning arrived; a couple arrived on the streets of Opera Town.

A tall, broad-chested male with black hair, fair skin, and an orange flame mark on his forehead, he wore a white tunic and brown pants. He walked slowly and carefully while supporting his 7-month pregnant wife who was also dressed mogestly in a gown and with a hat hiding most of her face.

The man shielded her at every turn and supported her even at the smallest dent on the roads. The blue-haired lady chuckled, "You need to tone it down. People are giving weird looks, you know."

Rudra shook his head. "Anna, I agreed to let you walk around the town on foot, but don’t stop me from caring about my child."

Annasthasia looked at him with bright eyes. "I wanted to see what type of life my sister lives every day in this town; I needed to experience the town myself."

Rudra looked at her and sighed. "Seriously, you could have done so later when you were not carrying our child, but you picked the peak time of your pregnancy; it’s not safe this way!!"

Anne smiled and cupped Rudra’s face. "When you are with me!! Then I don’t need to worry."

Rudra’s brows furrowed. "You are good with words."

Anna smiled while her head turned to look at the city square; one of her hands supported her belly while the other was wrapped around Rudra’s arm.

"Seems like we are about to reach the Miller mansion," Anna spoke, but suddenly Rudra stopped and stopped Anna as well. She looked at his tense expression and then activated her own divine senses; she sensed it several feet ahead and saw the Miller mansion.

She observed a pile of dead bodies and a lot of commotion in the area.

Rudra looked at her. "The scent of blood is thick; I am taking you up now; your morning walk ends here."

He said it, and without letting Anna speak, he lifted her in the air carefully and flew into the sky before arriving in the skies above the Miller mansion, since exerting mana for pregnant ladies was not recommended, only for weaker ranks, definitely not Anna’s, but Rudra was careful, which Anna always called out as overcautiousness.

Suddenly, since they hadn’t hidden their auras, they sensed a presence approaching them at full speed. The intent seemed hostile, so Rudra called out instantly.

’Chromos’

As soon as the word left his mouth, a black metallic spear with ancient red castings appeared in the air; its temperature reached unimaginable thresholds, and it floated in the air independently.

It sensed the hostile presence approaching and changed direction itself without waiting; under Rudra’s order, it launched downwards to intercept the enemy.

Meanwhile, Rudra closed his eyes, and his vision integrated with Chromos. He looked at the approaching figure, and seeing the blue sapphire hair similar to Anna’s, he stopped the spear in its tracks.

Samantha stopped as well, looking at the threatening flaming spear in front of her. A bead of sweat appeared as she felt the aura was too overwhelming for her to deal with.

Immediately, the spear disappeared, and in its stead, Rudra appeared, carrying Anna.

"Sister!!" Anne called out, making Samantha widen her eyes. Her gaze moved to her belly, and her eyes narrowed for a moment.

"Follow me," Samantha said before turning into a descent.

Rudra looked at the lady and then turned to look at Anna in his arms. "Is that the only expression your sister carries??"

Anna chuckled, "It’s a long story, but we sisters had a hint of sourness in our bond."

Rudra rolled his eyes. "Still, she should learn from you; you let out 100 expressions in 1 second, and your sis doesn’t change at all."

Anna turned to look at Rudra playfully. "So!! I keep you entertained, right?"

Rudra sighed jokingly, "Lord Miller must have a peaceful life," while shaking his head.

This sentence earned Rudra a heavy punch on his head.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 325. A Distant Memory of the Past - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[A/N: Just for clarity Rudra calls his wife Anna while others call her Anne. Now i don’t know why i made such a difference but i can’t seem to change that small habbit of Rudra’s character, so don’t think of it as a mistake]

------------

Anna and Rudra entered through the back entrance of the mansion, away from the areas stained by last night’s bloodshed.

Even from this quieter side of the estate, the atmosphere remained tense. Servants hurried through the corridors carrying trays, delivering messages, and escorting guests or officers who had arrived to investigate the incident.

The entire mansion felt restless.

Samantha personally guided the two of them toward a more secluded section of the estate. Along the way, several servants glanced curiously at the newcomers. Their clothing appeared modest by the standards of the Millers household, lacking the extravagance expected from noble guests.

None of them could have imagined that the man and woman walking quietly through the halls were actually the rulers of the flourishing Solaris Empire.

Their disguises were flawless enough to deceive nearly everyone in town.

Once they entered the room, Rudra carefully helped Anna settle onto a comfortable couch before taking a seat beside her.

A few moments later, Samantha returned with a maid carrying refreshments. Water, tea, and light snacks were placed neatly before them with all the formal courtesy expected toward guests.

Soon afterward, the room emptied.

And at that time the silence settled heavily between the sisters.

Anna and Samantha stared at each other without speaking, both uncertain where to begin after years of separation and unresolved emotions.

Before the awkward tension could deepen further, the door suddenly burst open.

"Mom!!"

A young man around twenty-three years old rushed into the room before abruptly freezing in place the moment he noticed the unfamiliar guests sitting across from Samantha.

Dereck Millers’ gaze shifted toward Anne.

The moment he saw her face, all color drained from his expression.

The resemblance between the two women was so uncanny that for a second he genuinely felt as though he were looking at another version of his own mother. The same blue hair. The same facial structure. Only certain expressions and wrinkles were different.

His eyes widened in disbelief.

Before he could even ask anything, Anna gave a light chuckle and said, "Hello!! I am your mom’s long-lost sister; we were separated during our birth and coincidently reunited today."

Dereck’s expression became even more stunned after hearing that.

Slowly, he turned toward Samantha as if seeking confirmation that this bizarre statement was some kind of joke.

Samantha silently nodded.

"Excuse me!!"

The words came out almost automatically before he quickly turned around and left the room.

His mind was already racing.

The mansion had barely survived a major incident the previous night, and now strangers had suddenly appeared claiming to be long-lost relatives. Even if the resemblance was undeniable, caution was still necessary.

He clearly intended to find his father immediately and report everything. Once the door closed behind him, silence returned again.

The sisters resumed staring at one another, neither willing to break first.

Rudra finally rolled his eyes at the unbearable atmosphere and stepped forward, extending a storage ring toward Samantha.

Samantha accepted it slowly before asking, "What is this?" Her tone remained cold and guarded.

Rudra smiled calmly. "In there is a wedding gift and some other specialties for the children as a gift from their aunt and uncle."

Samantha looked down at the ring for a moment before raising her eyes again.

"You expect something in return?"

Anna finally nodded softly. "We don’t Samantha, we just need you to bless our first child," she said with a faint but hopeful smile.

Samantha gave a dry scoff.

"Why does my nephew need blessings of his cursed aunt, that too still being in the womb?"

The bitterness in her voice immediately caused Anna’s expression to falter.

"I never considered you cursed," Anna replied quietly.

Samantha laughed bitterly.

"Oh please! I spent a lonely childhood despite having a twin. Do you know how pathetic that feels and how it changes everything in you??"

Her voice gradually sharpened with years of buried resentment.

"To be given avoidant looks from your own family, to feel like a cast out—if not for Father, I would have left that place sooner."

Anna listened to this and slowly lowered her head as tears slowly gathered in her eyes. her fingers clenged the helm of her gown.

"I was told..." she tried to explain, but the words refused to come out properly.

The guilt sitting inside her heart was too heavy.

Seeing her struggle, Rudra gently pulled her against his chest before looking toward Samantha with an apologetic expression.

"When you are a child, your mind is easy to mold into thinking what elders want you to think. Anna’s mind was molded as such to avoid her own sister, and that guilt is something she has been carrying in her heart since the day she realised how she messed up."

His tone remained calm and sincere.

"I can’t restore what you have lost. I don’t even expect you to forgive Anna, but I hope you can forgive our son. He is not born yet, but I am sure when he does, we will make him the best human being we can."

Rudra paused briefly before continuing in a gentler voice.

"Samantha, our son would need an aunt who can guide him in times when parents cannot; he would need an elder brother and sister too."

As he spoke, one of his hands gently caressed Anna’s shoulder, trying to calm her.

Anna herself could no longer meet Samantha’s eyes.

From the very beginning, this confrontation had been the thing she feared most. She had prepared herself mentally during the entire journey, yet hearing Samantha finally voice all the pain she had endured shattered whatever emotional composure Anna still possessed.

She broke down completely.

Meanwhile, Samantha watched her crying sister in silence.

It was strange.

As children, Anna had rarely cried. Even when injured, she usually held herself together unless their mother was involved somehow.

Seeing her like this now stirred emotions Samantha did not want to acknowledge.

After several long moments of silence, Anna finally regained enough composure to breathe properly again while Rudra quietly comforted her.

"It’s not good for the kid if you cry during pregnancy."

Samantha’s tone had softened noticeably this time. The hostility in her eyes had lessened, replaced instead by reluctant concern.

She folded her arms before asking, "Why do you specifically need my blessing?? I don’t think your medics and nannies would have allowed you to move at this stage of pregnancy??"

Rudra answered honestly.

"A wandering hermit told us that if our child gets the blessing of his aunt while being unborn, then he would go a long way in life."

Samantha narrowed her eyes immediately.

"You took the words of a wandering hermit so seriously??"

Rudra shook his head.

"That hermit definitely had seer-like abilities; she revealed a lot to us."

Samantha still looked unconvinced.

Then Rudra casually added, "She also told us that you have blessings of someone special."

The moment those words left his mouth, the atmosphere inside the room changed completely.

Samantha’s eyes widened.

A sudden chill spread through the air as the temperature in the room dropped sharply.

Both Rudra and Anne reacted instantly.

Rudra stood up at once and summoned a protective barrier of stellar fire around Anna without hesitation.

"Calm down."

The voice that answered to them no longer sounded entirely human.

Both of them stared in shock as Samantha’s blue pupils slowly vanished and were replaced by glowing green ones. At the same time, her voice gained an unnatural resonance, as though multiple tones were layered beneath her words.

The entire room filled with tiny green particles of light suspended motionlessly in the air.

"Who are you??" Rudra asked cautiously, his expression immediately turning vigilant.

"I am the special someone that the hermit told you about," Samantha replied.

Her entire demeanor had changed. The cold bitterness that usually defined her expression had disappeared completely, replaced instead by an oddly serene smile.

After sensing no immediate hostility, Rudra slowly lowered the barrier and released a quiet breath.

"Can we know your name?" Anna asked carefully with concern.

"No," the entity replied simply.

Anna and Rudra exchanged uneasy glances. By this point, they had already realized what they were witnessing.

Spirit possession.

And not an ordinary one.

For an entity to completely control someone in the Godhood stage, it would need to be vastly stronger than the possessed individual. Samantha herself already stood among the strongest beings within the Aris Realm.

Which meant the entity speaking through her was likely beyond the realm itself.

The green-eyed figure tilted its head slightly before speaking again through Samantha’s body.

"Tell me about this hermit."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 326. A Distant Memory of the Past - 3
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The green-eyed figure tilted its head slightly before speaking again through Samantha’s body.

"Tell me about this hermit."

Rudra looked toward Anna for a brief moment before answering.

"She called herself keeper of fate; we only know that much. She was a simple black-haired lady."

The entity possessing Samantha remained silent for several seconds after hearing that description. Samantha’s green eyes narrowed faintly, as though she were searching through distant memories or piecing together fragments of knowledge she had forgotten over eons.

The room gradually fell into silence.

The floating green particles still drifted through the air like suspended starlight, bathing the chamber in an ethereal glow. Neither Rudra nor Anna dared interrupt the entity’s thoughts; by now, Rudra understood this much: whoever she was, she was definitely a goddess or a stronger being.

After a few minutes, Samantha finally raised her head again and looked directly at the couple.

"You two carry powerful physiques belonging to outer gods," she said calmly.

Rudra frowned slightly. "What is an outer god??"

Samantha shook her head.

"It’s not your place to know; I will just fulfill the word of the hermit, though I don’t know her, I still will because you are here for a blessing, and I don’t refuse anyone who asks for my blessing."

In truth, she could refuse, but she had her personal agenda. While blessing the couple, she wanted to spread her influence and start her own religion in this world, while not revealing traces of her presence.

As soon as she finished speaking, Samantha slowly raised one hand.

The countless green particles scattered throughout the room immediately reacted. They began circling her palm before forming a spinning vortex of emerald energy. The entire room trembled faintly under the pressure released by the phenomenon.

Then, in an instant, the vortex shot forward and entered Anna’s womb.

Anna gasped sharply.

Her breathing became uneven for several moments as a strange warmth spread through her body. Rudra immediately moved closer to support her, but after a few tense seconds, her condition stabilized, and her breathing slowly returned to normal.

The possessed Samantha lowered her hand.

"Your child was supposed to lose his life during his mana awakening," she said.

The moment those words reached them, both Rudra and Anna froze completely.

Anne’s hands instinctively moved to her womb as fear flooded her face. Her fingers trembled uncontrollably while Rudra forced himself to remain calm enough to speak.

"Why do you say so?" Rudra asked carefully.

The entity answered without hesitation.

"As I said, both of you carry outer god physiques that are powerful on their own; normally, such people are not supposed to marry or have a child together, but oh well, what can we do about the love of fledglings who think love is absolute?"

Despite the casual tone, the words carried an undeniable weight.

"Well, I suppose your marriage was definitely not supported by your two clans, am I not right?"

Rudra’s expression stiffened slightly while Anna unconsciously tightened her grip around his hand.

The entity had spoken the truth.

Back then, the Luneia Clan had already begun searching for a suitable groom for Annasthasia. Yet strangely enough, despite being one of the most talented and influential young rulers in the world, Rudra Solaris had never once appeared among the proposed candidates.

At the time, neither of them had understood why. But it had never mattered to them.

Their feelings for one another had already taken root long before that, during their years together at the World Academy.

Anastasia still remembered those days vividly. Their stolen meetings. Their secret conversations and exchange of letters.

The understanding they shared, even when the entire world seemed determined to keep them apart.

Eventually, the pressure from their clans became unbearable.

So one night, Rudra secretly brought Anna away from the Luneia Clan under everyone’s noses. Together, they traveled to the Drakemor Empire, where Elder Minglong and Yun Long personally blessed their union and oversaw their vows of marriage.

Then, by the very next morning, Rudra publicly declared Annasthasia both the Empress of the Solaris Empire and the woman who held his heart.

The aftermath of this was chaotic.

The old ancestors from both clans had fiercely opposed the marriage. They constantly spoke of incompatibility, using strange phrases such as "day and night never go together," "sun and moon are forbidden to meet," and "yin and yang are destined opposites."

Yet none of them had ever explained the true reason behind their objections. Most likely, even they themselves did not fully understand the matter of physiques and childbirth.

Rudra silently clenched his jaw.

Truthfully, even if he had known about this complication beforehand, he doubted he would have given up on Anna. At worst, he would have simply accepted a childless future or adopted a child from the orphanage without much worry.

And by the way, Anna looked at him now; she was feeling the same. If necessary, she thought that she probably would have searched for a second wife for him, and rather than leave his side, she would have been okay to become a concubine.

To both of them, their love had always felt stronger than everything else.

The entity suddenly coughed lightly, breaking them out of their thoughts.

"You see the problem, right!! Your child is rare; he has somehow inherited both of your physiques, but that is a problem more than a good trait."

Rudra and Anna immediately focused on her again.

"When your child awakens, both of these dormant physiques will wake up and clash for supremacy over each other, and until one wins and the other’s physique is destroyed, they won’t stop, and in the process, they will destroy your child; either he will lose his mind or die from strain."

The explanation caused Anna’s face to lose all color.

Her lips trembled violently as tears gathered once more in her eyes. Both she and Rudra instinctively placed their hands over her womb as though trying to protect the unborn child from fate itself.

Seeing their reaction, the entity continued more calmly.

"But now it will be alright."

Those words immediately made both of them look up.

"I have planted the seed of dormancy inside the budding heart of the child; every physique in this cosmos roots from the heart. The seed will prevent the clash of physiques by keeping one of the physiques dormant, saving your child from pain and death. It can’t be undone now anyway."

Relief instantly appeared on Anna’s face, though Rudra still looked concerned.

"Which physique did you suppress?" he asked.

"The mother’s physique," Samantha replied.

Rudra frowned immediately.

"Why not mine? Why did you suppress Anne’s? Mine is very destructive, and I am sure it will try to eat the seed of dormancy itself soon enough."

Samantha suddenly chuckled softly.

"For the problem you mentioned, if such a scenario happens, then just bring your boy to me; I will reinforce the seed every few years."

"And as for why I chose to suppress Mother’s inherited physique? You see, if that boy fails to awaken powers and traits from the father’s side, then some wretches in society will question his mother’s fidelity to her husband. Rudra, Anne has left her home for you; at least grant her some peace of mind."

Rudra immediately fell silent after hearing that. The conversation had turned in a direction he had not even considered.

He slowly turned toward Anna and held her hands firmly before speaking with complete seriousness.

"I don’t want my son to have no traits of his mother at all; that is not fair."

His eyes carried absolute certainty.

"He wouldn’t need to prove he is my son through looks or physique alone. The day he begins to shine, no one will dare question his origin. When he walks a path as high as mine and leaves his mark upon this world, everyone will know that only someone carrying the blood of Rudra could reach such heights."

A faint smile slowly appeared on Rudra’s face.

"I bet when people witness his deed, they would say, "Like father, like son."

Samantha clicked her tongue in amusement.

"You are a good man, Rudra, much better than many. Ok, I can do something about your concern. I can make his hair and eyes go after his mother despite having your physique that grants black hair and eyes. Is that okay with you?"

Rudra immediately nodded with a smile.

The moment Anna heard that, tears finally spilled from her eyes completely. Her gaze softened as she looked at Rudra with overwhelming affection.

She suddenly hugged him tightly.

"I would never regret having made that choice. I am so thankful for the past self that I ran away from my home for you!!!"

As soon as she finished speaking, she leaned forward and planted a deep kiss on Rudra’s lips.

"Urgh!!! Fledglings! I’ll take my leave."

The entity spoke in obvious annoyance before immediately relinquishing control over Samantha’s body.

The green particles filling the room gradually faded away.

A moment later, Samantha herself regained control.

Her head felt dizzy as she slowly opened her eyes.

The very first thing she saw was the couple in front of her sharing an extremely passionate kiss.

Her brows twitched instantly.

"Ugh!! What are you two doing?" she asked, completely out of context with everything that had just happened.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 327. William’s Revival - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Thunder rumbled across the skies of Dawnhold Province in the Holy Sun Empire as a violent storm swept over the imperial city. Rain lashed against the towering buildings while cold winds howled through the streets like restless spirits.

At one corner of the city stood an abandoned castle.

Its silver architecture still reflected faint flashes of lightning despite the years of neglect. Tall spires pierced into the stormy heavens, while elegant towers and marble halls spoke of a time when the place had once belonged to a flourishing and prestigious clan.

Now, however, the castle stood empty and deserted in the middle of the city, completely untouched by humans.

The Moon Clan that once occupied it had long since disappeared after its downfall nearly a decade ago. Dust, weeds, and silence had become the only residents remaining within its walls; even thieves did not try to barge inside, fearing they might be caught by the church and misunderstood as members of the moon clan.

Yet tonight, one of the descendants of that very clan had returned, though no one stopped her and the church was now destroyed, yet instead of pride, only grief could be seen on her face as she carried her dead son.

The massive doors of the main hall burst open violently.

Annasthasia stepped inside while carrying William’s lifeless body in her arms.

The storm roared behind her as cold wind rushed into the abandoned hall, disturbing years of settled dust. Overgrown weeds and creeping vines trembled beneath the force of the wind before silence returned once more.

Everything inside was dark.

Then, with a faint hum, a small sphere of energy appeared beside Anna and illuminated the massive hall with pale silver light.

Thunder echoed again behind her.

Tears had long streaked down Annasthasia’s moist face, yet her expression remained frighteningly cold and composed. She did not stop walking even once as she carried William toward the upper floors of the castle.

Behind her, three women followed silently.

Yue and Andrea supported Tamasya from both sides as the latter continued sobbing uncontrollably while being drenched with rainwater. Her entire body trembled with grief, and even breathing seemed difficult for her.

Despite their own fear and shock, Yue and Andrea kept glancing toward Anna with concern. The woman looked too calm.

Far too calm for a mother carrying her dead son.

Yet Anna continued forward without hesitation until they finally arrived at the highest spire of the castle.

The chamber at the top was wide and circular, lined with cracked crystal windows through which moonless stormlight occasionally flashed. At the center stood a large crystal platform covered in dust from years of abandonment.

Anna carefully laid William’s body upon it from his side; the massive sword pinned to him rested against the surface of the platform.

Then did she finally turn toward Tamasya, Yue, and Andrea.

"We have to take out this sword, but the problem is that it has passed through his mana core; we need to take the blade out without breaking the core any further."

Her tone remained steady and clinical, almost as though she were discussing a patient rather than her own son.

"What do you intend to do??" Andrea asked carefully, uncertain whether she even wanted to hear the answer.

Anna looked directly into her eyes.

"I will revive my son."

The declaration caused different reactions among the women.

Andrea and Yue stared at her in stunned disbelief.

Meanwhile, Tamasya simply shook her head weakly. She had checked William’s condition countless times already. His breathing had stopped. His heart had stopped. His life force was gone.

Revival sounded impossible.

"Tamasya, you will be holding his core in place using your superior control over shadow while Andrea and Yue will pull out the sword."

Tamasya barely reacted to the instructions. Her mind remained trapped in grief, and her quiet sobbing continued as though she had not even heard Anna’s words.

"TAMASYA!!"

Anna’s voice suddenly thundered through the chamber.

"GET YOURSELF TOGETHER!!! WILLIAM NEEDS YOU!!"

The shout shook Tamasya from her daze.

She slowly raised her head and looked toward Anna with hollow, lifeless eyes. For several long moments, neither of them spoke.

Then Tamasya silently stood up from the ground she had collapsed on.

Anna understood the answer hidden within that silence and gave a slight nod for everyone to begin.

Anna anchored William’s body firmly against the crystal platform using mana while Tamasya summoned strands of shadow energy around the damaged mana core, carefully holding it so it would not collapse further.

Yue and Andrea stepped toward the sword.

The moment their hands wrapped around the hilt, both women immediately felt a crushing pressure descend upon them. It was as though the sword itself carried the weight of a mountain.

Still, they gritted their teeth and pulled.

The blade did not move even slightly.

Frowning deeply, they gathered more strength and tried again with far greater force.

Nothing happened.

Minutes passed.

Both Yue and Andrea strained with everything they had, yet the sword remained embedded inside William’s body as though reality itself refused to let it budge.

Finally, both women turned toward Anna nervously.

"What should we do now? The sword won’t budge."

By now, sweat covered Annasthasia’s face.

Her mind raced desperately for a solution.

The goddess had clearly told them that removing the seal from William’s physique was the only way to save him, but she had never expected the sword itself to become an obstacle impossible to move.

Anna knew she was running out of time.

She believed she only had until the next sunrise before William’s soul would truly disappear forever.

If that happened, she would lose her sun completely.

"Remove the core as well!!"

The sudden suggestion came from Tamasya.

Her sobbing had finally stopped, but her tone remained cold. Her face had become emotionless, her eyes hard as frozen stone.

"Can someone even live without a mana core??" Yue asked immediately, horrified by the idea.

Before anyone else could answer, Andrea suddenly spoke.

"I think he has a spiritual core as well; even if he loses his mana core, he will still be able to use spiritual energy."

Anna’s eyes flickered rapidly as she thought through every possible outcome.

In the end, she slowly nodded.

Even if William became crippled for the rest of his life, she did not care anymore. She would protect him herself. She would remain beside him forever if necessary.

She only wanted her son alive.

Nothing else mattered at this point.

Seeing Anna’s agreement, Tamasya gave a silent nod.

Then she turned toward William’s pale face.

For the first time since entering the castle, the coldness in her expression cracked slightly. Her lips trembled painfully as she wrapped one arm around his shoulders and rested her forehead briefly against his chest.

"I am sorry."

The apology sounded utterly broken.

Then, without hesitation, one of her hands gripped the sword’s blade directly.

With terrifying force, she pulled it out.

Squelch

A horrifying sound echoed through the chamber. William’s mana core was violently ripped out together with the sword.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 328. William’s Revival - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A horrifying sound echoed through the chamber. William’s mana core was violently ripped out, together with the sword.

The once beautiful white crystal core, now dim and cracked, emerged from his abdomen, impaled through the center by the blade. The sword had pierced directly through it so cleanly that the core looked moments away from splitting into two pieces entirely.

Blood, flesh, and torn veins splattered across the crystal platform.

The scene looked like a living being’s very heart was being carved out, the removal of the source that defined life, power, and existence itself.

Anna immediately laid William flat against the platform again before preparing for the next step, which was to remove the seal.

"What will you do??" Tamasya asked quietly.

Anna’s hands trembled uncontrollably now.

"I...I will have to remove the seal from his heart." As she spoke, she slowly took out a small dagger.

Using mana, she desperately attempted to prevent further blood loss, though William’s body now contained a horrifying open wound that none of them knew could even heal properly anymore.

Yue and Andrea remained deeply shaken.

They quickly stored away both the sword and the damaged core before moving closer to assist Anna. Even now, fear lingered inside them. Some of them worried that Anna was only mutilating William’s body further out of desperation.

Still, none of them stopped her. Anna carefully made a cut near William’s chest and flesh. As she did that every few seconds, her hands shook violently.

Tamasya noticed it immediately but remained silent. Inside her heart, frustration and rage burned endlessly, seeing the helplessness they were in.

William had sent all of them away into the domain to protect them while he alone faced Lux. The memory made her clench her fists tightly.

She was angry at him. Angry that he chose to bear everything alone.

Angry that he left them behind. If only he were awake right now, she would have screamed all of that directly into his face.

From the corner of her eyes, she suddenly noticed Anna collapsing onto her knees.

The woman’s breathing had become erratic. Her widened eyes were filled with terror.

Only now, after everything, had the true despair of losing her son finally caught up to her.

Anna bit her lips harshly, trying to steady herself, but her hands refused to obey.

Finally, she looked helplessly toward Yue and Andrea and began explaining exactly what needed to be done. Both women stepped forward without complaint.

Yet the moment they picked up the dagger and approached William’s corpse, their courage faltered completely.

The gaping hole in his stomach. The opened chest cavity.

The sheer brutality of the scene in front of them broke them down.

They had killed before. They had seen gore and flesh countless times.

But this was different; this time the flesh belonged to their known, their nephew, and that made their blood run cold. They felt blasphemous touching the corpse that was supposed to be respected.

Even if there was truth in Anne’s assumptions, one wrong movement inside his heart would destroy whatever tiny chance of revival remained.

Neither of them dared to gamble with that small chance.

Their hands trembled beneath the weight of the responsibility.

Tamasya finally lost her patience as she looked at their faces.

Without another word, she walked forward and snatched the dagger from them.

Then she began the procedure herself.

The blade sliced through William’s chest until his motionless heart became visible beneath layers of torn flesh.

Somehow, Tamasya’s hands remained steady throughout the process. She felt as though she no longer had any choice but to continue this until she had a clear answer; she was desperate to hear William’s voice again.

Carefully, she cut into the heart that had now stopped beating.

Under the sharp cut of the dagger, the flesh parted cleanly beneath the heart’s walls.

Inside the organ rested a small green jade stone embedded like the seed of a tree; several vessels and tubes connected themselves to this seed.

Tamasya immediately summoned wisps of shadow energy that slithered into William’s chest and wrapped around the crystal.

"I am breaking it."

Shadow’s voice echoed inside her mind.

The next instant, the green jade seal shattered into dust. The shadow wisps retreated immediately afterward.

Tamasya took a deep breath as she took her hand out of William’s chest cavity. She looked at her bloodied hands with a hardened yet restrained expression as tears threatened to burst from her eyes.

Suddenly, without warning, a blinding eruption of energy exploded outward.

CRASH

The force sent all four women flying several feet backward from William’s body.

"Urghh!!" Tamasya’s body crashed into the wall of the room.

Anna groaned painfully as she forced herself upright again; her eyes remained locked onto the crystal platform. Meanwhile, Yue and Andrea, who had been looking after Anne, were thrown back with immense force.

William’s body had begun radiating an overwhelming light into the stormy night.

Then, suddenly, the storm above the Holy Sun Empire stopped completely, as if compelled by the light itself.

The roaring winds vanished. The dark clouds parted as though torn open by unseen hands, revealing a massive full moon hanging in the sky.

Moonlight poured down through the shattered windows and bathed William’s body in silver radiance.

Slowly, in front of Anne, Tamasya, Andrea, and Yue, his body slowly rose from the crystal platform and levitated into the air.

His expression remained calm and peaceful, and his breath remained dormant with no signs of revival, standing in terrifying contrast to the blood covering his chest and the trembling crimson hands of Tamasya below.

The four women stared upward in complete shock.

Slowly, within their shattered hearts, a fragile spark of hope began to return.

Perhaps...

William could still come back. Under this hope, they kept on witnessing the otherworldly phenomenon, but soon William’s body rose to an untrackable height in the sky and disappeared.

Tamasya, along with the other, finally took to the sky and followed behind him. But they could not find him anymore, as if the boy had disappeared from this world completely.

A bad premonition rose in their hearts, and they frantically started searching for him.

Meanwhile, William’s body was floating quite a few miles above the ground, and the full moon was directly facing the dead boy.

Under the radiant moonlight, William’s heart stitched back together and started healing. Although the rest of the injuries remained even then.

The figure of the moon in the sky shook violently for a few moments when something happened, something that the whole of Aris would remember forever.

From another corner of the land, the sun suddenly rose while the moon had not set. The light of these two celestial bodies was mixed; the sun was glowing only faintly, yet its brilliance was not ignorable.

William’s body slowly started regenerating under the sun’s light while the moonlight did its own work.

From far away in the sky, Miss Fate looked at the unfolding phenomena with a subtle smile.

"The sun and the moon have come together to tend to his injuries."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 329. Kang - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

No Man’s Land...

A small wooden cart slowly moved through the dense forest; its old wheels were creaking softly as they rolled over uneven ground. A lone steed pulled the cart forward at an unhurried pace while a cold wind whispered through the trees overhead.

The forest around them was unnaturally silent.

No birds chirped among the branches, and no beasts stirred within the undergrowth. The stillness in the environment carried an oppressive feeling, as though the entire forest were watching the travelers pass through it.

Inside the cart, several figures sat quietly beneath dark hooded robes that concealed their appearance and identities. None of them spoke. Their bodies remained motionless in meditative postures as they silently recovered their strength and mana.

Only the faint rattling of the cart disturbed the silence.

Then, suddenly, a sound was heard.

Swish!

An arrow tore through the air and landed directly in front of the cart.

The horse immediately halted with a startled snort.

The atmosphere changed, and several footsteps soon echoed through the forest from all directions as rough-looking figures emerged from the shadows between the trees. Their appearances were unkempt, their clothes were ragged, and their weapons were crude and poorly maintained.

The group consisted of several races.

Humans, elves, dwarves, titans, and vampires stood together among the bandits. Only dragons and phoenixes were absent from the gathering; these two races were never involved in acts they considered lowly.

At their front stood a bald vampire man with broad shoulders and crimson eyes. His rough face carried the confidence of someone accustomed to violence and robbery.

The group numbered several dozen in total.

One glance at their expressions was enough to reveal their intentions.

They were bandits.

The vampire leader stepped forward while resting a heavy machete over his shoulder.

"Choose!! Hand over all your belongings or try to resist and end up leaving your heads as well," he threatened the travelers in front of him.

Despite the threat, no response came from the cart.

The hooded driver remained completely silent; his face remained hidden beneath dark cloth. Even the figures sitting inside the carriage did not react.

The eerie calmness unsettled several of the bandits. Then, from inside the cart, a calm voice finally sounded.

"My turn."

The sound of shifting cloth followed as one of the figures stepped out from the carriage.

A young man slowly walked around the cart and stopped before the bandits. He reached up calmly and lowered his hood.

Sea-green hair fell neatly around his face, contrasting sharply against his bright blond eyes. His appearance was clean and refined, lacking even a single visible scar. Rather than a wandering traveler, he looked more like a noble youth who had accidentally wandered into dangerous territory.

The bald vampire immediately burst into laughter.

"Oh, a human noble brat!! It seems like we are in for a haul today."

Several bandits behind him laughed as well.

In response, the young man calmly summoned a finely crafted sword into his hand. The sheath was made of rich velvety material, and jewels on it gleamed faintly beneath the dim forest light.

With one smooth motion, he unsheathed it completely and lowered himself into a proper fighting stance.

The bandit leader scoffed loudly.

"So you choose the second option."

Around him, the rest of the bandits raised their weapons threateningly while the vampire himself pointed his machete toward the young swordsman.

Their confidence lasted only a few seconds.

Then the young man released his aura.

A powerful B-ranked pressure exploded outward from his body as the fire elemental mana erupted around him like a living inferno; the blade in his hand was covered in flames.

The smile on the vampire’s face vanished instantly.

Only he himself had reached B-rank among the bandits. Everyone else in his group was C-rank or below.

Still, after a brief moment of hesitation, he forced himself to stay calm.

They held the advantage in numbers, and this boy was likely the strongest among the travelers.

The vampire gritted his teeth and unleashed his own aura to resist the pressure.

Blood-red energy surged around him.

"Bloody bell of paralysis."

The vampire leader immediately cast his spell while smashing several glass bottles filled with beast blood against the ground.

Unlike high-ranked vampires, low-level vampires could not manipulate the blood flowing within living enemies directly. Instead, they relied on external blood sources such as dead beasts or sacrificed victims to cast blood techniques.

The spilled blood rose into the air unnaturally.

Moments later, it condensed into a massive crimson bell floating above the battlefield.

The bell rang loudly.

[Ding!]

[Ding!!]

The eerie sound waves spread toward the young swordsman.

The vampire burst into cruel laughter.

"Hahaha! "Bitch, this spell will paralyze you for a minute. Now I just need to walk over and finish you off."

Confident in his technique, he gestured toward one of his subordinates.

An elf assassin with darkness affinity immediately nodded and vanished from sight.

The next instant, the assassin reappeared beside the young swordsman with a dagger aimed directly toward his neck.

Meanwhile, the bandit leader shifted his gaze toward the silent, robed figures inside the carriage.

None of them had reacted even now.

Since he could not sense their cultivation at all, he assumed they were merely weak travelers frozen in fear of bandits, and this noble was the strongest among them.

Slash.

A severed head suddenly hit the ground.

The entire forest fell silent.

The bandits stared in shock at the rolling head of the elf assassin.

Before anyone could react, the young blond swordsman calmly flicked his blade once, scattering blood from its edge.

The blood bell floating overhead shattered apart the very next second.

A single sword slash had dispersed the spell completely.

"You!!!"

The vampire leader’s eyes widened in disbelief.

But before he could even process what had happened, more heads began falling.

The young swordsman moved through the battlefield like flowing fire itself. His flame-infused blade carved cleanly through necks with terrifying precision. Every movement was efficient and controlled without a single wasted motion.

One bandit after another collapsed before they even understood what was happening.

Panic immediately spread among the remaining survivors.

The vampire leader’s face twisted with rage as he rushed forward and intercepted the young man before the rest of his subordinates could be slaughtered.

"Who are you!!"

The vampire roared furiously.

"State your name before dying by my hands!!"

The young man stopped briefly and looked directly into the bald vampire’s crimson eyes.

His expression remained cold and emotionless.

"Just a puny human."

The moment he finished speaking, mana surged violently around him.

"Ember Bloom."

The whispered words triggered the spell instantly.

A massive burning rose erupted beneath the feet of the vampire leader and the surrounding bandits. Towering petals made entirely of fire bloomed outward in all directions, swallowing the battlefield within blazing heat.

The weaker bandits died almost immediately; their screams disappeared within the flames.

The vampire leader himself grimaced in agony as the fire consumed his body.

"Spell mastery at the level of a Grandmaster. Fuck, where did you guys find this guy!!"

The voice came from inside the carriage where the three robed figures sat calmly.

One of them finally removed his hood, revealing the face of Desmond Klembred, the dwarf prince.

Beside him, another figure lowered his hood as well, revealing Galeion Skybreak.

"I don’t know; ask Maximus and Ethan. They brought that human with us. What was his name again?"

A third man finally removed his hood.

It was Ethan.

"Alfred Draconis, heir of the Draconis house."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 330. Kang - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Ethan told the two of them without pause.

"Though I did not remember him being this impressive, I have only achieved the level of a master with one of my spells recently. I can’t believe someone from the B class rose to that level so soon."

Desmond stared at the battlefield outside the carriage with visible disbelief in his eyes while listening to Ethan’s words. The flames created by Alfred’s spell still burned across the forest floor, illuminating the corpses of the fallen bandits.

The dwarf prince then turned his attention toward the driver of the cart.

At the front, Maximus sat quietly with the reins in his hands; his hood was partially lowered now. During the journey, all of them had taken turns either driving the carriage or handling the dangers along the road.

Traveling through No Man’s Land was never safe, and everyone had long since accepted the possibility of being attacked at any moment.

Now all three young men inside the carriage looked toward Maximus, waiting for an explanation.

Maximus shook his head slowly.

"We recently met during a banquet; this guy said that he wanted to grow strong and then challenged me to a duel."

He paused briefly before continuing.

"I defeated him, but his persistence is so strong. I remember that he was nothing like that before getting kidnapped by the Clayman cult."

"So he was the one from the academy who got kidnapped??" Desmond asked.

Maximus nodded.

"He has changed completely after that; even after that banquet, he has been challenging me continuously, and to be honest, his growth has been astonishing. He is improving at a monstrous rate, as if a switch was flipped inside him during the past incident."

Maximus glanced toward Alfred, who was calmly wiping blood from his blade outside.

"He is a descendant of the Duke of Draconis and also considered talented from the start, so in the beginning, I was not surprised much."

"But slowly, I realized that his temperament had also changed. Look how effortlessly he is cleaving heads and making decisions with a cool mind; I truly believe that something has changed within him."

His words caused everyone inside the carriage to grow thoughtful.

"Near-death experiences change people," Ethan spoke quietly, his tone noticeably lower than before.

The atmosphere inside the carriage became heavy for a brief moment.

Then suddenly "Argghhhhh!!!"

The furious scream of the surviving bandit leader drew everyone’s attention again.

The heavily injured vampire charged directly toward the cart in desperation. Blood covered most of his body, and severe burns from Alfred’s flames blackened his skin.

Before anyone could react, the bandit grabbed Maximus from behind and pressed a blade tightly against his neck.

"Stay away, or I will kill this guy."

His voice trembled with panic and rage.

Alfred looked at the scene calmly without even the slightest trace of concern appearing on his face.

"Sigh!! I wanted to take my time killing you, but it seems you were really in a hurry to die."

As he spoke, he slowly sheathed his sword. The bandit leader frowned in confusion.

Before he could understand what was happening, his head exploded under the force of Maximus’s punch that he launched from the back of his hand.

Bone fragments, blood, and flesh erupted outward like a volcano before the lifeless body collapsed beside the carriage.

Silence followed immediately afterward.

Maximus remained completely unharmed.

After all, he had already advanced into A-rank recently. A wounded B-rank bandit threatening him was no different from a child holding a knife.

Meanwhile, the rest of the group had advanced significantly as well during recent months. Ethan and Galeion had both stepped into S-rank, while Desmond stood at A-rank alongside Maximus.

Alfred calmly walked back toward the carriage with his sword still in hand.

Once he arrived before Maximus, he cupped his fists respectfully and bowed slightly.

"Thanks for your assistance, senior brother Maximus."

Maximus’s expression immediately twisted in irritation.

"What the hell are you saying? I am not your senior; call me Maximus, and that greeting I have never seen before. Can you just be normal!!"

He rubbed his forehead tiredly while speaking.

Alfred’s behavior genuinely exhausted him sometimes. The young man constantly forgot noble etiquette and insisted on calling him "senior brother" in the strangest situations possible.

"You are greater than me in terms of strength, so it’s only natural I call you ’senior.’ When I surpass you, I will call you ’junior brother.’"

Maximus took a deep breath to suppress the sudden urge to punch him in the face.

Eventually, he simply gave up trying to argue.

Inside the carriage, Ethan, Galeion, and Desmond exchanged strange looks with one another. They had all noticed Alfred’s unusual behavior by now.

Not only had his strength grown rapidly, but even the way he spoke and carried himself often felt strangely foreign, though it was much more refined than theirs.

Still, none of them pressed the issue further. Soon, the topic shifted elsewhere.

"How much distance is left?" Galeion asked.

Ethan unfolded the old map resting on his lap and studied it carefully beneath the dim light entering the carriage.

"About 1 day."

The map was the sole reason this entire journey had begun.

Recently, Ethan had stumbled upon an ancient-looking map while visiting a black market auction. Rather than blindly trusting it, he had carefully torn away a small section of the parchment and taken it to an appraiser.

His reasoning had been simple.

If the parchment turned out to be recent, then the map was likely fake. But if it truly originated from an ancient era as the auctioneer claimed, then the inheritance marked upon it might actually exist.

To his surprise, the appraiser had informed him that the parchment was nearly 10,000 years old.

The moment Ethan heard that, his instincts immediately told him this opportunity was worth investigating. Ancient inheritances generally contained strong spells and techniques, and a lot of treasures that could help one elevate their strength.

But still, he did not get arrogant; he knew that such places were meant to be dangerous, which is why he needed people with him.

Initially, he had planned to go along with just Maximus, but then he decided to tell all his influential friends like Desmond, Klara, Galeion, and others.

Ethan knew that when heirs of emperors moved, they were tailed by several high-ranking experts who ensured their safety. Ethan was counting on these experts to protect them in case any life-threatening situation happened.

With that intention, he had sent letters to Galeion, Kara, Desmond, Kathrine, Seraphine, and even William, since William had some solid backing too, according to what he had heard from rumours.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 331. Kang - 3 (Rebirth)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Still, Ethan had, from the start, remained cautious about the authenticity of the map as well.

That was why he had invited Desmond and Galeion to help verify the map further. After getting it examined through their own sources, both of them found out that this map must be the real deal and eventually decided to accompany him on the expedition.

Meanwhile, Ethan’s relationship with Maximus had also deepened considerably after the latter finally opened up about his past and his desire to regain Lia a few months back.

Both of them had promised William to become strong enough to deserve his assistance and to be good enough to assist him as well.

Ethan understood that hunger for strength well enough. That was why he had invited Maximus to join them as well.

Meanwhile, the girls had their own plans, and William was nowhere to be found, which Ethan, Max, and Desmond had some hint about why, but they did not talk about it much on this trip and decided to focus on the ruin exploration.

At the end of his preparations, only Deson and Galeion had tagged along; Ethan was sure there were experts from both of their factions tailing them at all times, and this development reassured Ethan. Meanwhile, on the surface, he acted ignorant, pretending to be a hot-blooded young man who was excited to go on an adventure.

As for convincing Klaus, it had been quite easy to get permission from him; in fact, he had sent him away a bit too enthusiastically.

There was also a trade deal going on with some high noble; maybe his master was excited about that.

Desmond was not restricted much by his father since the dwarven emperor had secluded himself in his smithy, researching some new material.

Maximus’s movements were not restricted much anyway, especially after his elder brother Edward fell sick and developed a minor mental problem. Almost everyone is focused on him giving Maximus a worry-free opportunity to leave on an adventure.

The only unexpected addition to the group had been Alfred.

Initially, everyone had hesitated to allow him to accompany them. Yet somehow, through persistence, strange logic, and surprisingly convincing words, Alfred had managed to force his way into the expedition.

That incident alone had made Ethan realize just how valuable persuasive ability could sometimes be, though it wasn’t like he did not welcome the idea of him tagging along; after all, he was the heir of a duke, so surely there would be experts sent for him, too.

A while later, Alfred finished thoroughly looting every corpse left behind by the bandits before finally climbing into the back of the carriage.

The moment he sat down, everyone stared at him strangely.

Desmond’s face twitched slightly before he finally voiced the question lingering in his mind.

"Are you sure you are the son of a duke?? And that too, someone as rich as Draconis??"

The dwarf genuinely struggled to believe it.

He had never seen a noble heir behave so greedily before, especially one willing to strip dead bandits of even the smallest valuables.

Alfred answered without embarrassment.

"I am robbing them just like they robbed many innocents; this is my way of ensuring justice!!"

Desmond’s expression twitched even harder after hearing such shameless reasoning.

Eventually, no one bothered responding anymore.

Silence gradually settled over the carriage once again as everyone returned to meditation or rest.

Meanwhile, Alfred leaned quietly against the wooden wall of the cart while observing the sword resting across his lap.

In truth, Alfred Draconis had died nearly six months ago during the incident involving the Clayman Cult.

The one currently occupying this body was Kang.

In his previous life, he had been known across the world as Mystic Kang, one of the strongest beings of an ancient era.

Back then, the Aris Continent was not even called Aris, and it had not been separated from the major landmasses of the scarred lands.

It had been a completely different age.

The demons had only recently appeared during his time, and humanity had crushed them repeatedly beneath overwhelming force. The other races of the world existed separately and constantly fought one another for dominance.

Alliances between different races had been almost unimaginable.

That was why, after awakening 50,000 years into the future inside the body of a young noble, Kang had experienced endless confusion.

Even now, he still found it difficult to fully understand the current dynamics of the world.

Why were all the races united?

What was the point?

As far as Kang remembered, demons were little more than pests that could easily be exterminated.

So why had alliances against them become necessary?

Those questions constantly lingered in his mind.

Still, he had carefully controlled himself and avoided asking anything suspicious that might expose him.

However, the biggest shock came later.

The natives of this scarred world had actually betrayed their own races and formed cults that worshipped demon gods.

The very same demon gods Kang once forced to sweep the floors of his palace.

Every time he thought about it, frustration welled inside him.

How had the world degraded to this extent?

After awakening fifty thousand years later, all he could find were weakened descendants carrying mere traces of the former glory of their races.

Still, Kang believed there was a reason he had been brought into this era.

He believed the Supreme Mother had sent him here with a purpose.

To unite the Scarred lands again.

To establish dominance over the demonic race once more and also elevate humanity’s level to its former glory.

And perhaps most importantly...

To fix the utterly absurd and stupid cultivation structure of this era.

Kang’s expression darkened slightly at the thought.

Some fool had apparently named the third cultivation stage as the "Godhood Realm."

Every time Kang remembered that, he cursed the unknown individual internally.

The amount of exaggeration and gaslighting involved was unbearable, in his opinion.

As the carriage continued moving through the silent forest, Kang slowly shifted his gaze toward Ethan’s figure.

"Heaven’s blessed," he murmured.

Then his eyes moved toward Maximus.

"Heaven’s forsaken."

A faint, amused smile appeared on Kang’s face.

For heaven’s blessed and heaven’s forsaken to travel together as comrades...

Even after 50,000 years of life experience, Kang still found the situation absurdly odd and amusing.

He leaned back against the cart and shook his head lightly.

"What a day to be alive!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 332. Sun - Moon heavenly confrontation.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

William’s body floated several miles above the ground beneath the vast night sky. The full moon hung directly above him, enormous and radiant, as though the heavens themselves had fixed their gaze upon the dead boy.

Silver moonlight poured over his motionless body.

Under that strange celestial radiance, the torn flesh around William’s heart slowly began to stitch itself back together. The damage to the organ healed gradually, thread by thread, as though invisible hands were mending it carefully.

However, the rest of the wounds across his body remained. The massive injuries around his abdomen and chest showed little change for the moment.

Then suddenly, the full moon in the sky trembled.

For several brief moments, the glowing celestial body in the sky shook violently.

And then something the entire world of Aris would remember forever.

From another edge of the horizon, sunlight suddenly emerged even though the moon had not yet set. Dawn appeared in the middle of the night itself.

The sun slowly rose into the sky while the full moon continued hanging opposite it.

The light of both celestial bodies mixed across the heavens.

The sun was dim compared to its usual brilliance, as though it had risen before its proper time, yet its golden radiance was still impossible to ignore.

Moonlight and sunlight intertwined around William’s floating body, bathing him in two opposite energies simultaneously.

Under the influence of the moonlight and sunlight working together, the rest of his shattered body slowly began regenerating.

His muscles reformed and bones repaired themselves. Torn veins and flesh stitched together with miraculous speed.

Gradually, a cocoon of radiant energy formed around William. Silver and gold lights wrapped around him together until his entire body disappeared within the glowing shell.

Far below, Tamasya floated several miles upward until she finally arrived close enough to witness the phenomenon clearly.

She stared at William’s figure in complete silence.

To her, it looked as though the heavens themselves were baptizing him beneath the combined light of the sun and moon.

The sight was so surreal and divine that even breathing felt difficult.

Never in her life had she imagined she would witness something so otherworldly with her own eyes.

And she was not the only one.

Across the entire continent, people awakened from their sleep.

Whether emperor or beggar, noble or commoner, cultivator or ordinary mortal, everyone looked toward the impossible sight unfolding in the sky.

The entire world had come alive.

In the distant No Man’s Land, Ethan and his companions had set up camp and fallen asleep inside their tents after a long day of travel.

Suddenly, Ethan’s eyes opened.

The cloth ceiling of the tent glowed brightly as though morning had arrived.

Frowning slightly, he stepped outside. The moment he looked upward, he froze.

The sun had risen halfway into the sky despite the night still existing. Meanwhile, the moon appeared far larger than normal, dominating the sky with an overwhelming presence.

Ethan stared at the phenomenon quietly before murmuring to himself, "It’s the second time I have seen the sun rising during the night."

"Can you tell me about the first instance?" A sudden familiar voice of a boy came from nearby.

Ethan turned and saw Alfred standing beside the camp while holding a sword in his hand. His upper body was bare, and sweat covered his skin as though he had been training throughout the night instead of sleeping.

Ethan observed him silently for a moment before giving a faint nod.

Meanwhile, far away in the Babylonian Empire, a massive palace carved directly into the peak of a mountain overlooked the sleeping lands below.

Sara Babylon stepped outside onto one of the stone balconies and silently watched the impossible sky.

The young lionkin’s golden eyes reflected both the moonlight and sunlight at once, making her think she was dreaming.

Behind her, another figure emerged quietly.

It was Inari Mistborn.

The nine-tailed foxkin stood with her hands behind her back while staring toward the heavens with widened eyes. Even someone like her could not remain unaffected by the phenomenon occurring tonight.

Slowly, Inari rested one hand on Sara’s shoulder.

"Times are about to change, Sara; we need to be prepared."

After speaking, she guided Sara back inside the palace.

Meanwhile, at the center of the phenomenon, Annasthasia, Yue, and Andrea arrived beside Tamasya and stared at the cocoon of energy surrounding William.

The aura radiating from it was overwhelming.

It felt sacred and untouchable.

Inside the cocoon, William’s unconscious body floated peacefully while countless invisible changes occurred within him.

Deep inside his unconscious mind, several notifications rang one after another.

[Ding!! The Celestial Moon Consort’s physique has awakened.]

[Ding!! Physique is detected to be the result of forced intervention, incompatible and unjustifiable to the host’s existence.]

[resolving incompatibility...]

[Ding!! The lunar goddess has observed the host.]

[Ding!! The lunar goddess has taken an interest in the host. Intervention has been detected.]

[Ding!! The lunar goddess’s touch has been blocked.]

[Ding!! Absolute adaptation is activating...]

[Ding!! The Celestial Moon Consort’s physique has turned into the Divine Lunar Sovereign’s physique.]

[Ding!! The lunar goddess has intervened again... Intervention has been allowed by Administrator Orion.]

[Ding!! The lunar goddess has placed the mark of the apostle on the host.]

[The host’s soul is being summoned to the Lunar realm.]

Almost immediately after the final notification sounded, the cocoon surrounding William shattered apart.

The radiant energy dispersed into the night sky.

William’s body suddenly began falling freely from the sky.

Tamasya reacted instantly.

She flashed upward and caught him before he could descend any further.

Holding him carefully in her arms, she immediately examined his condition.

The wounds across his body had healed completely.

Even the massive hole in his abdomen was gone without a trace.

His appearance somehow looked even more radiant and refined than before, as though the combined energies of the sun and moon had reshaped him entirely.

However, one noticeable difference remained.

A strange mark had appeared upon his forehead.

Tamasya frowned and rubbed the symbol repeatedly, attempting to wipe it away or at least understand what it was, but the mark remained completely unmoved.

Soon, Annasthasia arrived beside them.

The moment she saw William’s healed body, hope immediately filled her eyes again. She quickly moved closer and tried sensing his breathing.

Nothing.

Not even the faintest breath escaped his lips.

He was not breathing.

Tamasya realized it at the same moment.

Slowly, she turned toward Anna with widened eyes.

No words needed to be spoken anymore.

The crushing despair returned once again.

Tamasya stared blankly at William’s peaceful face, trying desperately to understand what she was seeing. Then her gaze slowly drifted upward toward the sky, where the sun and moon still faced one another silently.

The four women remained there for several long minutes.

Clouds drifted around them quietly while an unbearable silence filled the air.

Then suddenly, Yue’s eyes widened faintly.

A vision had struck her unexpectedly.

It was not something she consciously attempted to see. The revelation simply arrived inside her mind without warning.

"He will not die."

Yue spoke softly.

But nobody paid attention to her words.

She looked toward Annasthasia’s grief-stricken expression and Tamasya’s hollow eyes before deciding not to explain further.

William was fateless.

Only now it hit her that a fateless would not die so easily if he were being treated so specially by that mysterious woman who is the master of her master.

That conviction had appeared within her the moment the vision ended.

The vision had been brief. She had only seen William opening his eyes.

She did not know the time, location, or date of this happening.

Only the sight of him waking up.

Eventually, Tamasya slowly descended back toward the highest spire of the Moon Clan’s castle together with Annasthasia and Andrea.

Meanwhile, Yue remained floating beneath the sky for several moments longer, silently staring at the sun and moon confronting one another before finally following behind them.

Twenty hours later...

William’s body lay motionless upon the crystal platform once again.

Tamasya sat beside it with her head resting near the edge of the platform while staring blankly at his face. Her eyes had long since become hollow from grief and exhaustion.

Annasthasia sat nearby while gently holding William’s head in her lap.

For nearly twenty hours, she had remained there continuously stroking his hair and face without moving away even once.

Occasionally, tears would fall from her eyes onto William’s calm face.

Those tears carried endless regret, grief, and helplessness.

Andrea and Yue stood farther away in silence.

Both women were heartbroken as well, but the pain inside Annasthasia and Tamasya was so overwhelming that neither of them even dared make unnecessary noise.

After all, both women had lost an irreplaceable part of their lives.

Andrea silently pursed her lips while Yue repeatedly attempted to divine William’s future over and over again.

Every attempt of hers failed.

It was as though fate itself refused to show her anything regarding him.

Then, finally, the heavenly phenomenon in the sky began to end.

The moon disappeared along with the remaining night.

A fresh morning sun rose naturally into the heavens, almost as though the celestial bodies had finally corrected their paths.

Yet despite the beautiful sunrise outside, the atmosphere inside the Moon Castle remained unbearably gloomy.

Another tear slipped from Annasthasia’s eyes and landed softly upon William’s face.

This time, however, something changed as William’s face twitched slightly under the hot drops of tears.

Anne immediately froze.

Before she could even process what she had seen, a deep sound emerged from William’s chest.

Then suddenly, he inhaled a large, deep breath that entered his lungs automatically.

The sound echoed clearly through the silent chamber.

Tamasya instantly stood up in shock and stared toward William’s body.

He was alive.

Yet completely unaware of the chaos he had caused, William merely shifted slightly on the crystal platform before turning comfortably to the other side.

Then, with an utterly peaceful expression, he buried himself deeper into sleep.

After all...

His mother’s lap was the most comfortable pillow he could ever have.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 333. Nir - goddess of moon
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

faint rustling sounds

Ssssss..

William’s eyes snapped open as he took deep breaths filled with relief that he was alive; the last thing he remembered was getting stabbed in the back by Lux.

His body felt so weak and refused to get up, as if his back were pinned to the cold floor. His mind felt hazy as he remembered the last moment he spent laughing at his situation.

He had felt it; he had felt weak.

William’s hands clenched as he gritted his teeth. "How could I be this careless..."

How could he forget that he had lost his family because he was weak once? How could he not prepare for more? How could he remain pathetically weak?

The reason he had died laughing was not that the situation was absurd, but because, even after having something as absurd as the 100x returns system, he had failed even to stand a chance against someone like Lux.

Even though he was only 16 years old, he still felt that he needed to raise his standards; it was just a feeling, but he knew that confronting the gods would have been inevitable.

He was truly a fool through and through; he did not even know how to use such tools for his advantage. What a pathetic way to die!!.

[You are not entirely a fool; you were just mid.] Orion’s voice sounded.

"Huh?? You are still here??" William asked Orion with a surprised expression.

[Well, yeah, you are not dead yet, so unfortunately, yes.]

"Fucker!! You should be thankful you are with me."

[What are you going to do, ass?]

"F...!!!"

"Oh, our baby has woken up!!" Suddenly, a soothing feminine voice sounded in William’s right ear, taking him off guard.

He quickly turned around only to see a lady lying beside him resting her head over her folded hand, looking at him with a serene smile. She chuckled as she looked at the change in expression on his face.

A weird thing in this situation was that this lady had a strip of silk tied around her eyes, and that was supposed to obstruct her vision, yet William felt her gaze all over his body. He almost freaked out looking at this lady lying down so close to him; he almost screamed the names of Tamsya and Seraphine for help.

He had not even sensed her here before she had spoken up; moreover, her scent and her dress were too irresistible for him, and he hated this biological reaction in his body.

She was wearing midnight blue robes adorned with nets and fabric woven out of pure silver; her lips were cherry red, and her face was smooth. She wore a robe that revealed the curves she carried so gracefully. The high slit of her gown revealed the shape of her legs, making her look enticing for the hardiest of men.

"Fuck!!!" William’s teeth bit into his lips as he straightened his face and looked at the scene in front of him; it was a deep, starry sky as if he had traveled into outer space.

At first, he had not paid much attention to the dark expanse, but now, as he turned, he noticed the cloudy surface he was lying on with a light, chilly mist that was concentrated on the ground.

William immediately tried to stand up, but his back seemed unalive.

"Oh my dear, you want to stand up!!" The lady spoke in a mature voice that sounded like a blessing to William’s ears; he felt his mind calm down and exhaustion disappear.

William’s breathing calmed down, and he looked at the lady in blue silvery robes coming closer before her hands lightly waved over his face, leaving silvery glitters that landed on his skin.

The lady waved her hand over his chest, abdomen, and legs, and as soon as the silvery energy touched his skin, life returned to him; he felt his control back.

Slowly and steadily, he sat up and looked around the realm he was in.

’Beautiful’ was an understatement; this realm was literal imagery of what one would call a sanctuary. It was a vast, plain space with mist gathered over the floor, covering it whole. In front of him was a massive throne, and behind the throne was a miniature figure of the moon itself.

The lady who had been lurking around him moments ago floated towards the throne and sat on it lazily; her pristine white legs hung around the left armrest while her head rested over the right one, and her hair hung by the edge.

She moved her smooth legs to and fro like a child with a pout on her face and the side gaze with which she looked at William’s silent figure as if he were not even witnessing her antics.

Her behavior did not match the otherworldly face and mature figure she possessed.

"Who are you??" William asked, although he had some idea as he looked at her crown decorated with a silvery crescent moon.

The moon’s projection behind her could only mean she was related to the moon god, or she herself was the moon goddess.

Listening to William’s question, the whimsical goddess straightened on the throne and coughed lightly before a serious expression was etched on her face; her hands came to rest over the armrest of the throne, and her legs joined together to make her posture look elegant and charming, unlike before.

"William!!" she addressed him by the name, not as ’mortal’ or ’human’ or by any lowly names that William was used to from beings of such a level.

"My name is Nir; I am the goddess of the moon," she spoke, now looking at him directly.

"I will be direct with my intentions," Nir spoke.

"I have had my eyes on you since the day you were born," she spoke, making Williams’ eyes widen.

"Orion."

[She doesn’t know what she is not supposed to know, although yes, she has been keeping an eye on you from the start.] Orion answered instantly, making William sigh in relief.
