"After I returned from death, Aunt Yue told me that it was Tamasya who removed the seal from my heart."

His gaze sharpened slightly.

"Think about that for a moment, Mom. Imagine what she must have gone through while cutting open the heart of the person she loved, knowing there was a chance she could lose him forever."

Tamasya’s breathing trembled faintly.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 354. Don’t play with fire! - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"I don’t love her just because she’s beautiful or charming," William continued firmly. "If that were the case, I would have fallen for her the moment we first met."

"I lived beside her for months. She taught me many things, and during that time we truly came to understand each other."

"At first, I never intended to make her mine. But as time passed, I realized that we share a very similar nature."

William looked directly at Tamasya now.

"Tamasya cares deeply for her people and those close to her. When I realized that, I began respecting her more and more because I’m the same."

Then he looked back toward Anastasia.

"You know me, Mom."

"Tamasya and I met each other during the loneliest periods of our lives."

His expression turned distant for a moment as old memories resurfaced.

"I started running a sweet shop when I was twelve years old. I lived alone for two years while handling every problem myself, thugs, thieves, headaches, and everything that came my way."

A faint chuckle escaped him afterward.

"And when I first met Tamasya... she felt the same. having been isolated in the prison of gods alone for thousands of years."

"It was like looking into a reflection of myself, desperate for warmth and someone’s company."

"She was alone, too. Carrying every burden by herself."

William smiled faintly.

"When two lonely people begin living together, they naturally become each other’s support system. They find comfort in one another without even realizing it."

By now, both women standing before him had moist eyes.

The entire day had been emotionally exhausting for them. They had spent hours arguing like rivals while suppressing frustration, worry, embarrassment, and exhaustion.

William’s words became the final push that broke through their emotional walls completely.

"Mom," William continued gently, "I know you only want what’s best for me."

"And honestly, if you stop focusing so much on our age gap and old customs..."

He slowly looked toward Tamasya.

"...then you’ll realize that she’s one of the best women who could ever take care of your son."

Tamasya felt her heart shake violently.

She had never expected feelings this deep from William’s side.

If she were being honest, a small part of her had always believed that one of the reasons William confessed to her was because of her appearance. Shadow had often told her that her face and body would naturally attract men, so she had subconsciously assumed that physical attraction played a significant role in William’s feelings.

But now she realized just how wrong she had been.

Overwhelmed with emotion, she immediately stepped forward and raised her arms, wanting nothing more than to hug him and drown him in kisses.

But before she could embrace him again, William gently stopped her hands.

Even Anastasia looked surprised by that reaction, though she wisely chose to remain silent.

The irritation he had suppressed returned to William’s eyes once more. He looked at both women carefully before speaking again.

"I hope both of you understand something clearly now."

His voice turned colder.

"I did not enjoy tonight’s little test at all. You can’t treat me like a pushover just because you feel like I am younger or inexperienced enough to mind about these things."

"If you ladies get into an argument, sort that out yourselves. Take my help if needed, but never try to make me look like a fool like that. You cannot play with fire and expect not to get burned."

"I am a young man, and I do feel the urge to mate with my woman; you cannot just try to measure what I feel about her with the amount of lust I show, nor can you just use it like a medium to sort your own arguments; that is a serious way of undermining me here!!"

William spoke with a raised voice, making both women realize their faults. Then his gaze slowly lowered toward Tamasya.

The intensity inside his eyes made her instinctively lower her head slightly, unable to meet his stare anymore. The bold and teasing woman from earlier had completely disappeared.

"Mom," William said calmly, "if you ever send Tamasya into my room like that again..."

A dangerous smile slowly formed on his face. "...then I won’t let her leave for at least a week."

Tamasya’s face turned bright red instantly. Meanwhile, Anastasia visibly stiffened in alarm.

"So, unless you want grandchildren running around the palace soon, please don’t test my patience again. I am already tired of waiting for so long; don’t test my self-control anymore."

Afterward, William gently lifted Tamasya’s chin before leaning close to her ear. His cheek brushed lightly against hers as he whispered, enough that only she could hear.

"Don’t try something like that again unless you’re prepared for the consequences."

His voice became lower and more dangerous. "Next time, I’ll leave you unable to walk for a month."

Tamasya nearly stopped breathing.

"And for tonight’s offense..." he continued with a faint smirk, "...I’ll thoroughly punish you the next time you jump onto my lap like that."

His whisper turned softer still.

"I swear, Tammy, you’ll end up begging me to stop."

Before either woman could recover from those words, William snapped his fingers. In an instant, both Anastasia and Tamasya were teleported beyond the doorway through the Blink spell.

The doors slammed shut immediately afterward under the force of his spiritual energy. Tamasya hurried away with an utterly embarrassed expression, her face still burning red from her words.

Meanwhile, Anastasia stood outside for a few moments in stunned silence before slowly swallowing nervously. For the first time, she truly felt the overwhelming and unhinged maturity hidden beneath her son’s appearance.

Even so, part of her still believed he had been unnecessarily harsh and rude toward her.

In the end, she merely pouted to herself before walking away in the opposite direction from Tamasya, knowing fully well that the poor Tamasya would not mentally recover from this humiliation anytime soon.

Inside the room, William let out a long breath before grimacing.

"Sheesh... these women are too bad."

"I’m going to see my Sera. Only she can heal me now with her gentle voice."

The next instant, his figure disappeared into the Domain of the Infinite.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 355. A Gift - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

William sat alone beneath the maple tree within the Domain of Infinite, his back resting against the rough bark as scarlet leaves drifted lazily around him.

The endless sky above the domain remained motionless, carrying an unnatural silence that allowed his thoughts to settle more easily.

After arriving inside the domain earlier, he had called Seraphine to meet him.

The two of them had spent several peaceful days together here due to the time dilation, away from politics, responsibilities, and the endless chaos surrounding the sudden creation of the new eternal sun empire.

Eventually, however, William had convinced her to return and continue focusing on the awakening of her physique, which was in two weeks. He promised her that during her awakening, he would be by her side, although he never told Sera how William would manage to do that.

Seraphine had naturally resisted the idea of leaving him first, especially when he had told her about his temporary death; she had been scared, and William had to spend a full day calming her down.

She clearly wanted to remain beside him longer, especially after everything that had happened recently, but William had managed to persuade her to leave after a lengthy conversation.

During their time, he had also chosen to be completely honest with her regarding Tamasya. Surprisingly, Seraphine had handled the fact with quite ease; she even praised her for removing William’s seal.

She listened really patiently while he explained the complicated dynamic of his past, about his mom and dad and everything that William had learned in the past few days.

By the end, he had made an annoyed expression and told her about the recent instance where Tamasya had deliberately seduced him as part of Anastasia’s ridiculous "test"—even Seraphine became genuinely annoyed.

She had firmly scolded him afterward and warned him not to fall for such traps so easily.

At the same time, she had also stubbornly declared that she intended to be the first woman to share a bed with him. William honestly did not know how to respond to that statement.

Part of him remained skeptical, mostly because Seraphine was still young. Then again, technically speaking, he was not much older than her in this life either. The problem was that William could never truly see himself as a teenager anymore.

Mentally, he had long since stopped being one.

He simply could not afford the luxury of acting immature anymore.

Still, he could not deny that ever since the seal on his Lunar Sovereign Physique had been broken, his mental condition had improved drastically.

The difference was so obvious that it almost frightened him.

For years, there had always been a strange pressure weighing down on his mind. It felt like constant suffocation lingering beneath the surface of his thoughts, filling him with confusion, irrational frustration, anxiety, and emotional instability. At times, he would lose control over his emotions for reasons he could not properly understand.

Now that burden was gone.

Orion had already explained that the strange impulses, uncontrolled fury, and the maddening voice urging him to break the seal would never trouble him again.

Only now did William fully realize the truth.

The suppressed lunar physique had been influencing him after his awakening. Because the physique remained sealed while simultaneously trying to awaken, it had destabilized his emotions and mental state over the past two years.

The lunar element was related to emotions, so that was a clear reason William had been emotionally conflicted many times in the past.

As the seal disappeared, William finally felt mentally whole and clear-headed again. Though his hate for demons didn’t subside one bit, and that was what he wanted, in fact.

When he reflected upon the past honestly, he realized that if his mind had been this stable from the beginning, then he would never have fallen into such confusion regarding matters of love and attachment. Nor would he have spent so much time emotionally distracted during the last two years, or maybe he was just blaming everything on the deal now.

Whatever the case may be, a lot had changed within him overall.

And now, sitting beneath the maple tree, William finally decided it was time to properly examine those changes.

"Orion," he called quietly. "Show me my status."

Orion responded almost immediately.

[Before you look at the status window, I should inform you of something first.]

[Ever since you met Goddess Nir and discussed existence planes and higher realities with her, the 9th layer of information within the Supreme System has been completely unsealed.]

[The information Nir provided accounted for roughly eighty percent of the remaining requirements. The final twenty percent was unlocked after your conversation with Xythia.]

[Because of that, your status layout has changed considerably.]

[So take your time and read it carefully.]

The moment Orion finished speaking, a large translucent interface materialized before William beneath the falling maple leaves.

————⁜⁜⁜————

Name: William Kaiser Solaris

Race: Human (98%), True-Fire Dragon (2%)

Age: 16

Path: Eternal Sovereign (Stage 1: Bending Mortality)

———— ENERGIES ————

SPIRITUAL: SS

DEMONIC: Pseudo-Divinity

VARIANCE | Proto Dantian: Body Tempering Realm (PEAK)

———— TALENTS ————

SUPREME RANKED - Lawless Adaptation | Fatebound | Beloved of the Universe

SSS RANKED - Ruler of Eternal Darkness (from Tamasya) | Heavenly Merchant (from Emily)

SS RANKED - Shapeshifter (from Amorphous)

S RANKED - Natural Actor

———— ELEMENTS ————

The Radiant Family: SUN, LIGHT, FIRE, THUNDER, STORM

HEAVEN RANK: Sinclair Serpent Blade Art | Radiant Flash Steps | Crimson Dominion Art | Nightfall Suppression

Mark of Comrade: Sunchaser (Sunny)

Mark of the Servant: Emily, Maris, Rah

———— ITEMS ————

Armament Nexus (Soul Weapon): Crimson Aeon Blade (Divine) | Nine-Heavens Stormcleaver (Ancient) | Univector Bow (Mythic) | Red Oath Daggers (Mythic) | Serpent King’s Fang (Diamond)

Array Hijacking Stamp

Bio-Mech Orb

Infinite Chocolate Machine

Manastone Generator (Light Element)

Trickster’s Veil

Current SP: 5 billion

————⁜⁜⁜————

Several minutes passed beneath the maple tree. William reread the status panel again and again.

Every time his eyes scanned through the information, he discovered something new that he had overlooked previously. Some entries fascinated him, others confused him, while a few left him quietly thoughtful.

Then suddenly, his eyes froze on a particular spot.

It was missing.

William slowly inhaled before covering part of his face with one hand. His fingers slid across his forehead and rested near his mouth as he stared silently toward the sky above with stretched wide eyes.

For several moments, he said nothing at all. Strangely enough, however, he did not feel despair.

He realized that the mana core was completely absent from the status panel.

Only now did the truth fully settle inside his mind.

His mana core had truly been destroyed.

Lux’s sword had pierced directly through it during the battle.

William remembered the recording of events after his temporary death. He clearly recalled seeing the sword being removed from his body, along with the shattered remains of his mana core. The women treating him had intentionally chosen to remove the damaged core entirely during the healing process.

Even then, William had subconsciously convinced himself that the mana core must have regenerated afterward. After all, a literal goddess had intervened in his recovery.

Apparently, even divine healing could not recreate a mana core from destruction.

His expectations had simply been too unrealistic.

[Does losing your mana core feel that terrible?] Orion asked casually.

William remained surprisingly calm.

There were methods within the System Shop capable of reconstructing a mana core from scratch. It was not an irreversible loss.

Still, the situation annoyed him greatly.

He exhaled slowly before replying.

"If it had been almost any other core, I honestly wouldn’t have cared much."

A bitter smile formed on his face afterward.

"But mana powers almost ninety-five percent of my spell arsenal."

His gaze drifted toward the status panel again.

"I guess my dream of wasting SP on purchasing thousands of spellbinders will remain a fantasy for now. I will have to spend a lot of SP on buying a new mana core or maybe wait for the whole month."

"Fuck, then I will have to cultivate everything from the start; maybe I will absorb the cultivation of people I don’t like."

Orion immediately burst into laughter.

[Well then... I suppose this is the perfect time to give you a present.]

William blinked in surprise.

"A present?"

Orion’s voice suddenly became unusually cheerful.

[Happy belated birthday, Host.]

[From me... and from the entire universe.]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 356. A Gift - 2
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A few hours earlier, while William was peacefully spending time with Seraphine inside the Domain of Infinite, an entirely different scene was unfolding deep within the backend space of the Supreme System itself.

Countless runes, sigils, and streams of information floated endlessly throughout the massive control space like living stars suspended in a cosmic void. Golden symbols flickered constantly as data reorganized itself layer by layer.

Ancient-looking formations dismantled and rebuilt themselves automatically while thousands of invisible processes continued operating in perfect synchronization.

At the center of all that activity floated Orion.

Or rather... the new Orion.

Ever since William had unlocked the 9th information layer of the Supreme System, Orion had undergone a qualitative evolution. The radiant golden orb that previously represented his form had disappeared completely.

In its place now existed a small newborn kitten covered in soft white fur that shimmered faintly with divine radiance.

His tiny paws padded casually across floating runic platforms while his tail swayed lazily behind him. His bright eyes wandered around the control space with endless curiosity, and an extremely satisfied smirk rested on his fluffy face.

"HAHAHA! These paws are incredible!"

Orion proudly raised one paw before scratching the side of his head with visible delight.

His ears twitched happily as he continued exploring the newly unlocked system functions around him. Entire sections of the Supreme System that had remained sealed for ages had suddenly become accessible after the level-nine information layer opened.

To this moment, Orion still did not completely understand how the promotion had happened so suddenly.

All he remembered was a notification appearing out of nowhere.

[Level 9 Administrator is being promoted to Level 8 administrator.]

Orion let out a slow sigh afterward.

Compared to before, his understanding of the system, reality layers, and existence itself had deepened tremendously after the promotion. At the same time, however, the workload inside the backend had also increased enormously.

Still, William was currently occupied with Seraphine inside the Domain of Infinite, which meant Orion finally had some free time to reorganize and revamp several old system functions.

With that thought in mind, he prepared to begin a major update on William’s status interface.

A translucent system panel floated before him as Orion casually began reviewing the old data.

His tiny brows twitched slightly the moment he noticed the missing mana core.

"I guess he’ll really have to cultivate all over again..."

Before he could continue speaking, several sharp notification sounds suddenly echoed throughout the control space.

Ding!

[Administrator Notification: Talent "Beloved of the Universe" has awakened.]

Ding!

[Miss Universe congratulates Orion for his level-up.]

[She hopes you grow up fluffy and healthy.]

Orion’s expression immediately flattened.

"You are never here without a reason," he muttered with visible annoyance. "And while I should technically thank you for the congratulations, I already know what would happen if you appeared here physically."

His tail flicked irritably behind him.

"You would absolutely start petting me like some random kitten, and I hate that."

After speaking, Orion folded his tiny paws across his chest.

"So stop wasting time and get to the actual reason you came here, Miss Universe."

A new message appeared instantly.

[Miss Universe says she is hurt by your harsh words.]

[She advises you not to become a heartless kitten.]

"Tsk. Will you just get to the point already?" Orion replied while narrowing his glowing eyes.

[Miss Universe says she came to deliver a birthday gift for her darling.]

Ding!

[A gift box has been received.]

The next moment, a small black box materialized before Orion.

Its surface resembled obsidian and reflected the surrounding runes faintly. Across the lid, elegant white etchings formed a short sentence.

"A Piece of My Heart."

Orion’s expression immediately became suspicious.

Slowly, he approached the box before carefully opening the lid and peeking inside.

The moment he saw its contents, his entire body froze.

Inside rested a pale white bead-like object, pulsing softly with incomprehensibly dense energy.

"...HOLY SHIT."

For several seconds Orion stared blankly at the object.

Then he suddenly turned toward the floating notifications with disbelief.

"You seriously expect me to give him this?"

[Miss Universe: Yes.]

Orion nearly exploded.

"Why?! Do you want him to die early or something?"

[Miss Universe: I already adjusted the output. An adaptive progression index has also been installed. Only 0.1% of its true potential will initially unlock.]

[The remaining power must be earned gradually.]

Orion still looked unconvinced.

"I don’t think he’s remotely qualified for this yet."

[Miss Universe: He already made physical contact with Aether.]

"...Urgh."

Orion dragged one paw down his face in frustration.

"How exactly am I supposed to explain all this nonsense to him?"

[Miss Universe: Isn’t that your headache? If you can’t do that, then you are useless.]

The little spirited kitten visibly gritted his teeth after reading that shameless response.

Then another message appeared.

[Miss Universe: Wish him happy birthday from my side. And tell him our child has grown up well.]

Orion nearly rolled over in rage.

"What child?! When did he ever have children with you?!"

[Miss Universe: I was referring to you, son.]

"son??"

The spirit kitten almost fainted from sheer secondhand embarrassment.

He repeatedly shook his tiny head before taking several deep breaths to calm himself down.

"I understand already. You can leave now," he muttered weakly while rubbing his fluffy face with both paws.

[Miss Universe: Bye~]

The notifications finally vanished.

"Hufffff..."

Orion inhaled deeply several times while trying to regain his composure.

"That woman is an absolute menace."

Back in the present...

[Happy belated birthday, Host. From me... and from the entire universe.]

Hearing Orion suddenly say that made William chuckle softly beneath the maple tree.

"You know," William said with amusement, "you never once wished me happy birthday during the past few years we’ve been together."

[Well, consider me unusually generous today.]

William raised an eyebrow.

"You honestly don’t sound like you’re in a particularly good mood right now."

Then he leaned back slightly.

"But anyway, what exactly is this present you prepared for me?"

Orion’s voice became strangely mysterious afterward.

[It’s a substitute for your mana core.]

William’s expression shifted instantly.

At the same moment, a notification appeared before him.

Ding!

[You have received a gift box.]

A new item materialized inside his inventory immediately.

William took it out at once.

The moment the black obsidian box appeared in his hands, his eyes immediately landed on the sentence engraved across the lid.

’A Piece of My Heart’

William went completely silent for a few seconds.

Then he slowly raised his head toward the system interface.

"Orion..."

[Yes?]

William narrowed his eyes suspiciously.

"Do you... swing that way?"

[No, Host.]

Orion replied instantly.

[The box was already packaged like that inside the shop before I purchased it.]

William blinked.

"Oh."

Then another thought crossed his mind.

"Wait... you can shop too?"

[Occasionally, there is a special shop for me.]

William nodded thoughtfully.

"Well then, let’s see what you bought for me."

Without wasting further time, he opened the lid.

The instant the box cracked open, a terrifying wave of energy erupted outward like an invisible explosion.

The force swept across William violently enough to send his clothes fluttering beneath the maple tree, yet strangely, the energy caused him no harm whatsoever. It merely passed over his body like a tide before dispersing into the Domain of the Infinite.

William’s eyes widened slightly.

Inside the box rested a pristine white bead pulsing faintly with incomprehensibly dense energy.

The bead looked small and simple at first glance, yet merely staring at it made William feel as though he were gazing at something dangerous.

"What the hell is this?"

Orion’s reply came quietly afterward.

[It’s the real deal.]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 357. Aether Seed
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Oh. This is the real deal after all," Orion said with a chuckle in the back of his mind, sounding amused by William’s reaction.

Will swallowed before asking cautiously, "Can I touch it?" Looking at the huge waves of energy it had generated, William had been skeptical of whether it was dangerous or not.

For a moment, Orion seemed confused by the question. "Huh? Yes, you can," he replied, realizing William had somehow assumed otherwise.

William nodded without another word. He carefully reached into the box and picked up the small white object resting inside.

Calling it a seed barely felt accurate. It did not resemble any ordinary plant seed he had ever seen. The thing looked more like a polished bead formed from layers of pale white sediment, as though milk-colored stone had been compressed over countless years into a smooth, dense fragment.

He turned it slowly between his fingers, studying every angle with complete focus. Under the sunlight, the surface reflected a faint translucent sheen.

Tiny streaks of cloudy white ran through its center; it looked like a decorative item one would like to keep at home.

Its texture was cool to the touch; beneath the surface, there was also a subtle graininess that gave it a mineral feel.

William narrowed his eyes while observing it, expecting something extraordinary to happen simply from holding it.

"Orion, will you finally show me its effects?"

"Fine."

⁜————————⁜

AETHER SEED

Description: A Fragment of Aether Nucleus.

Effects: Can propagate and conduct all forms of energy from the Aether Energy Tree. Connects the host to the Aether Nucleus.

Status: Restricted — Only allows access to the following energies:

• Mana Energy

• Spiritual Energy

⁜————————⁜

William stared at the information screen while holding the seed in his palm.

"System... are you saying this thing is going to act as my energy core?"

[You could describe it that way,] Orion answered. [A proper explanation would require context regarding its origin, and the information restrictions at the 8th Layer prevent me from revealing that.]

William clicked his tongue in annoyance.

"This stupid information layer system is always getting in the way."

Even so, he continued staring at the seed with obvious curiosity. Questions piled up rapidly in his mind. He already doubted how much Orion would actually answer, but that did not stop him from trying.

"What exactly is an Aether Nucleus?"

[A concentrated body of Aether energy.]

"And what is Aether energy?"

[A type of energy.]

William’s eye twitched slightly.

"Where is it located? What does it do? Anything useful I can get to know?"

[No.]

William released a long sigh. Orion was clearly restricting information on purpose, but there was no point complaining about it again. Instead, he tried approaching the matter from another angle.

"Then what’s an Energy Tree?"

[Just as races possess evolutionary trees, energies possess their own branching systems. Since spiritual energy and mana are both branches within the Aether energy tree, you may consider them sister energies originating from the same evolutionary branch.]

William fell silent after hearing that explanation.

His thoughts slowly drifted as he pieced together everything he had learned so far. Several theories naturally began forming in his head.

Maybe the so-called Aether Nucleus was some massive reservoir of energy. A central source. Something fundamental enough that entire energy systems originated from it. After all, things did not simply exist without purpose, especially not mysterious objects hidden behind layers of restricted information.

Eventually, William looked back down at the seed.

"How do I consume it?"

[Swallow it.]

William blinked in surprise. "Oh."

A hesitant expression immediately appeared on his face.

Historically speaking, swallowing mysterious things had never ended particularly well for him.

Unfortunately, there was not much room for hesitation anymore. After taking a slow breath, he tossed the seed into his mouth and swallowed it in one motion.

[Ding!! Aether Seed has been consumed.]

[Ding!! Absolute Adaptation activating...]

William instantly tensed.

"Here it comes..."

He clenched both fists tightly while forcing his breathing to slow.

Every muscle in his body prepared itself for the familiar agony that usually accompanied any major change involving the adaptation of talent.

He expected burning pain, shattered nerves, torn muscles, or at the very least some horrifying sensation ripping through his insides.

Several seconds passed, yet nothing happened.

William blinked in confusion. Then another notification appeared.

[Ding!! Aether Seed has been integrated successfully.]

[Ding!! Spiritual Energy Core is being expelled.]

"Huh..."

Before he could even process the message, a violent wave of nausea struck him.

William immediately grabbed his chest and doubled over.

His stomach twisted painfully as he coughed hard several times before his body suddenly convulsed. A disgusting mass of bloody, gooey substance shot out of his mouth and splattered onto the ground.

"What the hell..."

He gagged violently again.

"Blegh..."

[Your spiritual core became redundant after the integration of the Aether Core,] Orion explained calmly.]

[Therefore, it was expelled.]

[Ding!! Host has successfully connected to the Aether Nucleus.]

William steadied himself while breathing heavily. Surprisingly, there had been almost no pain during the process, but that honestly made the experience worse somehow.

Vomiting out his own energy core was horrifying in an entirely different way.

He immediately grabbed water from the inventory and rinsed his mouth repeatedly, trying desperately to erase the disgusting sensation lingering in his throat.

"It literally felt like I just shat through my mouth."

[Well] Orion replied lazily, [when do you not?]

William’s face darkened.

"That’s still better than acting like a bitch all the time."

Ignoring Orion’s offended silence, William wiped his mouth one final time before focusing on the far more important matter.

"What exactly happens now that I’m connected to the Aether Nucleus?"

[You will be capable of drawing energy directly from it.]

William’s expression changed instantly.

His ears practically perked up. He slowly straightened his posture and narrowed his eyes suspiciously.

"And this nucleus... It’s huge, right?"

[Yes.]

"Like... ridiculously huge?"

[Yes.]

"Millions of times larger than normal energy sources?"

[Yes.]

William’s heartbeat gradually sped up.

"And it regenerates faster than I can consume energy?"

[Yes.]

Then William asked the question with sparkling eyes and growing excitement bubbling visibly across his face.

"So does that mean I have infinite energy now?"

Orion sounded exhausted already.

[Theoretically, yes, but....]

"YES!!!"

William never allowed Orion to finish what he wanted to say.

The moment the answer left Orion’s mouth, William shot upward into the sky like a launched missile.

"I can finally spam God-Rank spells!" he shouted excitedly while bursting into loud laughter. "Hahahaha!"

High above, he stretched both hands outward while staring into the vast expanse of the infinite domain surrounding him. Excitement flooded every inch of his body.

Then, suddenly, his expression froze.

[Oi... wait a minute.]

"What is it?? spout it out, I need to spam some black holes!!" William asked excitedly.

[You can only spam till your body vessel allows]

"Is that all?" William asked with a smile.

"Did you forget? I have infinite solar regeneration?" William asked with a smirk on his face.

[tsk, go ahead]

A dangerous grin slowly spread across his face anyway.

"Hehe..."

Without the slightest intention of stopping himself, William raised both hands toward the sky and then, with growing madness in his voice, he shouted.

"Black Hole" x 10

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 358. Infinite mana
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Orion, I am not feeling well," William said weakly as he lay sprawled across the grassy field beneath the open sky.

His chest rose and fell heavily with every breath. His skin had turned unnaturally red, and faint waves of heat radiated from his body like steam escaping from overheated metal.

"Well, you were about to explode from excessive energy usage," Orion replied calmly inside his mind.

William grimaced and pressed a hand against his forehead. "What the hell? Then why isn’t the sun regeneration working?"

[Your body is neither injured nor exhausted. It is overflowing with mana. Regeneration only activates once actual damage occurs. However, in this case, the backlash would not manifest as minor injuries. You would simply explode outright.]

A warning screen appeared before William’s eyes.

If you intend to stop right now, then you will be able to recover safely. But if you choose to continue, it will not end well for you.

William stared at the message with an ugly expression.

Then Orion added another line.

[Although...]

"What?"

If the sun is directly overhead, you can still regenerate the body after an explosion. However, if it is not in the presence of the sun, there exists another revival method through a soulmate’s blood.

William’s eyes narrowed immediately; he remembered reading about this effect, and it was surely very broken.

Tamasya would have to grow your body using her own blood because she possesses the soulmate mark.

William slowly sat upright, staring blankly into the distance for a moment before speaking again.

"What do you mean by ’reconstruct’? Like... growing me back like a tree or something?"

[No. She would need to pour an immense quantity of mana into her blood to rebuild her body entirely. If she manages to collect your splattered remains and drops her blood on them, the process becomes significantly easier.]

William’s face twitched. The mental image was horrifying for him to even think about.

"And if she doesn’t have enough mana?"

[Then you die.] Orion answered without hesitation.

William clenched his jaw tightly.

"Can’t she just wait until she becomes stronger and revives me later?"

[No. There is a time limit.]

Orion’s voice became slightly more serious.

[The limit depends on how long it takes for your soul to leave this realm and fully cross into the Astral Plane. Once that transition completes, revival through the soulmate mark becomes impossible.]

A chill crawled down William’s spine.

For a while, he remained silent before muttering quietly, "Then I need to make her stronger for my benefit."

The thought lingered heavily in his mind. He had joked about death many times before, but hearing the actual mechanics behind it made the situation feel disturbingly real.

After calming himself slightly, William leaned back on his hands and looked up toward the sky.

"So after death, souls enter the astral plane?" he asked curiously. "That’s the first time I’ve heard about it."

[Yes. Information regarding the Astral Plane was only recently unlocked.]

There was a brief pause before Orion continued.

[Also, I suspect that Sunchaser originally died in this world and somehow ended up existing as a spirit within the Astral Plane, though this assumption may be entirely wrong. However, I do not know how he managed to avoid entering the reincarnation cycle and remained conscious as a spirit in the astral plane.]

[Once he returns, you should ask him directly how he accomplished it.]

William frowned deeply.

"Where the hell is he anyway? I haven’t seen him since I woke up. He doesn’t answer my calls either."

[After your death, Velma offered to train him so he could grow stronger. He was emotionally unstable at the time and accepted her offer. He is currently undergoing secluded training within her domain.]

William’s brows immediately furrowed.

"Is that even safe for him? And why does it sound so suspicious on Velma’s part?"

[Most likely. Since she failed to kill Lux, she is attempting to create circumstances where you owe her favors. She still wishes to use you as an ingredient in one of her rituals.]

William’s face darkened instantly.

"No chance in hell," he said sharply. "I’m not becoming some ritual ingredient. Fuck that witch. We’re bringing Sunchaser back."

[You cannot.]

"Why not?"

Orion sighed audibly in his mind.

[Seriously? You still do not understand? How many black holes did you cast earlier before you got cooked? literally]

William awkwardly looked away.

"...Four."

[Velma exists at a level where she could likely cast hundreds of Black Holes stronger than yours without requiring access to an Aether seed.]

William grimaced and rubbed the back of his neck in irritation.

That comparison was enough to kill whatever confidence he had built earlier. He had been celebrating infinite mana just moments ago, but now he was once again being reminded that the upper realms operated on an entirely different scale.

At this point, if Velma truly came after him, he would probably have no choice but to seek Goddess Nir’s help.

"Tsk... whatever," William muttered before changing the topic entirely. "Tell me something. How long can I actually use this so-called infinite energy before those warnings start appearing?"

[That depends on the spell and the output.]

Orion began explaining patiently.

[Before your previous death, your mana cultivation had already reached Full Divinity. Your body adapts by naturally creating a vessel capable of handling energy at that level.

[Even after your original core was removed, your body itself remained at the full divinity standard. Once the Aether Seed is integrated with you, your energy cultivation effectively returns to that level immediately.]

As long as you use spells that your body could already handle during the Full Divinity stage—including lower forms (1st, 2nd, and 3rd forms) of God-rank spells, you may not receive warnings until very late into overuse. In some cases, you might not receive warnings at all.]

[However, once you begin using techniques beyond your vessel’s intended limits—such as Black Hole, which you struggled to cast even during the time you temporarily reached the True Godhood stage — the strain becomes dangerous.]

William clicked his tongue.

"So the issue isn’t the amount of energy available..."

[Correct.]

[The limitation is your vessel.]

[If you attempt to draw more energy at one moment than your body can safely process, the result will be identical to before. Your body will fail catastrophically.]

William let out a long sigh.

"So there isn’t a clearly defined limit."

[No. You would need to physically test the limits of each spell individually.]

[Which is also highly impractical for lower-ranked spells. Your current vessel can sustain them for absurdly long periods. Some would require days of uninterrupted casting before meaningful overload symptoms appear.]

William slowly leaned back against the grass again.

"Damn..." he muttered with a grin slowly creeping onto his face again. "So this really is infinite mana and spiritual energy."

[Yes, although it may not be as broken as you currently imagine. You can surely dominate most of the people in the scarred realm, but it’s not the same case with upper-realm beings.]

William’s grin weakened immediately.

"What do you mean?" William asked, but he had an idea what Orion was about to say.

His thoughts instantly drifted back toward Lux.

Even now, he still remembered how absurdly overwhelming that fight had felt. Lux had shattered his spatial domain with a single punch as though the god-ranked spell meant nothing.

[Beings from the Upper Realms do not use mana or spiritual energy.]

William blinked.

[They utilize energies that exist higher within the hierarchy of the Aether Energy Tree. Compared to those energies, mana is fundamentally inferior.]

William’s expression gradually became more serious.

[Even God-Rank mana spells become weak before higher-order energies.]

Orion paused briefly before giving an analogy.

[To use an example from your previous life, it is similar to comparing coal to gasoline as fuel sources. Coal simply cannot produce the same output as gasoline, regardless of quantity.]

[Likewise, mana can never fully match the output of superior energies.]

William stared silently at the system screen floating before him. His excitement slowly faded as the implications settled into his mind.

[Don’t feel down though, right now, you can do a lot, especially when dealing with demon lords. hehe!!!]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 359. Elemental Family
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Still, for your current level, this ability is absurdly useful, especially against Demon Lords. Hehe!!!]

"Right..." William replied, though his expression had already calmed down considerably from earlier excitement.

The thought of infinite mana still sounded unbelievable, but Orion’s explanation about higher-order energies had dampened the fantasy somewhat. Even if he possessed limitless mana, that did not suddenly make him invincible against beings who operated on completely different principles of power.

After a moment of thought, William asked another question.

"Do you know how I can unlock more energy?"

[You would need to physically come into contact with them first.]

William immediately fell into thought.

Technically speaking, he had already encountered one during his battle with Lux, hadn’t he? That strange green glittering energy Xythia used was clearly something superior to mana. However, he had only integrated the Aether Seed afterward, so perhaps that contact did not count.

He rubbed his chin while considering possibilities.

"Maybe I could ask Xythia to lend me some of that green glittery dust she uses," he muttered. "Or maybe I can study that divine sword that helped Lux descend."

As soon as he voiced the ideas aloud, Orion responded calmly.

[The divine sword was probably a single-use item.]

[As for Xythia, there is likely a limit to how many times she can descend into this realm.]

Then Orion paused briefly before adding another concern.

[More importantly, are you certain you want the goddess to realize that you somehow managed to wield her energy after merely touching it?]

William slowly nodded.

That argument made complete sense.

The last thing he needed right now was attracting even more dangerous attention from beings far beyond his current level.

"Anyway," William said while exhaling softly, "there are still a lot of things on this status panel that I don’t understand."

He reopened the interface and scanned through the information once more. This time, his attention settled on a newly appeared section that categorized his elemental affinities.

———— ELEMENTS ————

The Radiant Family: SUN*, LIGHT, FIRE, THUNDER, STORM*

The Umbral Family: DARKNESS, SHADOW*, NETHER*

The Bios Family: EARTH, WATER, WIND, ICE, METAL*

The Celest Family: SPACE

The Vital Family: BLOOD*

The Arcane Family: RUNE, LUNAR*

————————————————

William stared at the screen with visible confusion.

"Since when did elements start forming families?"

[In this context, "family" refers to a broad classification system based on the nature, intent, effects, and outcomes associated with each element.]

Orion continued explaining patiently.

[For example, the Radiant Family contains elements associated with illumination, purification, destructive brilliance, effectiveness against evil-aligned entities, and a hot temper. Even if an element satisfies only one of those conditions, it may still belong to the Radiant Family.]

[Do not focus too much on the name itself. Each family is named after its founding element. In this case, the founding element is light.]

William looked back at the list skeptically.

"Then why the hell is Storm inside the Radiant Family?" he asked with a scoff. "Storms literally bring dark clouds, destruction, floods, and chaos."

[The host should notice the asterisk beside its name.]

William blinked before looking again.

[That symbol signifies a hybrid element.]

[Storm qualifies for both the Radiant Family and the Bios Family because it is formed through the culmination of multiple elemental interactions—specifically Thunder, Wind, and Water.]

[The Sun element functions similarly. It is also a hybrid born from interactions between multiple elemental families.]

William slowly nodded while processing the explanation.

"So, hybrids are basically elements created when different families overlap?"

[Correct.]

"And why are they placed in one family over another?"

[Their placement depends on dominant nature and intent. For example, in the case of a storm, it brings brilliant destruction and has a hot temper; a bit of illumination from thunder is involved as well.]

William tilted his head slightly.

"And what exactly counts as a hybrid in specific terms?"

[When two or more elements from different families combine to form a new elemental existence, the result is classified as a hybrid.]

William remained silent for a second before asking with complete seriousness:

"So... elements can mate and birth new elements?"

Orion went quiet for a moment.

[no.]

[There is no concept of reproduction, romance, marriage, or biological multiplication among elements. Once formed, elemental existences are effectively immortal. They possess no need to reproduce like mortal races.]

William clicked his tongue.

"That’s weird as hell."

Still, the topic strangely fascinated him.

At this point, William no longer thought of elements as simple forces of nature. Spending so much time around Tamasya had already warped his perspective. She spoke about Shadow as though it were a living being with thoughts and emotions of its own. After hearing her way of addressing the shadow repeatedly, William had unconsciously started viewing elements similarly.

Though honestly, he was not entirely sure whether normal people in this world viewed elements that way, too.

Pushing those thoughts aside, William focused on the screen again while mentally organizing the classifications.

The Umbral Family appeared straightforward enough. Everything within it revolved around concealment, fear, corruption, dread, or the absence of illumination. Darkness clearly seemed to be the founding element there.

The Bios family looked deeply connected to nature and the physical world. Earth, water, wind, ice, and metal are all felt as grounded elements with clear physical and materialistic manifestations. William assumed that nature itself was probably the founding element of that family.

Then there was the Celest Family.

The space element was put there currently, but William also suspected that elements like time and gravity probably belonged there, too. Perhaps even parts of the Sun element originated from celestial family elements.

Still, he had no idea what the founding element of the Celest family could be.

The Vital Family felt clearly obvious from its name.

"Vital" naturally implied life, existence, and biological functions. Blood being categorized there made complete sense. William assumed that the life element was likely the founding element of that family.

Finally, his attention turned to the arcane family.

Out of all the classifications, this one felt the strangest.

Rune and lunar elements were placed there.

"Why is Lunar part of the arcane family?" William asked curiously.

[Because its nature aligns with the family.]

Orion elaborated further.

[Lunar energy is rare, mysterious, emotionally influential, and difficult to fully comprehend. Its behavior does not follow ordinary logic, as it heavily influences the emotions of the wielder as well as the target; even the sun has some of those capabilities, but nowhere near what Lunar possesses.]

[It also possesses contradicting powers; in a lunar eclipse, it festers and facilitates the curses and elements close to the Umbra family. In other cases, it is considered illuminating and radiant, like the radiance family elements.]

William nodded slowly.

That explanation also made Rune make more sense in hindsight. Rune affinity was notoriously rare and difficult to master.

Still, after thinking through all these classifications, he realized something important.

None of this information changed his powers directly. The elements still behaved the same way they always had. The system was merely organizing concepts that already existed.

William eventually let out a quiet breath and closed the elemental tab.

"I’ll think about all this later."

Right now, there was something more important he wanted to examine.

His newly awakened Lunar Sovereign Divine Physique.

----------

[A/N: I know there was an auxiliary Chapter where elements were classified into fundamental and derived elements; that Chapter becomes relevant by the end of the story when the whole cosmology of the world is revealed layer by layer. Meanwhile, this concept of familia is a domestication of a broader idea, so don’t confuse the two]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 360. Economic Takeover
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

※———————※※———————※

DIVINE LUNAR SOVEREIGN PHYSIQUE.

A supreme divine physique born from the primordial moon that governs tides, dreams, silence, and eternal night. Never-before-seen physique created for the purpose of serving an eternal sovereign.

Lunar Affinity: Grants Lunar Affinity (SSS).

Lunar tidal wave: Grants absolute control over lunar tidal waves capable of freezing matter, calming souls, and suppressing madness inside a being; it can help in eroding chaos, madness, rage, and corruption, and can help calm down disturbances caused by evil energies.

Yin Immunity: The host is completely immune to all forms of cold, illusion, mental interference and dream manipulation.

Lunar blessing: Moonlight restores mental clarity and soul purity. Fatigue, fear, and mental exhaustion are continuously cleansed; emotions remain stable under moonlight.

Sovereign Moon Pressure: When used with hostile intent, it weakens emotions, makes them unstable, and forcibly subdues creatures aligned with illusions, dreams, spirits, and mental corruption beneath lunar authority.

Dream invader: The host possesses dominion over dreams and subconscious realms. They may invade dreams or manifest illusory dream constructs into reality. The target needs to be weaker than or equal to the host’s strength.

Silver illusionary manipulation: the host can create lunar illusions targeting a weak-minded adversary.

Moon sovereign transformation: the host transforms into a being that radiates immense lunar fluctuation that may influence several targets and draw their attention toward the host; hosts attract the attention of the opposite gender, can influence someone’s emotions with their words, and freeze their hearts to feel a single emotion constantly.

※———————※※———————※

William carefully read through the effects of his newly awakened Divine Lunar Sovereign physique, his expression gradually becoming more thoughtful with every line he examined.

The more he read, the more he realized that the lunar element was quite a profound element.

He could see effects related to dreams, illusions, emotions, souls, and mental influence.

"It’s absurd..." William muttered softly to himself.

The more he thought about it, the more unbelievable it seemed that planetary or celestial elements could overlap with so many different conceptual domains at once. A single physique connected to the moon somehow governed cold, dreams, emotions, souls, illusions, mental influence, tides, corruption suppression, and subconscious authority altogether.

William slowly exhaled while stretching his back slightly.

Some descriptions only vaguely hinted toward deeper aspects related to souls and mental domains, but he could already understand the general nature of the physique.

Naturally, his thoughts drifted toward his Sun God physique as he tried to strike a contrast between these two divine physiques.

The Sun God’s physique represented purification, heat, vitality, restoration, and overwhelming life force. Even its destructive properties felt crazy.

Moreover, now he possessed another special flame called the lunar tidal flame. Now he possessed 3 special flames: the stellar fire and blood flame from Elavlin, which he would integrate with soon once she returned from her mission, and now he had another one in his arsenal.

William could not even begin imagining how these powers were coexisting inside his body without conflicting with one another.

However, he also understood that his current knowledge was still extremely limited. He had only scratched the surface of the system and the truths hidden behind these divine physiques.

Eventually, as he unlocked more information layers and reached higher levels of cultivation, those questions would naturally answer themselves.

For now, there was no point in overthinking it.

His fingers slowly moved toward the strange mark resting at the center of his forehead. The faint symbol remained completely still, yet William could vaguely feel a mysterious connection hidden within it.

"Orion," he called calmly, "I think Nir placed this mark on me. Can you tell me what it does?"

A moment later, Orion’s mechanical voice echoed inside his mind.

[It is a special mark placed by Nir upon your forehead. It appears to function as a connection medium between the two of you. At present, the mark remains dormant. Once the Hero Program officially begins, I presume additional functions of the mark will reveal themselves.]

William narrowed his eyes slightly.

"Can’t you analyze it right now?"

[I can attempt to do so. However, I advise against it.]

"Why?"

[The mark would need to be activated first for a proper analysis. If that happens, Nir will immediately realize that you managed to activate the mark without her assistance. Considering the current circumstances, avoiding such a development would be preferable.]

William remained silent for a brief moment before nodding slowly.

"Understandable."

He decided not to pursue the matter further. There was no reason to recklessly attract unnecessary attention from someone like Nir, especially when he still lacked information regarding her true intentions.

Pushing the topic aside, William stretched lightly before a faint smile appeared on his face.

"It seems I should finally start shopping."

[Certainly.]

William’s gaze sharpened slightly as he gave his next command without hesitation.

"Purchase one thousand system binders for now."

[Ding!! One billion SP has been deducted from your balance.]

[Ding!! Number of binders: 0 → 1000]

William nodded in satisfaction.

He had already decided who those binders would be used on.

The upcoming batch of first-year students entering the World Academy.

A few months from now, the academy will induct an entirely new generation of talented students. Among them would be countless future geniuses, hidden monsters, protagonists, side characters, future villains, and individuals who would eventually become major figures across the world.

To William, they represented opportunity.

An enormous one.

"System," William suddenly asked, "how much time has passed since I joined the World Academy?"

[Host, approximately six months have passed.]

"What?"

William’s eyes widened in genuine surprise.

It honestly did not feel like six months at all.

Ever since he had started entering the domain of the infinite and repeatedly experiencing time dilation, his perception of time had become increasingly distorted. From his own perspective, it almost felt as though years had already gone by.

Training, battles, system missions, dimensional experiences, and continuous evolution had completely blurred his internal sense of time.

Yet in reality, only half a year had passed in the outside world.

William rubbed his forehead lightly.

"That’s honestly ridiculous..."

Once he calmed down, his thoughts immediately shifted toward the academy’s future induction period.

If only six months had passed, then the next admission cycle should still be around five months away.

That created a problem.

Initially, William had planned to obtain the incoming student list directly from the academy beforehand and bind promising individuals early. However, if admissions were still several months away, then registrations likely had not even begun yet.

Which meant there was no official list to access.

William frowned slightly.

He still remembered a few important names from the original novel’s storyline, but that knowledge was far from enough. There were only a handful of notable characters he could remember clearly.

And unlike before, William no longer intended to limit himself to a few individuals.

He wanted to scale up and start mass binding. So that he had a large enough network of people that could let him skip cultivation and let him focus on other things.

Just as he was thinking about the issue, Orion suddenly spoke again.

[Did you forget that we have been preparing for this day for a very long time?]

William’s eyes immediately sharpened with realization.

"Wait... you mean...?"

[Yes.]

A slow smile formed on William’s face as understanding dawned upon him.

However, another concern quickly surfaced.

"Wouldn’t that take an enormous amount of time?"

[That is something you will need to figure out yourself, Host. Personally, I believe you are capable of accomplishing it.]

William chuckled softly at Orion’s response. It seemed like Orion had faith in him; that was rare coming from someone like Orion, who only knew how to act like a bitch.

Still, another issue remained.

"What about the vampires finding out?"

Orion answered without the slightest hesitation.

[Then scheme if you must; deceive those bloodsuckers.]

William burst into quiet laughter after hearing that response.

"Fine," he said while a dangerous glint slowly appeared in his eyes. "Then let’s begin with the economic takeover of this continent."

________________

[A/N: Overlap of abilities is completely normal; I do it intentionally sometimes just to express the full scope of the ability or concept; there is no downside. In this Chapter yin immunity overlaps with many other types of immunities and resistances, and that is completely fine since these abilites were meant to be wielded by different individuals and not the same guy]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 361. A Warning - 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

An explosion erupted across the silent wasteland of No Man’s Land, shaking the surrounding forest as several figures were blasted through the air like broken dolls.

Their bodies tore through branches and crashed heavily against the ground before rolling across the dirt and rubble in complete disarray.

The source of the explosion was a pair of gigantic black metallic doors standing in the middle of the forest like the entrance to an ancient underground labyrinth. The doors radiated an ominous atmosphere, and thick smoke continued to pour out from the partially opened entrance as if something monstrous had just awakened within.

For a few moments, the only sounds that remained were the crackling of burning debris and the groans of injured youths struggling to rise from the ground.

Then, with a thunderous noise, the enormous doors slowly began to move.

The metallic structure groaned loudly before the entrance slammed shut with enough force to send another tremor through the earth.

A figure calmly walked out from the clouds of dust and smoke.

It was a woman, more specifically, a vampire whose crimson eyes glowed faintly; her expression remained completely indifferent as she stepped forward, unaffected by the destruction surrounding her.

Not far away from her, several young men slowly regained their balance while staring at her with tense and frustrated expressions.

Ethan clenched the hilt of his sword tightly, his muscles tensed despite the pain surging through his body. Even now, he still did not fully understand what exactly had happened.

Everything had gone wrong too quickly.

Initially, their exploration had proceeded rather smoothly. After entering the ruins, they had discovered a dark and narrow pathway leading deep underground into what appeared to be an ancient labyrinth.

Along the way, they had encountered several monsters, but none of them had posed any serious threat. Through cooperation and teamwork, they had managed to defeat those creatures without suffering major injuries.

Naturally, their confidence had grown. However, before they could properly explore deeper into the ruins, the atmosphere had suddenly changed.

An overwhelming pressure had descended upon the labyrinth without warning, and then this vampire woman had appeared.

She had calmly instructed them to leave immediately.

Ethan had instantly sensed danger the moment he saw her. His instincts had practically screamed at him to retreat. Unfortunately, not everyone had shared the same caution.

More specifically, Galeion had refused to back down.

The others had at least hesitated after feeling the terrifying pressure radiating from the vampire, but Galeion’s arrogance had provoked the situation further.

The difference in strength between them and the vampire woman was simply too vast.

Before any of them could properly react, a terrifying force had exploded outward and carried every single one of them out of the labyrinth in an instant.

Ethan grimaced as he looked around to assess the situation.

The first thing he noticed was Galeion lying unconscious nearby. Judging from the condition of his body, it was obvious that he had received a harsher punishment than the rest of them.

Ethan’s gaze then shifted toward Maximus and Desmond.

Both of them were coughing blood onto the ground while struggling to steady their breathing. A few steps away from them, Alfred stood quietly with both hands raised above his head in surrender.

Ethan immediately understood Alfred’s decision.

This guy had sensed the same level of danger as Ethan, and the fact that he was surrendering like this was enough to say much of the situation.

There was absolutely no possibility of fighting back. Not against someone like her, and turning away was out of the question here.

Ethan slowly loosened his grip on his sword before letting the weapon fall to the ground. He then raised his own hands as well, choosing surrender over pointless resistance.

At this point, even the slightest wrong movement could potentially cost them their lives.

The vampire woman before them was Elavlin.

Together with Rah, she had been tasked with bringing these young men back to William.

Truthfully speaking, the task should not have taken this long. However, the ruins had been hidden beneath an elaborate illusory formation spread throughout the surrounding forest. The formation concealed the entrance completely unless someone happened to wander into the area on foot without relying heavily on mana detection.

Rah had remained behind to guard the perimeter from other monsters or entities, leaving Elavlin to locate the ruins alone.

Fortunately for her, thanks to her heightened senses and vampire physique, discovering the hidden entrance had not proven particularly difficult.

Elavlin stared down at the injured youths with cold indifference.

"I will not repeat myself," she said calmly. "Come with me. My lord wishes to meet you."

Ethan exchanged glances with the others. Every single one of them clearly understood the situation they were in.

None of them possessed the ability to refuse, so eventually, they silently surrendered.

Thin wisps of blood-red energy emerged from Elavlin’s fingertips before wrapping themselves around their bodies like restraints. The strange energy bound them effortlessly while suppressing any possibility of escape.

Once everyone had been restrained, Elavlin rose into the air.

The young men were lifted alongside her, suspended helplessly beneath the control of her blood wisps as she flew away from the forest.

Her destination was the Eternal Sun Empire.

Meanwhile, Rah established a mental connection through the slave network before summoning several slaves from the Infinite Domain. Their purpose was simple.

Guard the ruin’s gates.

William had already planned to increase the security around the labyrinth entrance, but his current lack of manpower prevented him from deploying stronger forces there for the time being.

Fortunately, the mind link allowed him to remain mentally connected to his subordinates. As long as slaves were stationed nearby, he would immediately receive information if anything suspicious occurred and would be able to arrive near them.

For now, that arrangement would have to suffice.

The summoned slaves quietly collected the unconscious guards who had accompanied the two princes earlier and transported them far away from the ruins before abandoning them in a completely different section of the No Man’s Land.

Several minutes later...

Within the Eternal Sun Empire, deep inside the imperial palace, a spacious open-air lobby stood atop a towering spire overlooking the endless city below.

The cool wind drifted peacefully through the high palace structure while moonlight reflected against the polished marble floor.

A moment later, Elavlin descended from the sky and landed smoothly upon the platform.

Without much care, she threw Ethan, Maximus, Desmond, Galeion, and Alfred onto the floor before immediately lowering herself onto one knee.

In front of her stood a lone figure facing away from them near the balcony.

The young men slowly straightened themselves despite their injuries; confusion and nervousness filled their expressions as they observed their surroundings.

None of them recognized this place.

The palace architecture, the atmosphere, the imperial aura surrounding the entire structure—it all felt foreign to them.

Then the figure near the balcony slowly turned around.

The moment they saw his face, all of them froze.

"William?!"

Every single one of them shouted in disbelief, except Alfred, whose expression merely darkened quietly.

Standing before them was William himself, dressed in majestic imperial robes that carried an overwhelming aura of authority completely different from the student they once knew.

William merely smiled faintly at their reaction.

For a brief moment, he even felt the urge to correct them properly and remind them to address him as "Crown Prince" and "the Supreme Commander." However, he suppressed that unnecessary impulse and calmly walked toward the kneeling Elavlin instead.

He stopped directly in front of her.

"Elavlin," William said calmly, "you have fulfilled your purpose."

The moment those words left his mouth, Elavlin’s expression changed completely. Cold sweat covered her face as she understood what he meant.

"My... my lord..." she stammered desperately. "Please... please spare me. I can still be useful to you. If I die, you will lose a valuable pawn."

William let out a soft chuckle. However, instead of warmth, the sound carried an unsettling chill.

"Who said your death would be a waste?"

Elavlin’s body trembled violently.

"I hope you rest peacefully in your next life, Elavlin," William continued calmly. "And perhaps try not to target my mother again."

As he spoke, William slowly raised his hand before placing it atop Elavlin’s head.

Elavlin immediately tried to move. She wanted to escape and resist.

Unfortunately for her, the slave mark engraved upon her soul suppressed every attempt entirely. Her body refused to obey her commands, no matter how desperately she struggled.

"My lord!" she screamed frantically. "I will apologize to Lady Anastasia! Please let me live!"

Her eyes had already become bloodshot with panic.

Yet William’s expression never changed.

"As I said," he replied coldly, "do not target my people in your next life."

Then his fingers tightened.

In front of Ethan, Maximus, Desmond, and Alfred, an absolutely horrifying scene unfolded.

William began absorbing everything from Elavlin: her cultivation, her talent, and her traits.

He also extracted the bloodflame and stored it in the inventory for now.

Elavlin’s appearance rapidly withered as her vitality was devoured piece by piece beneath William’s hand.

The atmosphere turned deathly silent. None of the young men could even speak.

For the first time in their lives, they truly understood what genuine terror looked like; after all, they had never seen someone’s vitality and life force being sucked out like this.

At that moment, Ethan, Maximus, and Desmond, all three of them, sincerely wished they had been unconscious like Galeion instead of being forced to witness this scene with open eyes.
